I've uploaded a code repository in the directory /testbed. Consider the following PR description:

<pr_description>
Oxc minifier removes `if` guard and changes runtime behavior
### Describe the bug

Since upgrading to Vite 8, the default Oxc minifier appears to incorrectly optimize one of my functions and changes its runtime behavior. I reported the bug downstream in https://github.com/vitejs/vite/issues/22234 but was told to report it here.

Here is a stripped down version of my code:

```ts
let rafId: number | undefined;

function foo() {
  if (rafId == null) {
    rafId = requestAnimationFrame(() => {
      console.log('callback');
    });
  }
}
```

Minified build output using oxc:

```js
function e(){requestAnimationFrame(()=>{console.log(`callback`)})}
```

Minified build output using esbuild:

```js
function c(){n==null&&(n=requestAnimationFrame(()=>{console.log("callback")}))}
```

The `if (rafId == null)` guard is gone entirely (when using oxc), which changes the behavior of the program. The original function schedules at most one pending animation frame at a time, while the minified output schedules a new one on every call.

### Reproduction

The bug only happens when using "Transformer" with target >= 2021 and "Minify Syntax" checked.

https://playground.oxc.rs/?options=%7B%22run%22%3A%7B%22lint%22%3Afalse%2C%22formatter%22%3Afalse%2C%22transform%22%3Atrue%2C%22isolatedDeclarations%22%3Afalse%2C%22whitespace%22%3Afalse%2C%22mangle%22%3Afalse%2C%22compress%22%3Atrue%2C%22scope%22%3Atrue%2C%22symbol%22%3Atrue%2C%22cfg%22%3Afalse%7D%2C%22parser%22%3A%7B%22extension%22%3A%22tsx%22%2C%22allowReturnOutsideFunction%22%3Atrue%2C%22preserveParens%22%3Atrue%2C%22allowV8Intrinsics%22%3Atrue%2C%22semanticErrors%22%3Atrue%7D%2C%22linter%22%3A%7B%7D%2C%22formatter%22%3A%7B%22useTabs%22%3Afalse%2C%22tabWidth%22%3A2%2C%22endOfLine%22%3A%22lf%22%2C%22printWidth%22%3A80%2C%22singleQuote%22%3Afalse%2C%22jsxSingleQuote%22%3Afalse%2C%22quoteProps%22%3A%22as-needed%22%2C%22trailingComma%22%3A%22all%22%2C%22semi%22%3Atrue%2C%22arrowParens%22%3A%22always%22%2C%22bracketSpacing%22%3Atrue%2C%22bracketSameLine%22%3Afalse%2C%22objectWrap%22%3A%22preserve%22%2C%22singleAttributePerLine%22%3Afalse%7D%2C%22transformer%22%3A%7B%22target%22%3A%22es2022%22%2C%22useDefineForClassFields%22%3Atrue%2C%22experimentalDecorators%22%3Atrue%2C%22emitDecoratorMetadata%22%3Atrue%7D%2C%22isolatedDeclarations%22%3A%7B%22stripInternal%22%3Afalse%7D%2C%22codegen%22%3A%7B%22normal%22%3Atrue%2C%22jsdoc%22%3Atrue%2C%22annotation%22%3Atrue%2C%22legal%22%3Atrue%7D%2C%22compress%22%3A%7B%7D%2C%22mangle%22%3A%7B%22topLevel%22%3Atrue%2C%22keepNames%22%3Afalse%7D%2C%22controlFlow%22%3A%7B%22verbose%22%3Afalse%7D%2C%22inject%22%3A%7B%22inject%22%3A%7B%7D%7D%2C%22define%22%3A%7B%22define%22%3A%7B%7D%7D%7D&code=let+rafId%3A+number+%7C+undefined%3B%0A%0Afunction+foo%28%29+%7B%0A++if+%28rafId+%3D%3D+null%29+%7B%0A++++rafId+%3D+requestAnimationFrame%28%28%29+%3D%3E+%7B%0A++++++console.log%28%27callback%27%29%3B%0A++++%7D%29%3B%0A++%7D%0A%7D

     2	use oxc_ast::ast::*;
     3	use oxc_semantic::{Scoping, SemanticBuilder};
     4	
     5	use crate::{
     6	    CompressOptions, ReusableTraverseCtx,
     7	    peephole::{Normalize, NormalizeOptions, PeepholeOptimizations},
     8	    state::MinifierState,
     9	};
    10	
    11	pub struct Compressor<'a> {
    12	    allocator: &'a Allocator,
    13	}
    14	
    15	impl<'a> Compressor<'a> {
    16	    pub fn new(allocator: &'a Allocator) -> Self {
    17	        Self { allocator }
    18	    }
    19	
    20	    pub fn build(self, program: &mut Program<'a>, options: CompressOptions) {
    21	        let scoping = SemanticBuilder::new().build(program).semantic.into_scoping();
    22	        self.build_with_scoping(program, scoping, options);
    23	    }
    24	
    25	    /// Returns total number of iterations ran.
    26	    pub fn build_with_scoping(
    27	        self,
    28	        program: &mut Program<'a>,
    29	        scoping: Scoping,
    30	        options: CompressOptions,
    31	    ) -> u8 {
    32	        let max_iterations = options.max_iterations;
    33	        let state = MinifierState::new(program.source_type, options, /* dce */ false);
    34	        let mut ctx = ReusableTraverseCtx::new(state, scoping, self.allocator);
    35	        let normalize_options = NormalizeOptions {
    36	            convert_while_to_fors: true,
    37	            convert_const_to_let: true,
    38	            remove_unnecessary_use_strict: true,
    39	        };
    40	        Normalize::new(normalize_options).build(program, &mut ctx);
    41	        Self::run_in_loop(max_iterations, program, &mut ctx)
    42	    }
    43	
    44	    pub fn dead_code_elimination(self, program: &mut Program<'a>, options: CompressOptions) -> u8 {
    45	        let scoping = SemanticBuilder::new().build(program).semantic.into_scoping();
    46	        self.dead_code_elimination_with_scoping(program, scoping, options)
    47	    }
    48	
    49	    /// Returns total number of iterations ran.
    50	    pub fn dead_code_elimination_with_scoping(
    51	        self,
    52	        program: &mut Program<'a>,
    53	        scoping: Scoping,
    54	        options: CompressOptions,
    55	    ) -> u8 {
    56	        let max_iterations = options.max_iterations;
    57	        let state = MinifierState::new(program.source_type, options, /* dce */ true);
    58	        let mut ctx = ReusableTraverseCtx::new(state, scoping, self.allocator);
    59	        let normalize_options = NormalizeOptions {
    60	            convert_while_to_fors: false,
    61	            convert_const_to_let: false,
    62	            remove_unnecessary_use_strict: false,
    63	        };
    64	        Normalize::new(normalize_options).build(program, &mut ctx);
    65	        Self::run_in_loop(max_iterations, program, &mut ctx)
    66	    }
    67	
    68	    /// Fixed-point iteration loop for peephole optimizations.
    69	    fn run_in_loop(
    70	        max_iterations: Option<u8>,
    71	        program: &mut Program<'a>,
    72	        ctx: &mut ReusableTraverseCtx<'a>,
    73	    ) -> u8 {
    74	        let mut iteration = 0u8;
    75	        loop {
    76	            PeepholeOptimizations.run_once(program, ctx);
    77	            if !ctx.state().changed {
    78	                break;
    79	            }
    80	            if let Some(max) = max_iterations {
    81	                if iteration >= max {
    82	                    break;
    83	                }
    84	            } else if iteration > 10 {
    85	                debug_assert!(false, "Ran loop more than 10 times.");
    86	                break;
    87	            }
    88	            iteration += 1;
    89	        }
    90	        iteration
    91	    }
    92	}

     1	mod convert_to_dotted_properties;

    10	mod minimize_not_expression;

    15	mod remove_unused_expression;
    16	mod remove_unused_private_members;

    20	use oxc_ast_visit::Visit;

    22	use oxc_syntax::symbol::SymbolId;
    23	use rustc_hash::FxHashSet;
    24	
    25	use oxc_allocator::Vec;
    26	use oxc_ast::ast::*;
    27	
    28	use crate::{ReusableTraverseCtx, Traverse, TraverseCtx, minifier_traverse::traverse_mut_with_ctx};
    29	
    30	pub use self::normalize::{Normalize, NormalizeOptions};
    31	
    32	/// Stateless peephole optimizer. The `dce` flag and `changed` state are stored in `MinifierState`.
    33	pub struct PeepholeOptimizations;
    34	
    35	impl<'a> PeepholeOptimizations {
    36	    pub fn run_once(&mut self, program: &mut Program<'a>, ctx: &mut ReusableTraverseCtx<'a>) {
    37	        traverse_mut_with_ctx(self, program, ctx);
    38	    }
    39	
    40	    pub fn commutative_pair<'x, A, F, G, RetF: 'x, RetG: 'x>(
    41	        pair: (&'x A, &'x A),
    42	        check_a: F,
    43	        check_b: G,
    44	    ) -> Option<(RetF, RetG)>
    45	    where
    46	        F: Fn(&'x A) -> Option<RetF>,
    47	        G: Fn(&'x A) -> Option<RetG>,
    48	    {
    49	        match check_a(pair.0) {
    50	            Some(a) => {
    51	                if let Some(b) = check_b(pair.1) {
    52	                    return Some((a, b));
    53	                }
    54	            }
    55	            _ => {
    56	                if let Some(a) = check_a(pair.1)
    57	                    && let Some(b) = check_b(pair.0)
    58	                {
    59	                    return Some((a, b));
    60	                }
    61	            }
    62	        }
    63	        None
    64	    }
    65	
    66	    /// Checks if a member expression's base object may be mutated.
    67	    ///
    68	    /// This is used to prevent incorrect transformations like:
    69	    /// `x.y || (x = {}, x.y = 3)` → `x.y ||= (x = {}, 3)`
    70	    ///
    71	    /// The `||=` operator evaluates `x.y` (capturing `x`) before the RHS reassigns `x`,
    72	    /// which would change the semantics.
    73	    pub fn member_object_may_be_mutated(
    74	        assignment_target: &AssignmentTarget<'a>,
    75	        ctx: &TraverseCtx<'a>,
    76	    ) -> bool {
    77	        let object = match assignment_target {
    78	            AssignmentTarget::ComputedMemberExpression(member_expr) => &member_expr.object,
    79	            AssignmentTarget::PrivateFieldExpression(member_expr) => &member_expr.object,
    80	            AssignmentTarget::StaticMemberExpression(member_expr) => &member_expr.object,
    81	            _ => return false,
    82	        };
    83	
    84	        Self::is_expression_that_reference_may_change(object, ctx)
    85	    }
    86	
    87	    /// Checks if an expression's reference may change due to mutation.
    88	    ///
    89	    /// Returns `true` if the expression references a symbol that may be mutated,
    90	    /// or if the expression is not a simple identifier/this reference.
    91	    pub fn is_expression_that_reference_may_change(
    92	        expr: &Expression<'a>,
    93	        ctx: &TraverseCtx<'a>,
    94	    ) -> bool {
    95	        match expr {
    96	            Expression::Identifier(id) => {
    97	                if let Some(symbol_id) = ctx.scoping().get_reference(id.reference_id()).symbol_id()
    98	                {
    99	                    Self::is_symbol_mutated(symbol_id, ctx)
   100	                } else {
   101	                    true
   102	                }
   103	            }
   104	            Expression::ThisExpression(_) => false,
   105	            _ => true,
   106	        }
   107	    }
   108	
   109	    /// Check if a symbol is mutated, using the O(1) cached `write_references_count`
   110	    /// from `SymbolValue` when available, falling back to the O(num_refs) scan in
   111	    /// `Scoping::symbol_is_mutated` for symbols without cached values.
   112	    ///
   113	    /// Only variable declarators have cached values (populated during
   114	    /// `exit_variable_declarator` → `init_symbol_value`); function declarations
   115	    /// and other binding kinds still take the fallback path.
   116	    fn is_symbol_mutated(symbol_id: SymbolId, ctx: &TraverseCtx<'a>) -> bool {
   117	        if let Some(sv) = ctx.state.symbol_values.get_symbol_value(symbol_id) {
   118	            sv.write_references_count > 0
   119	        } else {
   120	            ctx.scoping().symbol_is_mutated(symbol_id)
   121	        }
   122	    }
   123	}
   124	
   125	impl<'a> Traverse<'a> for PeepholeOptimizations {
   126	    fn enter_program(&mut self, _program: &mut Program<'a>, ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>) {
   127	        ctx.state.symbol_values.clear();
   128	        ctx.state.proto_write_symbols.clear();
   129	        ctx.state.changed = false;
   130	    }
   131	
   132	    fn exit_program(&mut self, program: &mut Program<'a>, ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>) {
   133	        if ctx.state.changed {
   134	            // Remove stale references by collecting the set of live references from
   135	            // the AST and retaining only those in each symbol's reference list.
   136	            // This is done as a batch rather than deleting references one at a time,
   137	            // because individual deletion (`delete_resolved_reference`) uses a linear
   138	            // scan (`.position()`) per call, so removing many references to the same
   139	            // symbol is O(n²) (happens in bundler output with thousands of unused
   140	            // `var import_X = __toESM(require_Y())` declarations).
   141	            let mut counter = ReferencesCounter::default();
   142	            counter.visit_program(program);
   143	            ctx.scoping_mut().retain_resolved_references(&counter.refs);
   144	        }
   145	        // Only check class_symbols_stack in full optimization mode (not DCE mode)
   146	        debug_assert!(ctx.state.dce || ctx.state.class_symbols_stack.is_exhausted());
   147	    }
   148	
   149	    fn exit_statements(&mut self, stmts: &mut Vec<'a, Statement<'a>>, ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>) {
   150	        Self::minimize_statements(stmts, ctx);
   151	    }
   152	
   153	    fn enter_statement(&mut self, stmt: &mut Statement<'a>, ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>) {
   154	        if ctx.state.dce {
   155	            return;
   156	        }
   157	        Self::keep_track_of_pure_functions(stmt, ctx);
   158	    }
   159	
   160	    fn exit_statement(&mut self, stmt: &mut Statement<'a>, ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>) {
   161	        if ctx.state.dce {
   162	            match stmt {
   163	                Statement::BlockStatement(_) => Self::try_optimize_block(stmt, ctx),
   164	                Statement::IfStatement(_) => Self::try_fold_if(stmt, ctx),
   165	                Statement::ForStatement(_) => Self::try_fold_for(stmt, ctx),
   166	                Statement::TryStatement(_) => Self::try_fold_try(stmt, ctx),
   167	                Statement::LabeledStatement(_) => Self::try_fold_labeled(stmt, ctx),
   168	                Statement::FunctionDeclaration(_) => {
   169	                    Self::remove_unused_function_declaration(stmt, ctx);
   170	                }
   171	                Statement::ClassDeclaration(_) => {
   172	                    Self::remove_unused_class_declaration(stmt, ctx);
   173	                }
   174	                Statement::ExpressionStatement(_) => {
   175	                    Self::try_fold_expression_stmt(stmt, ctx);
   176	                }
   177	                Statement::ImportDeclaration(_) => {
   178	                    Self::remove_unused_import_specifiers(stmt, ctx);
   179	                }
   180	                _ => {}
   181	            }
   182	        } else {
   183	            match stmt {
   184	                Statement::BlockStatement(_) => Self::try_optimize_block(stmt, ctx),
   185	                Statement::IfStatement(s) => {
   186	                    Self::minimize_expression_in_boolean_context(&mut s.test, ctx);
   187	                    Self::try_fold_if(stmt, ctx);
   188	                    if let Statement::IfStatement(if_stmt) = stmt
   189	                        && let Some(folded_stmt) = Self::try_minimize_if(if_stmt, ctx)
   190	                    {
   191	                        *stmt = folded_stmt;
   192	                        ctx.state.changed = true;
   193	                    }
   194	                }
   195	                Statement::WhileStatement(s) => {
   196	                    Self::minimize_expression_in_boolean_context(&mut s.test, ctx);
   197	                }
   198	                Statement::ForStatement(s) => {
   199	                    if let Some(test) = &mut s.test {
   200	                        Self::minimize_expression_in_boolean_context(test, ctx);
   201	                    }
   202	                    Self::try_fold_for(stmt, ctx);
   203	                }
   204	                Statement::DoWhileStatement(s) => {
   205	                    Self::minimize_expression_in_boolean_context(&mut s.test, ctx);
   206	                }
   207	                Statement::TryStatement(_) => Self::try_fold_try(stmt, ctx),
   208	                Statement::LabeledStatement(_) => Self::try_fold_labeled(stmt, ctx),
   209	                Statement::FunctionDeclaration(f) => {
   210	                    Self::init_function_declaration_symbol_value(f.id.as_ref(), ctx);
   211	                    Self::remove_unused_function_declaration(stmt, ctx);
   212	                }
   213	                Statement::ClassDeclaration(c) => {
   214	                    Self::init_class_declaration_symbol_value(c, ctx);
   215	                    Self::remove_unused_class_declaration(stmt, ctx);
   216	                }
   217	                Statement::ImportDeclaration(_) => Self::remove_unused_import_specifiers(stmt, ctx),
   218	                _ => {}
   219	            }
   220	            Self::try_fold_expression_stmt(stmt, ctx);
   221	        }
   222	    }
   223	
   224	    fn exit_for_statement(&mut self, stmt: &mut ForStatement<'a>, ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>) {
   225	        if ctx.state.dce {
   226	            return;
   227	        }
   228	        Self::substitute_for_statement(stmt, ctx);
   229	        Self::minimize_for_statement(stmt, ctx);
   230	    }
   231	
   232	    fn exit_return_statement(&mut self, stmt: &mut ReturnStatement<'a>, ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>) {
   233	        if ctx.state.dce {
   234	            return;
   235	        }
   236	        Self::substitute_return_statement(stmt, ctx);
   237	    }
   238	
   239	    fn exit_variable_declaration(
   240	        &mut self,
   241	        decl: &mut VariableDeclaration<'a>,
   242	        ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>,
   243	    ) {
   244	        if ctx.state.dce {
   245	            return;
   246	        }
   247	        Self::substitute_variable_declaration(decl, ctx);
   248	    }
   249	
   250	    fn exit_variable_declarator(
   251	        &mut self,
   252	        decl: &mut VariableDeclarator<'a>,
   253	        ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>,
   254	    ) {
   255	        Self::init_symbol_value(decl, ctx);
   256	    }
   257	
   258	    fn exit_expression(&mut self, expr: &mut Expression<'a>, ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>) {
   259	        if ctx.state.dce {
   260	            match expr {
   261	                Expression::TemplateLiteral(t) => {
   262	                    Self::inline_template_literal(t, ctx);
   263	                }
   264	                Expression::ObjectExpression(e) => Self::fold_object_exp(e, ctx),
   265	                Expression::BinaryExpression(_) => {
   266	                    Self::fold_binary_expr(expr, ctx);
   267	                    Self::fold_binary_typeof_comparison(expr, ctx);
   268	                }
   269	                Expression::UnaryExpression(_) => Self::fold_unary_expr(expr, ctx),
   270	                Expression::StaticMemberExpression(_) => {
   271	                    Self::fold_static_member_expr(expr, ctx);
   272	                }
   273	                Expression::ComputedMemberExpression(_) => {
   274	                    Self::fold_computed_member_expr(expr, ctx);
   275	                }
   276	                Expression::LogicalExpression(_) => Self::fold_logical_expr(expr, ctx),
   277	                Expression::ChainExpression(_) => Self::fold_chain_expr(expr, ctx),
   278	                Expression::CallExpression(_) => {
   279	                    Self::fold_call_expression(expr, ctx);
   280	                    Self::substitute_iife_call(expr, ctx);
   281	                    Self::remove_dead_code_call_expression(expr, ctx);
   282	                }
   283	                Expression::ConditionalExpression(_) => {
   284	                    Self::try_fold_conditional_expression(expr, ctx);
   285	                }
   286	                Expression::SequenceExpression(_) => {
   287	                    Self::remove_sequence_expression(expr, ctx);
   288	                }
   289	                Expression::AssignmentExpression(_) => {
   290	                    Self::remove_unused_assignment_expr(expr, ctx);
   291	                }
   292	                _ => {}
   293	            }
   294	        } else {
   295	            match expr {
   296	                Expression::TemplateLiteral(t) => {
   297	                    Self::inline_template_literal(t, ctx);
   298	                    Self::substitute_template_literal(expr, ctx);
   299	                }
   300	                Expression::ObjectExpression(e) => Self::fold_object_exp(e, ctx),
   301	                Expression::BinaryExpression(e) => {
   302	                    Self::substitute_swap_binary_expressions(e);
   303	                    Self::fold_binary_expr(expr, ctx);
   304	                    Self::fold_binary_typeof_comparison(expr, ctx);
   305	                    Self::minimize_loose_boolean(expr, ctx);
   306	                    Self::minimize_binary(expr, ctx);
   307	                    Self::substitute_loose_equals_undefined(expr, ctx);
   308	                    Self::substitute_typeof_undefined(expr, ctx);
   309	                    Self::substitute_rotate_binary_expression(expr, ctx);
   310	                }
   311	                Expression::UnaryExpression(_) => {
   312	                    Self::fold_unary_expr(expr, ctx);
   313	                    Self::minimize_unary(expr, ctx);
   314	                    Self::substitute_unary_plus(expr, ctx);
   315	                }
   316	                Expression::StaticMemberExpression(_) => {
   317	                    Self::fold_static_member_expr(expr, ctx);
   318	                    Self::replace_known_property_access(expr, ctx);
   319	                }
   320	                Expression::ComputedMemberExpression(_) => {
   321	                    Self::fold_computed_member_expr(expr, ctx);
   322	                    Self::replace_known_property_access(expr, ctx);
   323	                }
   324	                Expression::LogicalExpression(_) => {
   325	                    Self::fold_logical_expr(expr, ctx);
   326	                    Self::minimize_logical_expression(expr, ctx);
   327	                    Self::substitute_is_object_and_not_null(expr, ctx);
   328	                    Self::substitute_rotate_logical_expression(expr, ctx);
   329	                }
   330	                Expression::ChainExpression(_) => {

   333	                }
   334	                Expression::CallExpression(_) => {

     1	use oxc_allocator::TakeIn;
     2	use oxc_ast::ast::*;
     3	
     4	use oxc_semantic::ScopeFlags;
     5	use oxc_span::GetSpan;
     6	
     7	use crate::TraverseCtx;

     9	use super::PeepholeOptimizations;
    10	
    11	impl<'a> PeepholeOptimizations {
    12	    /// `MangleIf`: <https://github.com/evanw/esbuild/blob/v0.24.2/internal/js_parser/js_parser.go#L9860>
    13	    pub fn try_minimize_if(
    14	        if_stmt: &mut IfStatement<'a>,
    15	        ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>,
    16	    ) -> Option<Statement<'a>> {
    17	        Self::wrap_to_avoid_ambiguous_else(if_stmt, ctx);
    18	        if let Statement::ExpressionStatement(expr_stmt) = &mut if_stmt.consequent {
    19	            if if_stmt.alternate.is_none() {
    20	                let (op, e) = match &mut if_stmt.test {
    21	                    // "if (!a) b();" => "a || b();"
    22	                    Expression::UnaryExpression(unary_expr) if unary_expr.operator.is_not() => {
    23	                        (LogicalOperator::Or, &mut unary_expr.argument)
    24	                    }
    25	                    // "if (a) b();" => "a && b();"
    26	                    e => (LogicalOperator::And, e),
    27	                };
    28	                let a = e.take_in(ctx.ast);
    29	                let b = expr_stmt.expression.take_in(ctx.ast);
    30	                let expr = Self::join_with_left_associative_op(if_stmt.span, op, a, b, ctx);
    31	                return Some(ctx.ast.statement_expression(if_stmt.span, expr));
    32	            } else if let Some(Statement::ExpressionStatement(alternate_expr_stmt)) =
    33	                &mut if_stmt.alternate
    34	            {
    35	                // "if (a) b(); else c();" => "a ? b() : c();"
    36	                let test = if_stmt.test.take_in(ctx.ast);
    37	                let consequent = expr_stmt.expression.take_in(ctx.ast);
    38	                let alternate = alternate_expr_stmt.expression.take_in(ctx.ast);
    39	                let expr =
    40	                    Self::minimize_conditional(if_stmt.span, test, consequent, alternate, ctx);
    41	                return Some(ctx.ast.statement_expression(if_stmt.span, expr));
    42	            }
    43	        } else if Self::is_statement_empty(&if_stmt.consequent) {
    44	            if if_stmt.alternate.is_none()
    45	                || if_stmt.alternate.as_ref().is_some_and(Self::is_statement_empty)
    46	            {
    47	                // "if (a) {}" => "a;"
    48	                let mut expr = if_stmt.test.take_in(ctx.ast);
    49	                Self::remove_unused_expression(&mut expr, ctx);
    50	                return Some(ctx.ast.statement_expression(if_stmt.span, expr));
    51	            } else if let Some(Statement::ExpressionStatement(expr_stmt)) = &mut if_stmt.alternate {
    52	                let (op, e) = match &mut if_stmt.test {
    53	                    // "if (!a) {} else b();" => "a && b();"
    54	                    Expression::UnaryExpression(unary_expr) if unary_expr.operator.is_not() => {
    55	                        (LogicalOperator::And, &mut unary_expr.argument)
    56	                    }
    57	                    // "if (a) {} else b();" => "a || b();"
    58	                    e => (LogicalOperator::Or, e),
    59	                };
    60	                let a = e.take_in(ctx.ast);
    61	                let b = expr_stmt.expression.take_in(ctx.ast);
    62	                let expr = Self::join_with_left_associative_op(if_stmt.span, op, a, b, ctx);
    63	                return Some(ctx.ast.statement_expression(if_stmt.span, expr));
    64	            } else if let Some(stmt) = &mut if_stmt.alternate {
    65	                // "yes" is missing and "no" is not missing (and is not an expression)
    66	                match &mut if_stmt.test {
    67	                    // "if (!a) {} else return b;" => "if (a) return b;"
    68	                    Expression::UnaryExpression(unary_expr) if unary_expr.operator.is_not() => {
    69	                        if_stmt.test = unary_expr.argument.take_in(ctx.ast);
    70	                        if_stmt.consequent = stmt.take_in(ctx.ast);
    71	                        if_stmt.alternate = None;
    72	                        ctx.state.changed = true;
    73	                    }
    74	                    // "if (a) {} else return b;" => "if (!a) return b;"
    75	                    _ => {
    76	                        if_stmt.test = Self::minimize_not(
    77	                            if_stmt.test.span(),
    78	                            if_stmt.test.take_in(ctx.ast),
    79	                            ctx,
    80	                        );
    81	                        if_stmt.consequent = stmt.take_in(ctx.ast);
    82	                        if_stmt.alternate = None;
    83	                        Self::try_minimize_if(if_stmt, ctx);
    84	                        ctx.state.changed = true;
    85	                    }
    86	                }
    87	            }
    88	        } else {
    89	            // "yes" is not missing (and is not an expression)
    90	            if let Some(alternate) = &mut if_stmt.alternate {
    91	                // "yes" is not missing (and is not an expression) and "no" is not missing
    92	                if !matches!(alternate, Statement::IfStatement(_))
    93	                    && let Expression::UnaryExpression(unary_expr) = &mut if_stmt.test
    94	                    && unary_expr.operator.is_not()
    95	                {
    96	                    // "if (!a) return b; else return c;" => "if (a) return c; else return b;"
    97	                    if_stmt.test = unary_expr.argument.take_in(ctx.ast);
    98	                    std::mem::swap(&mut if_stmt.consequent, alternate);
    99	                    Self::wrap_to_avoid_ambiguous_else(if_stmt, ctx);
   100	                    ctx.state.changed = true;
   101	                }
   102	                // "if (!a) {} else if (b) {}" => "if (!a) {} if (b) {}" is handled by minimize_statements
   103	                // "if (a) return b; else {}" => "if (a) return b;" is handled by remove_dead_code
   104	            } else {
   105	                // "no" is missing
   106	                if let Statement::IfStatement(if2_stmt) = &mut if_stmt.consequent
   107	                    && if2_stmt.alternate.is_none()
   108	                {
   109	                    // "if (a) if (b) return c;" => "if (a && b) return c;"
   110	                    let a = if_stmt.test.take_in(ctx.ast);
   111	                    let b = if2_stmt.test.take_in(ctx.ast);
   112	                    if_stmt.test = Self::join_with_left_associative_op(
   113	                        if_stmt.test.span(),
   114	                        LogicalOperator::And,
   115	                        a,
   116	                        b,
   117	                        ctx,
   118	                    );
   119	                    if_stmt.consequent = if2_stmt.consequent.take_in(ctx.ast);
   120	                    ctx.state.changed = true;
   121	                }
   122	            }
   123	        }
   124	        None
   125	    }
   126	
   127	    /// Wrap to avoid ambiguous else.
   128	    /// `if (foo) if (bar) baz else quaz` ->  `if (foo) { if (bar) baz else quaz }`
   129	    fn wrap_to_avoid_ambiguous_else(if_stmt: &mut IfStatement<'a>, ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>) {
   130	        if let Statement::IfStatement(if2) = &mut if_stmt.consequent
   131	            && if2.consequent.is_jump_statement()
   132	            && if2.alternate.is_some()
   133	        {
   134	            let scope_id = ctx.create_child_scope_of_current(ScopeFlags::empty());
   135	            if_stmt.consequent =
   136	                Statement::BlockStatement(ctx.ast.alloc(ctx.ast.block_statement_with_scope_id(
   137	                    if_stmt.consequent.span(),
   138	                    ctx.ast.vec1(if_stmt.consequent.take_in(ctx.ast)),
   139	                    scope_id,
   140	                )));
   141	            ctx.state.changed = true;
   142	        }
   143	    }
   144	
   145	    fn is_statement_empty(stmt: &Statement<'a>) -> bool {
   146	        match stmt {
   147	            Statement::BlockStatement(block_stmt) if block_stmt.body.is_empty() => true,
   148	            Statement::EmptyStatement(_) => true,
   149	            _ => false,
   150	        }
   151	    }
   152	}

    11	use crate::TraverseCtx;

    13	use super::PeepholeOptimizations;
    14	
    15	/// Minimize Conditions
    16	///
    17	/// <https://github.com/google/closure-compiler/blob/v20240609/src/com/google/javascript/jscomp/PeepholeMinimizeConditions.java>
    18	impl<'a> PeepholeOptimizations {
    19	    // The goal of this function is to "rotate" the AST if it's possible to use the
    20	    // left-associative property of the operator to avoid unnecessary parentheses.
    21	    //
    22	    // When using this, make absolutely sure that the operator is actually
    23	    // associative. For example, the "+" operator is not associative for
    24	    // floating-point numbers.
    25	    pub fn join_with_left_associative_op(
    26	        span: Span,
    27	        op: LogicalOperator,
    28	        a: Expression<'a>,
    29	        b: Expression<'a>,
    30	        ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>,
    31	    ) -> Expression<'a> {
    32	        // "(a, b) op c" => "a, b op c"
    33	        if let Expression::SequenceExpression(mut sequence_expr) = a {
    34	            if let Some(right) = sequence_expr.expressions.pop() {
    35	                sequence_expr
    36	                    .expressions
    37	                    .push(Self::join_with_left_associative_op(span, op, right, b, ctx));
    38	            }
    39	            return Expression::SequenceExpression(sequence_expr);
    40	        }
    41	        let mut a = a;
    42	        let mut b = b;
    43	        // "a op (b op c)" => "(a op b) op c"
    44	        // "a op (b op (c op d))" => "((a op b) op c) op d"
    45	        loop {
    46	            if let Expression::LogicalExpression(logical_expr) = &mut b
    47	                && logical_expr.operator == op
    48	            {
    49	                let right = logical_expr.left.take_in(ctx.ast);
    50	                a = Self::join_with_left_associative_op(span, op, a, right, ctx);
    51	                b = logical_expr.right.take_in(ctx.ast);
    52	                continue;
    53	            }
    54	            break;
    55	        }
    56	        // "a op b" => "a op b"
    57	        // "(a op b) op c" => "(a op b) op c"
    58	        let mut logic_expr = ctx.ast.expression_logical(span, a, op, b);
    59	        Self::minimize_logical_expression(&mut logic_expr, ctx);
    60	        logic_expr
    61	    }
    62	
    63	    // `typeof foo === 'number'` -> `typeof foo == 'number'`
    64	    //  ^^^^^^^^^^ `ctx.expression_value_type(&e.left).is_string()` is `true`.
    65	    // `a instanceof b === true` -> `a instanceof b`
    66	    // `a instanceof b === false` -> `!(a instanceof b)`
    67	    //  ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ `ctx.expression_value_type(&e.left).is_boolean()` is `true`.
    68	    // `x >> +y !== 0` -> `x >> +y`
    69	    //  ^^^^^^^ ctx.expression_value_type(&e.left).is_number()` is `true`.
    70	    pub fn minimize_binary(expr: &mut Expression<'a>, ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>) {
    71	        let Expression::BinaryExpression(e) = expr else { return };
    72	        if !e.operator.is_equality() {
    73	            return;
    74	        }
    75	        let left = e.left.value_type(ctx);
    76	        let right = e.right.value_type(ctx);
    77	        if left.is_undetermined() || right.is_undetermined() {
    78	            return;
    79	        }
    80	        if left == right {
    81	            match e.operator {
    82	                BinaryOperator::StrictInequality => {
    83	                    e.operator = BinaryOperator::Inequality;
    84	                }
    85	                BinaryOperator::StrictEquality => {
    86	                    e.operator = BinaryOperator::Equality;
    87	                }
    88	                _ => {}
    89	            }
    90	        }
    91	        if !left.is_boolean() {
    92	            return;
    93	        }
    94	        if e.right.may_have_side_effects(ctx) {
    95	            return;
    96	        }
    97	        let Some(mut b) = e.right.evaluate_value(ctx).and_then(ConstantValue::into_boolean) else {
    98	            return;
    99	        };
   100	        match e.operator {
   101	            BinaryOperator::Inequality | BinaryOperator::StrictInequality => {
   102	                e.operator = BinaryOperator::Equality;
   103	                b = !b;
   104	            }
   105	            BinaryOperator::StrictEquality => {
   106	                e.operator = BinaryOperator::Equality;
   107	            }
   108	            BinaryOperator::Equality => {}
   109	            _ => return,
   110	        }
   111	        *expr = if b {
   112	            e.left.take_in(ctx.ast)
   113	        } else {
   114	            let argument = e.left.take_in(ctx.ast);
   115	            ctx.ast.expression_unary(e.span, UnaryOperator::LogicalNot, argument)
   116	        };
   117	        ctx.state.changed = true;
   118	    }
   119	
   120	    /// Compress `foo == true` into `foo == 1`.
   121	    ///
   122	    /// - `foo == true` => `foo == 1`
   123	    /// - `foo != false` => `foo != 0`
   124	    ///
   125	    /// In `IsLooselyEqual`, `true` and `false` are converted to `1` and `0` first.
   126	    /// <https://tc39.es/ecma262/multipage/abstract-operations.html#sec-islooselyequal>
   127	    pub fn minimize_loose_boolean(e: &mut Expression<'a>, ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>) {
   128	        let Expression::BinaryExpression(e) = e else { return };
   129	        if !matches!(e.operator, BinaryOperator::Equality | BinaryOperator::Inequality) {
   130	            return;
   131	        }
   132	        if let Some(ConstantValue::Boolean(left_bool)) = e.left.evaluate_value(ctx) {
   133	            e.left = ctx.ast.expression_numeric_literal(
   134	                e.left.span(),
   135	                if left_bool { 1.0 } else { 0.0 },
   136	                None,
   137	                NumberBase::Decimal,
   138	            );
   139	            ctx.state.changed = true;
   140	            return;
   141	        }
   142	        if let Some(ConstantValue::Boolean(right_bool)) = e.right.evaluate_value(ctx) {
   143	            e.right = ctx.ast.expression_numeric_literal(
   144	                e.right.span(),
   145	                if right_bool { 1.0 } else { 0.0 },
   146	                None,
   147	                NumberBase::Decimal,
   148	            );
   149	            ctx.state.changed = true;
   150	        }
   151	    }
   152	
   153	    /// Returns the identifier or the assignment target's identifier of the given expression.
   154	    pub fn extract_id_or_assign_to_id<'b>(
   155	        expr: &'b Expression<'a>,
   156	    ) -> Option<&'b IdentifierReference<'a>> {
   157	        match expr {
   158	            Expression::Identifier(id) => Some(id),
   159	            Expression::AssignmentExpression(assign_expr) => {
   160	                if assign_expr.operator == AssignmentOperator::Assign
   161	                    && let AssignmentTarget::AssignmentTargetIdentifier(id) = &assign_expr.left
   162	                {
   163	                    return Some(id);
   164	                }
   165	                None
   166	            }
   167	            _ => None,
   168	        }
   169	    }
   170	
   171	    /// Compress `a = a || b` to `a ||= b`
   172	    ///
   173	    /// This can only be done for resolved identifiers as this would avoid setting `a` when `a` is truthy.
   174	    pub fn minimize_normal_assignment_to_combined_logical_assignment(
   175	        expr: &mut AssignmentExpression<'a>,
   176	        ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>,
   177	    ) {
   178	        if !ctx.supports_feature(ESFeature::ES2021LogicalAssignmentOperators)
   179	            || !matches!(expr.operator, AssignmentOperator::Assign)
   180	        {
   181	            return;
   182	        }
   183	
   184	        let Expression::LogicalExpression(logical_expr) = &mut expr.right else { return };
   185	        // NOTE: if the right hand side is an anonymous function, applying this compression will
   186	        // set the `name` property of that function.
   187	        // Since codes relying on the fact that function's name is undefined should be rare,
   188	        // we do this compression even if `keep_names` is enabled.
   189	
   190	        let (
   191	            AssignmentTarget::AssignmentTargetIdentifier(write_id_ref),
   192	            Expression::Identifier(read_id_ref),
   193	        ) = (&expr.left, &logical_expr.left)
   194	        else {
   195	            return;
   196	        };
   197	        // It should also early return when the reference might refer to a reference value created by a with statement
   198	        // when the minifier supports with statements
   199	        if write_id_ref.name != read_id_ref.name || ctx.is_global_reference(write_id_ref) {
   200	            return;
   201	        }
   202	
   203	        let reference = ctx.scoping_mut().get_reference_mut(write_id_ref.reference_id());
   204	        reference.flags_mut().insert(ReferenceFlags::Read);
   205	
   206	        let new_op = logical_expr.operator.to_assignment_operator();
   207	        expr.operator = new_op;
   208	        expr.right = logical_expr.right.take_in(ctx.ast);
   209	        ctx.state.changed = true;
   210	    }
   211	
   212	    /// Compress `a = a + b` to `a += b`
   213	    pub fn minimize_normal_assignment_to_combined_assignment(
   214	        expr: &mut AssignmentExpression<'a>,
   215	        ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>,
   216	    ) {
   217	        if !matches!(expr.operator, AssignmentOperator::Assign) {
   218	            return;
   219	        }
   220	        let Expression::BinaryExpression(binary_expr) = &mut expr.right else { return };
   221	        let Some(new_op) = binary_expr.operator.to_assignment_operator() else { return };
   222	        if !Self::has_no_side_effect_for_evaluation_same_target(&expr.left, &binary_expr.left, ctx)
   223	        {
   224	            return;
   225	        }
   226	
   227	        Self::mark_assignment_target_as_read(&expr.left, ctx);
   228	
   229	        expr.operator = new_op;
   230	        expr.right = binary_expr.right.take_in(ctx.ast);
   231	        ctx.state.changed = true;
   232	    }
   233	
   234	    /// Compress `a -= 1` to `--a` and `a -= -1` to `++a`
   235	    #[expect(clippy::float_cmp)]
   236	    pub fn minimize_assignment_to_update_expression(
   237	        expr: &mut Expression<'a>,
   238	        ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>,
   239	    ) {
   240	        let Expression::AssignmentExpression(e) = expr else { return };
   241	        if !matches!(e.operator, AssignmentOperator::Subtraction) {
   242	            return;
   243	        }
   244	        let operator = if let Expression::NumericLiteral(num) = &e.right {
   245	            if num.value == 1.0 {
   246	                UpdateOperator::Decrement
   247	            } else if num.value == -1.0 {
   248	                UpdateOperator::Increment
   249	            } else {
   250	                return;
   251	            }
   252	        } else {
   253	            return;
   254	        };
   255	        let Some(target) = e.left.as_simple_assignment_target_mut() else { return };
   256	        let target = target.take_in(ctx.ast);
   257	        *expr = ctx.ast.expression_update(e.span, operator, true, target);
   258	        ctx.state.changed = true;
   259	    }
   260	}
   261

     1	use oxc_allocator::TakeIn;
     2	use oxc_ast::ast::*;
     3	use oxc_ecmascript::{
     4	    GlobalContext, ToJsString,
     5	    constant_evaluation::{ConstantEvaluation, ConstantValue, DetermineValueType, ValueType},
     6	    side_effects::MayHaveSideEffects,
     7	};
     8	use oxc_span::{GetSpan, SPAN};
     9	use oxc_syntax::operator::{BinaryOperator, LogicalOperator};
    10	
    11	use crate::TraverseCtx;
    12	
    13	use super::PeepholeOptimizations;
    14	
    15	/// Constant Folding
    16	///
    17	/// <https://github.com/google/closure-compiler/blob/v20240609/src/com/google/javascript/jscomp/PeepholeFoldConstants.java>
    18	impl<'a> PeepholeOptimizations {
    19	    #[expect(clippy::float_cmp)]
    20	    pub fn fold_unary_expr(expr: &mut Expression<'a>, ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>) {
    21	        let Expression::UnaryExpression(e) = expr else { return };
    22	        match e.operator {
    23	            // Do not fold `void 0` back to `undefined`.
    24	            UnaryOperator::Void if e.argument.is_number_0() => {}
    25	            // Do not fold `true` and `false` back to `!0` and `!1`
    26	            UnaryOperator::LogicalNot if matches!(&e.argument, Expression::NumericLiteral(lit) if lit.value == 0.0 || lit.value == 1.0) =>
    27	                {}
    28	            // Do not fold big int.
    29	            UnaryOperator::UnaryNegation if e.argument.is_big_int_literal() => {}
    30	            _ if e.may_have_side_effects(ctx) => {}
    31	            _ => {
    32	                if let Some(changed) = e.evaluate_value(ctx).map(|v| ctx.value_to_expr(e.span, v)) {
    33	                    *expr = changed;
    34	                    ctx.state.changed = true;
    35	                }
    36	            }
    37	        }
    38	    }
    39	
    40	    pub fn fold_static_member_expr(expr: &mut Expression<'a>, ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>) {
    41	        let Expression::StaticMemberExpression(e) = expr else { return };
    42	        // TODO: tryFoldObjectPropAccess(n, left, name)
    43	        if e.object.may_have_side_effects(ctx) {
    44	            return;
    45	        }
    46	        if let Some(changed) = e.evaluate_value(ctx).map(|value| ctx.value_to_expr(e.span, value)) {
    47	            *expr = changed;
    48	            ctx.state.changed = true;
    49	        }
    50	    }
    51	
    52	    pub fn fold_computed_member_expr(expr: &mut Expression<'a>, ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>) {
    53	        let Expression::ComputedMemberExpression(e) = expr else { return };
    54	        // TODO: tryFoldObjectPropAccess(n, left, name)
    55	        if e.object.may_have_side_effects(ctx) || e.expression.may_have_side_effects(ctx) {
    56	            return;
    57	        }
    58	        if let Some(changed) = e.evaluate_value(ctx).map(|value| ctx.value_to_expr(e.span, value)) {
    59	            *expr = changed;
    60	            ctx.state.changed = true;
    61	        }
    62	    }
    63	
    64	    pub fn fold_logical_expr(expr: &mut Expression<'a>, ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>) {
    65	        let Expression::LogicalExpression(e) = expr else { return };
    66	        if let Some(changed) = match e.operator {
    67	            LogicalOperator::And | LogicalOperator::Or => Self::try_fold_and_or(e, ctx),
    68	            LogicalOperator::Coalesce => Self::try_fold_coalesce(e, ctx),
    69	        } {
    70	            *expr = changed;
    71	            ctx.state.changed = true;
    72	        }
    73	    }
    74	
    75	    pub fn fold_chain_expr(expr: &mut Expression<'a>, ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>) {
    76	        let Expression::ChainExpression(e) = expr else { return };
    77	        let left_expr = match &e.expression {
    78	            match_member_expression!(ChainElement) => {
    79	                let member_expr = e.expression.to_member_expression();
    80	                if !member_expr.optional() {
    81	                    return;
    82	                }
    83	                member_expr.object()
    84	            }
    85	            ChainElement::CallExpression(call_expr) => {
    86	                if !call_expr.optional {
    87	                    return;
    88	                }
    89	                &call_expr.callee
    90	            }
    91	            ChainElement::TSNonNullExpression(_) => return,
    92	        };
    93	        let ty = left_expr.value_type(ctx);
    94	        if let Some(changed) = (ty.is_null() || ty.is_undefined())
    95	            .then(|| ctx.value_to_expr(e.span, ConstantValue::Undefined))
    96	        {
    97	            *expr = changed;
    98	            ctx.state.changed = true;
    99	        }
   100	    }
   101	
   102	    /// Try to fold a AND / OR node.
   103	    ///
   104	    /// port from [closure-compiler](https://github.com/google/closure-compiler/blob/09094b551915a6487a980a783831cba58b5739d1/src/com/google/javascript/jscomp/PeepholeFoldConstants.java#L587)
   105	    pub fn try_fold_and_or(
   106	        logical_expr: &mut LogicalExpression<'a>,
   107	        ctx: &TraverseCtx<'a>,
   108	    ) -> Option<Expression<'a>> {
   109	        let op = logical_expr.operator;
   110	        debug_assert!(matches!(op, LogicalOperator::And | LogicalOperator::Or));
   111	
   112	        let left = &logical_expr.left;
   113	        let left_val = left.evaluate_value_to_boolean(ctx);
   114	
   115	        if let Some(lval) = left_val {
   116	            // (TRUE || x) => TRUE (also, (3 || x) => 3)
   117	            // (FALSE && x) => FALSE
   118	            if if lval { op.is_or() } else { op.is_and() } {
   119	                return Some(logical_expr.left.take_in(ctx.ast));
   120	            } else if !left.may_have_side_effects(ctx) {
   121	                let should_keep_indirect_access =
   122	                    Self::should_keep_indirect_access(&logical_expr.right, ctx);
   123	                // (true && o.f) => (0, o.f)
   124	                if should_keep_indirect_access {
   125	                    return Some(ctx.ast.expression_sequence(
   126	                        logical_expr.span,
   127	                        ctx.ast.vec_from_array([
   128	                            ctx.ast.expression_numeric_literal(
   129	                                logical_expr.left.span(),
   130	                                0.0,
   131	                                None,
   132	                                NumberBase::Decimal,
   133	                            ),
   134	                            logical_expr.right.take_in(ctx.ast),
   135	                        ]),
   136	                    ));
   137	                }
   138	                // (FALSE || x) => x
   139	                // (TRUE && x) => x
   140	                return Some(logical_expr.right.take_in(ctx.ast));
   141	            }
   142	            // Left side may have side effects, but we know its boolean value.
   143	            // e.g. true_with_sideeffects || foo() => true_with_sideeffects, foo()
   144	            // or: false_with_sideeffects && foo() => false_with_sideeffects, foo()
   145	            let left = logical_expr.left.take_in(ctx.ast);
   146	            let right = logical_expr.right.take_in(ctx.ast);
   147	            let vec = ctx.ast.vec_from_array([left, right]);
   148	            let sequence_expr = ctx.ast.expression_sequence(logical_expr.span, vec);
   149	            return Some(sequence_expr);
   150	        } else if let Expression::LogicalExpression(left_child) = &mut logical_expr.left
   151	            && left_child.operator == logical_expr.operator
   152	        {
   153	            let left_child_right_boolean = left_child.right.evaluate_value_to_boolean(ctx);
   154	            let left_child_op = left_child.operator;
   155	            if let Some(right_boolean) = left_child_right_boolean
   156	                && !left_child.right.may_have_side_effects(ctx)
   157	            {
   158	                // a || false || b => a || b
   159	                // a && true && b => a && b
   160	                if !right_boolean && left_child_op.is_or()
   161	                    || right_boolean && left_child_op.is_and()
   162	                {
   163	                    let left = left_child.left.take_in(ctx.ast);
   164	                    let right = logical_expr.right.take_in(ctx.ast);
   165	                    let logic_expr =
   166	                        ctx.ast.expression_logical(logical_expr.span, left, left_child_op, right);
   167	                    return Some(logic_expr);
   168	                }
   169	            }
   170	        }
   171	        None
   172	    }
   173	
   174	    /// Try to fold a nullish coalesce `foo ?? bar`.
   175	    pub fn try_fold_coalesce(
   176	        logical_expr: &mut LogicalExpression<'a>,
   177	        ctx: &TraverseCtx<'a>,
   178	    ) -> Option<Expression<'a>> {
   179	        debug_assert_eq!(logical_expr.operator, LogicalOperator::Coalesce);
   180	        let left = &logical_expr.left;
   181	        let left_val = left.value_type(ctx);
   182	        match left_val {
   183	            ValueType::Null | ValueType::Undefined => {
   184	                Some(if left.may_have_side_effects(ctx) {
   185	                    // e.g. `(a(), null) ?? 1` => `(a(), null, 1)`
   186	                    let expressions = ctx.ast.vec_from_array([
   187	                        logical_expr.left.take_in(ctx.ast),
   188	                        logical_expr.right.take_in(ctx.ast),
   189	                    ]);
   190	                    ctx.ast.expression_sequence(logical_expr.span, expressions)
   191	                } else {
   192	                    let should_keep_indirect_access =
   193	                        Self::should_keep_indirect_access(&logical_expr.right, ctx);
   194	                    // (null ?? o.f) => (0, o.f)
   195	                    if should_keep_indirect_access {
   196	                        return Some(ctx.ast.expression_sequence(
   197	                            logical_expr.span,
   198	                            ctx.ast.vec_from_array([
   199	                                ctx.ast.expression_numeric_literal(
   200	                                    logical_expr.left.span(),
   201	                                    0.0,
   202	                                    None,
   203	                                    NumberBase::Decimal,
   204	                                ),
   205	                                logical_expr.right.take_in(ctx.ast),
   206	                            ]),
   207	                        ));
   208	                    }
   209	                    // nullish condition => this expression evaluates to the right side.
   210	                    logical_expr.right.take_in(ctx.ast)
   211	                })
   212	            }
   213	            ValueType::Number
   214	            | ValueType::BigInt
   215	            | ValueType::String
   216	            | ValueType::Boolean
   217	            | ValueType::Object => {
   218	                let should_keep_indirect_access =
   219	                    Self::should_keep_indirect_access(&logical_expr.left, ctx);
   220	                // (o.f ?? something) => (0, o.f)
   221	                if should_keep_indirect_access {
   222	                    return Some(ctx.ast.expression_sequence(
   223	                        logical_expr.span,
   224	                        ctx.ast.vec_from_array([
   225	                            ctx.ast.expression_numeric_literal(
   226	                                logical_expr.right.span(),
   227	                                0.0,
   228	                                None,
   229	                                NumberBase::Decimal,
   230	                            ),
   231	                            logical_expr.left.take_in(ctx.ast),
   232	                        ]),
   233	                    ));
   234	                }
   235	                // non-nullish condition => this expression evaluates to the left side.
   236	                Some(logical_expr.left.take_in(ctx.ast))
   237	            }
   238	            ValueType::Undetermined => None,
   239	        }
   240	    }
   241	
   242	    fn extract_numeric_values(e: &BinaryExpression<'a>) -> Option<(f64, f64)> {
   243	        if let (Expression::NumericLiteral(left), Expression::NumericLiteral(right)) =
   244	            (&e.left, &e.right)
   245	        {
   246	            return Some((left.value, right.value));
   247	        }
   248	        None
   249	    }
   250	
   251	    #[expect(clippy::cast_possible_truncation, clippy::cast_sign_loss)]
   252	    pub fn fold_binary_expr(expr: &mut Expression<'a>, ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>) {
   253	        let Expression::BinaryExpression(e) = expr else { return };
   254	        // TODO: tryReduceOperandsForOp
   255	
   256	        // https://github.com/evanw/esbuild/blob/v0.24.2/internal/js_ast/js_ast_helpers.go#L1136
   257	        // https://github.com/evanw/esbuild/blob/v0.24.2/internal/js_ast/js_ast_helpers.go#L1222
   258	        let span = e.span;
   259	        let changed = match e.operator {
   260	            BinaryOperator::Equality
   261	            | BinaryOperator::Inequality
   262	            | BinaryOperator::StrictEquality
   263	            | BinaryOperator::StrictInequality
   264	            | BinaryOperator::LessThan
   265	            | BinaryOperator::GreaterThan
   266	            | BinaryOperator::LessEqualThan
   267	            | BinaryOperator::GreaterEqualThan
   268	            | BinaryOperator::ShiftRight
   269	            | BinaryOperator::Instanceof => ctx.eval_binary(e),
   270	            BinaryOperator::BitwiseAnd | BinaryOperator::BitwiseOR | BinaryOperator::BitwiseXOR => {
   271	                ctx.eval_binary(e).or_else(|| Self::try_fold_left_child_op(e, ctx))
   272	            }
   273	            BinaryOperator::Addition => Self::try_fold_add(e, ctx),
   274	            BinaryOperator::Subtraction => {
   275	                // Subtraction of small-ish integers can definitely be folded without issues
   276	                Self::extract_numeric_values(e)
   277	                    .filter(|(left, right)| {
   278	                        left.is_nan()
   279	                            || left.is_finite()
   280	                            || right.is_nan()
   281	                            || right.is_finite()
   282	                            || (left.fract() == 0.0
   283	                                && right.fract() == 0.0
   284	                                && (left.abs() as usize) <= 0xFFFF_FFFF
   285	                                && (right.abs() as usize) <= 0xFFFF_FFFF)
   286	                    })
   287	                    .and_then(|_| ctx.eval_binary(e))
   288	            }
   289	            BinaryOperator::Multiplication
   290	            | BinaryOperator::Exponential
   291	            | BinaryOperator::Remainder => Self::extract_numeric_values(e)
   292	                .filter(|(left, right)| {
   293	                    *left == 0.0
   294	                        || left.is_nan()
   295	                        || left.is_infinite()
   296	                        || *right == 0.0
   297	                        || right.is_nan()
   298	                        || right.is_infinite()
   299	                        // Small number multiplication.
   300	                        || (e.operator == BinaryOperator::Multiplication
   301	                            && left.abs() <= 255.0
   302	                            && left.fract() == 0.0
   303	                            && right.abs() <= 255.0
   304	                            && right.fract() == 0.0)
   305	                })
   306	                .and_then(|_| ctx.eval_binary(e)),
   307	            BinaryOperator::Division => Self::extract_numeric_values(e)
   308	                .filter(|(_, right)| *right == 0.0 || right.is_nan() || right.is_infinite())
   309	                .and_then(|_| ctx.eval_binary(e)),
   310	            BinaryOperator::ShiftLeft => {
   311	                Self::extract_numeric_values(e).and_then(|(left, right)| {
   312	                    let result = e.evaluate_value(ctx)?.into_number()?;
   313	                    let left_len = Self::approximate_printed_int_char_count(left);
   314	                    let right_len = Self::approximate_printed_int_char_count(right);
   315	                    let result_len = Self::approximate_printed_int_char_count(result);
   316	                    (result_len <= left_len + 2 + right_len)
   317	                        .then(|| ctx.value_to_expr(span, ConstantValue::Number(result)))
   318	                })
   319	            }
   320	            BinaryOperator::ShiftRightZeroFill => {
   321	                Self::extract_numeric_values(e).and_then(|(left, right)| {
   322	                    let result = e.evaluate_value(ctx)?.into_number()?;
   323	                    let left_len = Self::approximate_printed_int_char_count(left);
   324	                    let right_len = Self::approximate_printed_int_char_count(right);
   325	                    let result_len = Self::approximate_printed_int_char_count(result);
   326	                    (result_len <= left_len + 3 + right_len)
   327	                        .then(|| ctx.value_to_expr(span, ConstantValue::Number(result)))
   328	                })
   329	            }
   330	            BinaryOperator::In => None,
   331	        };
   332	        if let Some(changed) = changed {
   333	            *expr = changed;
   334	            ctx.state.changed = true;
   335	        }
   336	    }
   337	
   338	    // https://github.com/evanw/esbuild/blob/v0.24.2/internal/js_ast/js_ast_helpers.go#L1128
   339	    #[expect(clippy::cast_possible_truncation, clippy::cast_sign_loss)]
   340	    #[must_use]
   341	    fn approximate_printed_int_char_count(value: f64) -> usize {
   342	        let mut count = if value.is_infinite() {
   343	            "Infinity".len()
   344	        } else if value.is_nan() {
   345	            "NaN".len()
   346	        } else {
   347	            1 + value.abs().log10().floor() as usize
   348	        };
   349	        if value.is_sign_negative() {
   350	            count += 1;
   351	        }
   352	        count
   353	    }

     1	use oxc_ast::ast::*;
     2	use oxc_syntax::operator::{BinaryOperator, UnaryOperator};

     6	/// JavaScript Language Type
     7	///
     8	/// <https://tc39.es/ecma262/#sec-ecmascript-language-types>
     9	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
    10	pub enum ValueType {
    11	    Undefined, // a.k.a `Void` in closure compiler
    12	    Null,
    13	    Number,
    14	    BigInt,
    15	    String,
    16	    Boolean,
    17	    Object,
    18	    Undetermined,
    19	}
    20	
    21	impl ValueType {
    22	    pub fn is_undefined(self) -> bool {
    23	        self == Self::Undefined
    24	    }
    25	
    26	    pub fn is_null(self) -> bool {
    27	        self == Self::Null
    28	    }
    29	
    30	    pub fn is_string(self) -> bool {
    31	        self == Self::String
    32	    }
    33	
    34	    pub fn is_number(self) -> bool {
    35	        self == Self::Number
    36	    }
    37	
    38	    pub fn is_bigint(self) -> bool {
    39	        self == Self::BigInt
    40	    }
    41	
    42	    pub fn is_boolean(self) -> bool {
    43	        self == Self::Boolean
    44	    }
    45	
    46	    pub fn is_object(self) -> bool {
    47	        self == Self::Object
    48	    }
    49	
    50	    pub fn is_undetermined(self) -> bool {
    51	        self == Self::Undetermined
    52	    }

    54	
    55	/// Based on `get_known_value_type` in closure compiler
    56	/// <https://github.com/google/closure-compiler/blob/v20240609/src/com/google/javascript/jscomp/NodeUtil.java#L1517>
    57	///
    58	/// Evaluate the expression and attempt to determine which ValueType it could resolve to.
    59	/// This function ignores the cases that throws an error, e.g. `foo * 0` can throw an error when `foo` is a bigint.
    60	/// To detect those cases, use [`crate::side_effects::MayHaveSideEffects`].
    61	pub trait DetermineValueType<'a> {
    62	    fn value_type(&self, ctx: &impl GlobalContext<'a>) -> ValueType;
    63	}
    64	
    65	impl<'a> DetermineValueType<'a> for Expression<'a> {
    66	    fn value_type(&self, ctx: &impl GlobalContext<'a>) -> ValueType {
    67	        match self {
    68	            Expression::BigIntLiteral(_) => ValueType::BigInt,
    69	            Expression::BooleanLiteral(_) | Expression::PrivateInExpression(_) => {
    70	                ValueType::Boolean
    71	            }
    72	            Expression::NullLiteral(_) => ValueType::Null,
    73	            Expression::NumericLiteral(_) => ValueType::Number,
    74	            Expression::StringLiteral(_) | Expression::TemplateLiteral(_) => ValueType::String,
    75	            Expression::ObjectExpression(_)
    76	            | Expression::ArrayExpression(_)
    77	            | Expression::RegExpLiteral(_)
    78	            | Expression::FunctionExpression(_)
    79	            | Expression::ArrowFunctionExpression(_)
    80	            | Expression::ClassExpression(_) => ValueType::Object,
    81	            Expression::MetaProperty(meta_prop) => {
    82	                match (meta_prop.meta.name.as_str(), meta_prop.property.name.as_str()) {
    83	                    ("import", "meta") => ValueType::Object,
    84	                    _ => ValueType::Undetermined,
    85	                }
    86	            }
    87	            Expression::Identifier(ident) => {
    88	                if ctx.is_global_reference(ident) {
    89	                    match ident.name.as_str() {
    90	                        "undefined" => ValueType::Undefined,
    91	                        "NaN" | "Infinity" => ValueType::Number,
    92	                        _ => ValueType::Undetermined,
    93	                    }
    94	                } else {
    95	                    ValueType::Undetermined
    96	                }
    97	            }
    98	            Expression::UnaryExpression(e) => e.value_type(ctx),
    99	            Expression::BinaryExpression(e) => e.value_type(ctx),
   100	            Expression::SequenceExpression(e) => {
   101	                e.expressions.last().map_or(ValueType::Undetermined, |e| e.value_type(ctx))
   102	            }
   103	            Expression::AssignmentExpression(e) => e.value_type(ctx),
   104	            Expression::ConditionalExpression(e) => e.value_type(ctx),
   105	            Expression::LogicalExpression(e) => e.value_type(ctx),
   106	            Expression::ParenthesizedExpression(e) => e.expression.value_type(ctx),
   107	            Expression::StaticMemberExpression(e) => e.value_type(ctx),
   108	            Expression::NewExpression(e) => e.value_type(ctx),
   109	            _ => ValueType::Undetermined,
   110	        }
   111	    }
   112	}
   113	
   114	impl<'a> DetermineValueType<'a> for BinaryExpression<'a> {
   115	    fn value_type(&self, ctx: &impl GlobalContext<'a>) -> ValueType {
   116	        match self.operator {
   117	            BinaryOperator::Addition => {
   118	                let left = self.left.to_primitive(ctx);
   119	                let right = self.right.to_primitive(ctx);
   120	                if left.is_string() == Some(true) || right.is_string() == Some(true) {
   121	                    return ValueType::String;
   122	                }
   123	                let left_to_numeric_type = left.to_numeric(ctx);
   124	                let right_to_numeric_type = right.to_numeric(ctx);
   125	                // we need to check both operands because the other operand might be undetermined and maybe a string
   126	                if left_to_numeric_type.is_number() && right_to_numeric_type.is_number() {
   127	                    return ValueType::Number;
   128	                }
   129	                if left_to_numeric_type.is_bigint() && right_to_numeric_type.is_bigint() {
   130	                    return ValueType::BigInt;
   131	                }
   132	                ValueType::Undetermined
   133	            }
   134	            BinaryOperator::Subtraction
   135	            | BinaryOperator::Multiplication
   136	            | BinaryOperator::Division
   137	            | BinaryOperator::Remainder
   138	            | BinaryOperator::ShiftLeft
   139	            | BinaryOperator::BitwiseOR
   140	            | BinaryOperator::ShiftRight
   141	            | BinaryOperator::BitwiseXOR
   142	            | BinaryOperator::BitwiseAnd
   143	            | BinaryOperator::Exponential => {
   144	                let left_to_numeric_type = self.left.to_numeric(ctx);
   145	                let right_to_numeric_type = self.right.to_numeric(ctx);
   146	                // if either operand is a number, the result is always a number
   147	                // because if the other operand is a bigint, an error is thrown
   148	                if left_to_numeric_type.is_number() || right_to_numeric_type.is_number() {
   149	                    ValueType::Number
   150	                } else if left_to_numeric_type.is_bigint() || right_to_numeric_type.is_bigint() {
   151	                    ValueType::BigInt
   152	                } else {
   153	                    ValueType::Undetermined
   154	                }
   155	            }
   156	            BinaryOperator::ShiftRightZeroFill => ValueType::Number,
   157	            BinaryOperator::Instanceof
   158	            | BinaryOperator::In
   159	            | BinaryOperator::Equality
   160	            | BinaryOperator::Inequality
   161	            | BinaryOperator::StrictEquality
   162	            | BinaryOperator::StrictInequality
   163	            | BinaryOperator::LessThan
   164	            | BinaryOperator::GreaterThan
   165	            | BinaryOperator::LessEqualThan
   166	            | BinaryOperator::GreaterEqualThan => ValueType::Boolean,
   167	        }
   168	    }
   169	}
   170	
   171	impl<'a> DetermineValueType<'a> for UnaryExpression<'a> {
   172	    fn value_type(&self, ctx: &impl GlobalContext<'a>) -> ValueType {
   173	        match self.operator {
   174	            UnaryOperator::Void => ValueType::Undefined,
   175	            UnaryOperator::UnaryNegation | UnaryOperator::BitwiseNot => {
   176	                let argument_ty = self.argument.value_type(ctx);
   177	                match argument_ty {
   178	                    ValueType::BigInt => ValueType::BigInt,
   179	                    // non-object values other than BigInt are converted to number by `ToNumber`
   180	                    ValueType::Number
   181	                    | ValueType::Boolean
   182	                    | ValueType::String
   183	                    | ValueType::Null
   184	                    | ValueType::Undefined => ValueType::Number,
   185	                    ValueType::Undetermined | ValueType::Object => ValueType::Undetermined,
   186	                }
   187	            }
   188	            UnaryOperator::UnaryPlus => ValueType::Number,
   189	            UnaryOperator::LogicalNot | UnaryOperator::Delete => ValueType::Boolean,
   190	            UnaryOperator::Typeof => ValueType::String,
   191	        }
   192	    }
   193	}
   194	
   195	impl<'a> DetermineValueType<'a> for AssignmentExpression<'a> {
   196	    fn value_type(&self, ctx: &impl GlobalContext<'a>) -> ValueType {
   197	        match self.operator {
   198	            AssignmentOperator::Assign => self.right.value_type(ctx),
   199	            AssignmentOperator::Addition => {
   200	                let right = self.right.value_type(ctx);
   201	                if right.is_string() { ValueType::String } else { ValueType::Undetermined }
   202	            }
   203	            AssignmentOperator::Subtraction
   204	            | AssignmentOperator::Multiplication
   205	            | AssignmentOperator::Division
   206	            | AssignmentOperator::Remainder
   207	            | AssignmentOperator::ShiftLeft
   208	            | AssignmentOperator::BitwiseOR
   209	            | AssignmentOperator::ShiftRight
   210	            | AssignmentOperator::BitwiseXOR
   211	            | AssignmentOperator::BitwiseAnd
   212	            | AssignmentOperator::Exponential => {
   213	                let right = self.right.value_type(ctx);
   214	                if right.is_bigint() {
   215	                    ValueType::BigInt
   216	                } else if !(right.is_object() || right.is_undetermined()) {
   217	                    ValueType::Number
   218	                } else {
   219	                    ValueType::Undetermined
   220	                }
   221	            }
   222	            AssignmentOperator::ShiftRightZeroFill => ValueType::Number,
   223	            AssignmentOperator::LogicalAnd
   224	            | AssignmentOperator::LogicalOr
   225	            | AssignmentOperator::LogicalNullish => ValueType::Undetermined,
   226	        }
   227	    }
   228	}
   229	
   230	impl<'a> DetermineValueType<'a> for ConditionalExpression<'a> {
   231	    fn value_type(&self, ctx: &impl GlobalContext<'a>) -> ValueType {
   232	        let left = self.consequent.value_type(ctx);
   233	        if left.is_undetermined() {
   234	            return ValueType::Undetermined;
   235	        }
   236	        let right = self.alternate.value_type(ctx);
   237	        if left == right {
   238	            return left;
   239	        }
   240	        ValueType::Undetermined
   241	    }
   242	}
   243	
   244	impl<'a> DetermineValueType<'a> for LogicalExpression<'a> {
   245	    fn value_type(&self, ctx: &impl GlobalContext<'a>) -> ValueType {
   246	        match self.operator {
   247	            LogicalOperator::And | LogicalOperator::Or => {
   248	                let left = self.left.value_type(ctx);
   249	                if left.is_undetermined() {
   250	                    return ValueType::Undetermined;
   251	                }
   252	                let right = self.right.value_type(ctx);
   253	                if left == right {
   254	                    return left;
   255	                }
   256	                ValueType::Undetermined
   257	            }
   258	            LogicalOperator::Coalesce => {
   259	                let left = self.left.value_type(ctx);
   260	                match left {
   261	                    ValueType::Undefined | ValueType::Null => self.right.value_type(ctx),
   262	                    ValueType::Undetermined => ValueType::Undetermined,
   263	                    _ => left,
   264	                }
   265	            }
   266	        }
   267	    }
   268	}
   269	
   270	impl<'a> DetermineValueType<'a> for StaticMemberExpression<'a> {
   271	    fn value_type(&self, ctx: &impl GlobalContext<'a>) -> ValueType {
   272	        if matches!(self.property.name.as_str(), "POSITIVE_INFINITY" | "NEGATIVE_INFINITY")
   273	            && ctx.is_global_expr("Number", &self.object)
   274	        {
   275	            return ValueType::Number;
   276	        }
   277	        ValueType::Undetermined
   278	    }
   279	}
   280	
   281	impl<'a> DetermineValueType<'a> for NewExpression<'a> {
   282	    fn value_type(&self, ctx: &impl GlobalContext<'a>) -> ValueType {
   283	        if ctx.is_global_expr("Date", &self.callee) {
   284	            return ValueType::Object;
   285	        }
   286	        ValueType::Undetermined
   287	    }
   288	}
