     1	use oxc_ast::ast::{Expression, NumberBase};
     2	
     3	use super::{ConstantEvaluation, ConstantEvaluationCtx, DetermineValueType, ValueType};
     4	
     5	/// <https://tc39.es/ecma262/#sec-abstract-equality-comparison>
     6	pub(super) fn abstract_equality_comparison<'a>(
     7	    ctx: &impl ConstantEvaluationCtx<'a>,
     8	    left_expr: &Expression<'a>,
     9	    right_expr: &Expression<'a>,
    10	) -> Option<bool> {
    11	    let left = left_expr.value_type(ctx);
    12	    let right = right_expr.value_type(ctx);
    13	    if left != ValueType::Undetermined && right != ValueType::Undetermined {
    14	        if left == right {
    15	            return strict_equality_comparison(ctx, left_expr, right_expr);
    16	        }
    17	        if matches!(
    18	            (left, right),
    19	            (ValueType::Null, ValueType::Undefined) | (ValueType::Undefined, ValueType::Null)
    20	        ) {
    21	            return Some(true);
    22	        }
    23	
    24	        if matches!((left, right), (ValueType::Number, ValueType::String))
    25	            || matches!(right, ValueType::Boolean)
    26	        {
    27	            if let Some(num) = right_expr.evaluate_value_to_number(ctx) {
    28	                let number_literal_expr = ctx.ast().expression_numeric_literal(
    29	                    oxc_span::SPAN,
    30	                    num,
    31	                    None,
    32	                    if num.fract() == 0.0 { NumberBase::Decimal } else { NumberBase::Float },
    33	                );
    34	                return abstract_equality_comparison(ctx, left_expr, &number_literal_expr);
    35	            }
    36	            return None;
    37	        }
    38	
    39	        if matches!((left, right), (ValueType::String, ValueType::Number))
    40	            || matches!(left, ValueType::Boolean)
    41	        {
    42	            if let Some(num) = left_expr.evaluate_value_to_number(ctx) {
    43	                let number_literal_expr = ctx.ast().expression_numeric_literal(
    44	                    oxc_span::SPAN,
    45	                    num,
    46	                    None,
    47	                    if num.fract() == 0.0 { NumberBase::Decimal } else { NumberBase::Float },
    48	                );
    49	                return abstract_equality_comparison(ctx, &number_literal_expr, right_expr);
    50	            }
    51	            return None;
    52	        }
    53	
    54	        if matches!(left, ValueType::BigInt) || matches!(right, ValueType::BigInt) {
    55	            let left_bigint = left_expr.evaluate_value_to_bigint(ctx);
    56	            let right_bigint = right_expr.evaluate_value_to_bigint(ctx);
    57	            if let (Some(l_big), Some(r_big)) = (left_bigint, right_bigint) {
    58	                return Some(l_big.eq(&r_big));
    59	            }
    60	        }
    61	
    62	        if matches!(left, ValueType::String | ValueType::Number | ValueType::BigInt)
    63	            && matches!(right, ValueType::Object)
    64	        {
    65	            return None;
    66	        }
    67	
    68	        if matches!(left, ValueType::Object)
    69	            && matches!(right, ValueType::String | ValueType::Number | ValueType::BigInt)
    70	        {
    71	            return None;
    72	        }
    73	
    74	        return Some(false);
    75	    }
    76	    None
    77	}
    78	
    79	/// <https://tc39.es/ecma262/#sec-strict-equality-comparison>
    80	#[expect(clippy::float_cmp)]
    81	pub(super) fn strict_equality_comparison<'a>(
    82	    ctx: &impl ConstantEvaluationCtx<'a>,
    83	    left_expr: &Expression<'a>,
    84	    right_expr: &Expression<'a>,
    85	) -> Option<bool> {
    86	    let left = left_expr.value_type(ctx);
    87	    let right = right_expr.value_type(ctx);
    88	    if !left.is_undetermined() && !right.is_undetermined() {
    89	        // Strict equality can only be true for values of the same type.
    90	        if left != right {
    91	            return Some(false);
    92	        }
    93	        return match left {
    94	            ValueType::Number => {
    95	                let lnum = left_expr.get_side_free_number_value(ctx)?;
    96	                let rnum = right_expr.get_side_free_number_value(ctx)?;
    97	                if lnum.is_nan() || rnum.is_nan() {
    98	                    return Some(false);
    99	                }
   100	                Some(lnum == rnum)
   101	            }
   102	            ValueType::String => {
   103	                let left = left_expr.get_side_free_string_value(ctx)?;
   104	                let right = right_expr.get_side_free_string_value(ctx)?;
   105	                Some(left == right)
   106	            }
   107	            ValueType::Undefined | ValueType::Null => Some(true),
   108	            ValueType::Boolean => {
   109	                let left = left_expr.evaluate_value_to_boolean(ctx)?;
   110	                let right = right_expr.evaluate_value_to_boolean(ctx)?;
   111	                Some(left == right)
   112	            }
   113	            ValueType::BigInt => {
   114	                let left = left_expr.get_side_free_bigint_value(ctx)?;
   115	                let right = right_expr.get_side_free_bigint_value(ctx)?;
   116	                Some(left == right)
   117	            }
   118	            ValueType::Object => None,
   119	            ValueType::Undetermined => unreachable!(),
   120	        };
   121	    }
   122	
   123	    // Then, try to evaluate based on the value of the expression.
   124	    // There's only one special case:
   125	    // Any strict equality comparison against NaN returns false.
   126	    if left_expr.is_nan() || right_expr.is_nan() {
   127	        return Some(false);
   128	    }
   129	    None
   130	}
   131

    29	pub trait ConstantEvaluationCtx<'a>: MayHaveSideEffectsContext<'a> {
    30	    fn ast(&self) -> AstBuilder<'a>;
    31	}
    32	
    33	pub trait ConstantEvaluation<'a>: MayHaveSideEffects<'a> {
    34	    /// Evaluate the expression to a constant value.
    35	    ///
    36	    /// Use the specific functions (e.g. [`ConstantEvaluation::evaluate_value_to_boolean`], [`ConstantEvaluation::evaluate_value`]).
    37	    ///
    38	    /// - target_ty: How the result will be used.
    39	    ///   For example, if the result will be converted to a boolean,
    40	    ///   passing `Some(ValueType::Boolean)` will allow us to utilize that information.
    41	    fn evaluate_value_to(
    42	        &self,
    43	        ctx: &impl ConstantEvaluationCtx<'a>,
    44	        target_ty: Option<ValueType>,
    45	    ) -> Option<ConstantValue<'a>>;
    46	
    47	    /// Evaluate the expression to a constant value.
    48	    ///
    49	    /// If you know the result will be converted to a specific type, use other functions (e.g. [`ConstantEvaluation::evaluate_value_to_boolean`]).
    50	    fn evaluate_value(&self, ctx: &impl ConstantEvaluationCtx<'a>) -> Option<ConstantValue<'a>> {
    51	        self.evaluate_value_to(ctx, None)
    52	    }
    53	
    54	    /// Evaluate the expression to a constant value and convert it to a number.
    55	    fn evaluate_value_to_number(&self, ctx: &impl ConstantEvaluationCtx<'a>) -> Option<f64> {
    56	        self.evaluate_value_to(ctx, Some(ValueType::Number))?.to_number(ctx)
    57	    }
    58	
    59	    /// Evaluate the expression to a constant value and convert it to a bigint.
    60	    fn evaluate_value_to_bigint(&self, ctx: &impl ConstantEvaluationCtx<'a>) -> Option<BigInt> {
    61	        self.evaluate_value_to(ctx, Some(ValueType::BigInt))?.into_bigint()
    62	    }
    63	
    64	    /// Evaluate the expression to a constant value and convert it to a boolean.
    65	    fn evaluate_value_to_boolean(&self, ctx: &impl ConstantEvaluationCtx<'a>) -> Option<bool> {
    66	        self.evaluate_value_to(ctx, Some(ValueType::Boolean))?.to_boolean(ctx)
    67	    }
    68	
    69	    /// Evaluate the expression to a constant value and convert it to a string.
    70	    fn evaluate_value_to_string(
    71	        &self,
    72	        ctx: &impl ConstantEvaluationCtx<'a>,
    73	    ) -> Option<Cow<'a, str>> {
    74	        self.evaluate_value_to(ctx, Some(ValueType::String))?.to_js_string(ctx)
    75	    }
    76	
    77	    fn get_side_free_number_value(&self, ctx: &impl ConstantEvaluationCtx<'a>) -> Option<f64> {
    78	        let value = self.evaluate_value_to_number(ctx)?;
    79	        // Calculating the number value, if any, is likely to be faster than calculating side effects,
    80	        // and there are only a very few cases where we can compute a number value, but there could
    81	        // also be side effects. e.g. `void doSomething()` has value NaN, regardless of the behavior
    82	        // of `doSomething()`
    83	        (!self.may_have_side_effects(ctx)).then_some(value)
    84	    }
    85	
    86	    fn get_side_free_bigint_value(&self, ctx: &impl ConstantEvaluationCtx<'a>) -> Option<BigInt> {
    87	        let value = self.evaluate_value_to_bigint(ctx)?;
    88	        (!self.may_have_side_effects(ctx)).then_some(value)
    89	    }
    90	
    91	    fn get_side_free_boolean_value(&self, ctx: &impl ConstantEvaluationCtx<'a>) -> Option<bool> {
    92	        let value = self.evaluate_value_to_boolean(ctx)?;
    93	        (!self.may_have_side_effects(ctx)).then_some(value)
    94	    }
    95	
    96	    fn get_side_free_string_value(
    97	        &self,
    98	        ctx: &impl ConstantEvaluationCtx<'a>,
    99	    ) -> Option<Cow<'a, str>> {
   100	        let value = self.evaluate_value_to_string(ctx)?;
   101	        (!self.may_have_side_effects(ctx)).then_some(value)
   102	    }
   103	}
   104	
   105	impl<'a, T: ConstantEvaluation<'a>> ConstantEvaluation<'a> for Option<T> {
   106	    fn evaluate_value(&self, ctx: &impl ConstantEvaluationCtx<'a>) -> Option<ConstantValue<'a>> {
   107	        self.as_ref().and_then(|t| t.evaluate_value(ctx))
   108	    }
   109	
   110	    fn evaluate_value_to(
   111	        &self,
   112	        ctx: &impl ConstantEvaluationCtx<'a>,
   113	        target_ty: Option<ValueType>,
   114	    ) -> Option<ConstantValue<'a>> {
   115	        self.as_ref().and_then(|t| t.evaluate_value_to(ctx, target_ty))
   116	    }
   117	}
   118	
   119	impl<'a> ConstantEvaluation<'a> for IdentifierReference<'a> {
   120	    fn evaluate_value_to(
   121	        &self,
   122	        ctx: &impl ConstantEvaluationCtx<'a>,
   123	        _target_ty: Option<ValueType>,
   124	    ) -> Option<ConstantValue<'a>> {
   125	        match self.name.as_str() {
   126	            "undefined" if ctx.is_global_reference(self) => Some(ConstantValue::Undefined),
   127	            "NaN" if ctx.is_global_reference(self) => Some(ConstantValue::Number(f64::NAN)),
   128	            "Infinity" if ctx.is_global_reference(self) => {
   129	                Some(ConstantValue::Number(f64::INFINITY))
   130	            }
   131	            _ => self
   132	                .reference_id
   133	                .get()
   134	                .and_then(|reference_id| ctx.get_constant_value_for_reference_id(reference_id)),
   135	        }
   136	    }
   137	}
   138	
   139	impl<'a> ConstantEvaluation<'a> for Expression<'a> {
   140	    fn evaluate_value_to(
   141	        &self,
   142	        ctx: &impl ConstantEvaluationCtx<'a>,
   143	        target_ty: Option<ValueType>,
   144	    ) -> Option<ConstantValue<'a>> {
   145	        let result = match target_ty {
   146	            Some(ValueType::Boolean) => self.to_boolean(ctx).map(ConstantValue::Boolean),
   147	            Some(ValueType::Number) => self.to_number(ctx).map(ConstantValue::Number),
   148	            Some(ValueType::BigInt) => self.to_big_int(ctx).map(ConstantValue::BigInt),
   149	            Some(ValueType::String) => self.to_js_string(ctx).map(ConstantValue::String),
   150	            _ => None,
   151	        };
   152	        if result.is_some() {
   153	            return result;
   154	        }
   155	
   156	        match self {
   157	            Expression::BinaryExpression(e) => e.evaluate_value_to(ctx, target_ty),
   158	            Expression::LogicalExpression(e) => e.evaluate_value_to(ctx, target_ty),
   159	            Expression::UnaryExpression(e) => e.evaluate_value_to(ctx, target_ty),
   160	            Expression::Identifier(ident) => ident.evaluate_value_to(ctx, target_ty),
   161	            Expression::NumericLiteral(lit) => Some(ConstantValue::Number(lit.value)),
   162	            Expression::NullLiteral(_) => Some(ConstantValue::Null),
   163	            Expression::BooleanLiteral(lit) => Some(ConstantValue::Boolean(lit.value)),
   164	            Expression::BigIntLiteral(lit) => lit.to_big_int(ctx).map(ConstantValue::BigInt),
   165	            Expression::StringLiteral(lit) => {
   166	                Some(ConstantValue::String(Cow::Borrowed(lit.value.as_str())))
   167	            }
   168	            Expression::StaticMemberExpression(e) => e.evaluate_value_to(ctx, target_ty),
   169	            Expression::ComputedMemberExpression(e) => e.evaluate_value_to(ctx, target_ty),
   170	            Expression::CallExpression(e) => e.evaluate_value_to(ctx, target_ty),
   171	            Expression::SequenceExpression(e) => {
   172	                // For sequence expression, the value is the value of the RHS.
   173	                e.expressions.last().and_then(|e| e.evaluate_value_to(ctx, target_ty))
   174	            }
   175	            _ => None,
   176	        }
   177	    }
   178	}
   179	
   180	impl<'a> ConstantEvaluation<'a> for BinaryExpression<'a> {
   181	    fn evaluate_value_to(
   182	        &self,
   183	        ctx: &impl ConstantEvaluationCtx<'a>,
   184	        target_ty: Option<ValueType>,
   185	    ) -> Option<ConstantValue<'a>> {
   186	        // FIXME: skipped for now to avoid performance regression, can be removed
   187	        if target_ty == Some(ValueType::Boolean) {
   188	            return None;
   189	        }
   190	
   191	        binary_operation_evaluate_value_to(self.operator, &self.left, &self.right, ctx, target_ty)
   192	    }
   193	}
   194	
   195	pub fn binary_operation_evaluate_value<'a, Ctx: ConstantEvaluationCtx<'a>>(
   196	    operator: BinaryOperator,
   197	    left: &Expression<'a>,
   198	    right: &Expression<'a>,
   199	    ctx: &Ctx,
   200	) -> Option<ConstantValue<'a>> {
   201	    binary_operation_evaluate_value_to(operator, left, right, ctx, None)
   202	}
   203	
   204	fn binary_operation_evaluate_value_to<'a>(
   205	    operator: BinaryOperator,
   206	    left: &Expression<'a>,
   207	    right: &Expression<'a>,
   208	    ctx: &impl ConstantEvaluationCtx<'a>,
   209	    _target_ty: Option<ValueType>,
   210	) -> Option<ConstantValue<'a>> {
   211	    match operator {
   212	        BinaryOperator::Addition => {
   213	            use crate::to_primitive::ToPrimitive;
   214	            let left_to_primitive = left.to_primitive(ctx);
   215	            let right_to_primitive = right.to_primitive(ctx);
   216	            if left_to_primitive.is_string() == Some(true)
   217	                || right_to_primitive.is_string() == Some(true)
   218	            {
   219	                let lval = left.evaluate_value_to_string(ctx)?;
   220	                let rval = right.evaluate_value_to_string(ctx)?;
   221	                return Some(ConstantValue::String(lval + rval));
   222	            }
   223	            let left_to_numeric_type = left_to_primitive.to_numeric(ctx);
   224	            let right_to_numeric_type = right_to_primitive.to_numeric(ctx);
   225	            if left_to_numeric_type.is_number() || right_to_numeric_type.is_number() {
   226	                let lval = left.evaluate_value_to_number(ctx)?;
   227	                let rval = right.evaluate_value_to_number(ctx)?;
   228	                return Some(ConstantValue::Number(lval + rval));
   229	            }
   230	            if left_to_numeric_type.is_bigint() && right_to_numeric_type.is_bigint() {
   231	                let lval = left.evaluate_value_to_bigint(ctx)?;
   232	                let rval = right.evaluate_value_to_bigint(ctx)?;
   233	                return Some(ConstantValue::BigInt(lval + rval));
   234	            }
   235	            None
   236	        }
   237	        BinaryOperator::Subtraction => {
   238	            let lval = left.evaluate_value_to_number(ctx)?;
   239	            let rval = right.evaluate_value_to_number(ctx)?;
   240	            Some(ConstantValue::Number(lval - rval))
   241	        }
   242	        BinaryOperator::Division => {
   243	            let lval = left.evaluate_value_to_number(ctx)?;
   244	            let rval = right.evaluate_value_to_number(ctx)?;
   245	            Some(ConstantValue::Number(lval / rval))
   246	        }
   247	        BinaryOperator::Remainder => {
   248	            let lval = left.evaluate_value_to_number(ctx)?;
   249	            let rval = right.evaluate_value_to_number(ctx)?;
   250	            Some(ConstantValue::Number(if rval.is_zero() { f64::NAN } else { lval % rval }))
   251	        }
   252	        BinaryOperator::Multiplication => {
   253	            let lval = left.evaluate_value_to_number(ctx)?;
   254	            let rval = right.evaluate_value_to_number(ctx)?;
   255	            Some(ConstantValue::Number(lval * rval))
   256	        }
   257	        BinaryOperator::Exponential => {
   258	            let lval = left.evaluate_value_to_number(ctx)?;
   259	            let rval = right.evaluate_value_to_number(ctx)?;
   260	            let result = lval.powf(rval);
   261	            // For now, ignore the result if it large or has a decimal part
   262	            // so that the output does not become bigger than the input.
   263	            if result.is_finite() && (result.fract() != 0.0 || result.log10() > 4.0) {
   264	                return None;
   265	            }
   266	            Some(ConstantValue::Number(result))
   267	        }
   268	        BinaryOperator::ShiftLeft => {
   269	            let left = left.evaluate_value_to_number(ctx)?;
   270	            let right = right.evaluate_value_to_number(ctx)?;
   271	            let left = left.to_int_32();
   272	            let right = right.to_uint_32() & 31;
   273	            Some(ConstantValue::Number(f64::from(left << right)))
   274	        }
   275	        BinaryOperator::ShiftRight => {
   276	            let left = left.evaluate_value_to_number(ctx)?;
   277	            let right = right.evaluate_value_to_number(ctx)?;
   278	            let left = left.to_int_32();
   279	            let right = right.to_uint_32() & 31;
   280	            Some(ConstantValue::Number(f64::from(left >> right)))
   281	        }
   282	        BinaryOperator::ShiftRightZeroFill => {
   283	            let left = left.evaluate_value_to_number(ctx)?;
   284	            let right = right.evaluate_value_to_number(ctx)?;
   285	            let left = left.to_uint_32();
   286	            let right = right.to_uint_32() & 31;
   287	            Some(ConstantValue::Number(f64::from(left >> right)))
   288	        }
   289	        BinaryOperator::LessThan => is_less_than(ctx, left, right).map(|value| match value {
   290	            ConstantValue::Undefined => ConstantValue::Boolean(false),
   291	            _ => value,
   292	        }),
   293	        BinaryOperator::GreaterThan => is_less_than(ctx, right, left).map(|value| match value {
   294	            ConstantValue::Undefined => ConstantValue::Boolean(false),
   295	            _ => value,
   296	        }),
   297	        BinaryOperator::LessEqualThan => is_less_than(ctx, right, left).map(|value| match value {
   298	            ConstantValue::Boolean(true) | ConstantValue::Undefined => {
   299	                ConstantValue::Boolean(false)
   300	            }
   301	            ConstantValue::Boolean(false) => ConstantValue::Boolean(true),
   302	            _ => unreachable!(),
   303	        }),
   304	        BinaryOperator::GreaterEqualThan => {
   305	            is_less_than(ctx, left, right).map(|value| match value {
   306	                ConstantValue::Boolean(true) | ConstantValue::Undefined => {
   307	                    ConstantValue::Boolean(false)
   308	                }
   309	                ConstantValue::Boolean(false) => ConstantValue::Boolean(true),
   310	                _ => unreachable!(),
   311	            })
   312	        }
   313	        BinaryOperator::BitwiseAnd => {
   314	            if left.value_type(ctx).is_bigint() && right.value_type(ctx).is_bigint() {
   315	                let left_biginit = left.evaluate_value_to_bigint(ctx)?;
   316	                let right_bigint = right.evaluate_value_to_bigint(ctx)?;
   317	                return Some(ConstantValue::BigInt(left_biginit & right_bigint));
   318	            }
   319	            let left_int = left.evaluate_value_to_number(ctx)?.to_int_32();
   320	            let right_int = right.evaluate_value_to_number(ctx)?.to_int_32();
   321	            Some(ConstantValue::Number(f64::from(left_int & right_int)))
   322	        }
   323	        BinaryOperator::BitwiseOR => {
   324	            if left.value_type(ctx).is_bigint() && right.value_type(ctx).is_bigint() {
   325	                let left_biginit = left.evaluate_value_to_bigint(ctx)?;
   326	                let right_bigint = right.evaluate_value_to_bigint(ctx)?;
   327	                return Some(ConstantValue::BigInt(left_biginit | right_bigint));
   328	            }
   329	            let left_int = left.evaluate_value_to_number(ctx)?.to_int_32();
   330	            let right_int = right.evaluate_value_to_number(ctx)?.to_int_32();
   331	            Some(ConstantValue::Number(f64::from(left_int | right_int)))
   332	        }
   333	        BinaryOperator::BitwiseXOR => {
   334	            if left.value_type(ctx).is_bigint() && right.value_type(ctx).is_bigint() {
   335	                let left_biginit = left.evaluate_value_to_bigint(ctx)?;
   336	                let right_bigint = right.evaluate_value_to_bigint(ctx)?;
   337	                return Some(ConstantValue::BigInt(left_biginit ^ right_bigint));
   338	            }
   339	            let left_int = left.evaluate_value_to_number(ctx)?.to_int_32();
   340	            let right_int = right.evaluate_value_to_number(ctx)?.to_int_32();
   341	            Some(ConstantValue::Number(f64::from(left_int ^ right_int)))
   342	        }
   343	        BinaryOperator::Instanceof => {
   344	            if let Expression::Identifier(right_ident) = right {
   345	                let name = right_ident.name.as_str();
   346	                if matches!(name, "Object" | "Number" | "Boolean" | "String")
   347	                    && ctx.is_global_reference(right_ident)
   348	                {
   349	                    let left_ty = left.value_type(ctx);
   350	                    if left_ty.is_undetermined() {
   351	                        return None;
   352	                    }
   353	                    if name == "Object" {
   354	                        if !left_ty.is_object() {
   355	                            return Some(ConstantValue::Boolean(false));
   356	                        }
   357	                        if match<response clipped><NOTE>To save on context only part of this file has been shown to you. You should retry this tool after you have searched inside the file with `grep -n` in order to find the line numbers of what you are looking for.</NOTE>

     1	use oxc_ast::ast::Expression;
     2	
     3	use crate::GlobalContext;
     4	
     5	/// `ToBoolean`
     6	///
     7	/// <https://tc39.es/ecma262/multipage/abstract-operations.html#sec-toboolean>
     8	pub trait ToBoolean<'a> {
     9	    fn to_boolean(&self, ctx: &impl GlobalContext<'a>) -> Option<bool>;
    10	}
    11	
    12	impl<'a> ToBoolean<'a> for Expression<'a> {
    13	    fn to_boolean(&self, ctx: &impl GlobalContext<'a>) -> Option<bool> {
    14	        // 1. If argument is a Boolean, return argument.
    15	        // 2. If argument is one of undefined, null, +0𝔽, -0𝔽, NaN, 0ℤ, or the empty String, return false.
    16	        // 3. NOTE: This step is replaced in section B.3.6.1.
    17	        // 4. Return true.
    18	        match self {
    19	            Expression::Identifier(ident) => match ident.name.as_str() {
    20	                "NaN" | "undefined" if ctx.is_global_reference(ident) => Some(false),
    21	                "Infinity" if ctx.is_global_reference(ident) => Some(true),
    22	                _ => None,
    23	            },
    24	            Expression::RegExpLiteral(_)
    25	            | Expression::ArrayExpression(_)
    26	            | Expression::ArrowFunctionExpression(_)
    27	            | Expression::ClassExpression(_)
    28	            | Expression::FunctionExpression(_)
    29	            | Expression::NewExpression(_)
    30	            | Expression::ObjectExpression(_) => Some(true),
    31	            Expression::NullLiteral(_) => Some(false),
    32	            Expression::BooleanLiteral(boolean_literal) => Some(boolean_literal.value),
    33	            Expression::NumericLiteral(lit) => {
    34	                Some(if lit.value.is_nan() { false } else { lit.value != 0.0 })
    35	            }
    36	            Expression::BigIntLiteral(big_int_literal) => Some(!big_int_literal.is_zero()),
    37	            Expression::StringLiteral(string_literal) => Some(!string_literal.value.is_empty()),
    38	            Expression::TemplateLiteral(template_literal) => {
    39	                // only for ``
    40	                template_literal
    41	                    .quasis
    42	                    .first()
    43	                    .filter(|quasi| quasi.tail)
    44	                    .and_then(|quasi| quasi.value.cooked.as_ref())
    45	                    .map(|cooked| !cooked.is_empty())
    46	            }
    47	            Expression::SequenceExpression(e) => {
    48	                e.expressions.last().and_then(|expr| expr.to_boolean(ctx))
    49	            }
    50	            _ => None,
    51	        }
    52	    }
    53	}
    54

     1	#![expect(clippy::print_stdout)]
     2	//! # Minifier Example
     3	//!
     4	//! This example demonstrates the Oxc minifier with options for compression,
     5	//! mangling, and source map generation.
     6	//!
     7	//! ## Usage
     8	//!
     9	//! Create a `test.js` file and run:
    10	//! ```bash
    11	//! cargo run -p oxc_minifier --example minifier [filename] [options]
    12	//! ```
    13	//!
    14	//! ## Options
    15	//!
    16	//! - `--mangle`: Enable variable name mangling
    17	//! - `--nospace`: Remove extra whitespace
    18	//! - `--twice`: Test idempotency by running twice
    19	//! - `--sourcemap`: Generate source maps
    20	//! - `--max-iterations <u8>`: Set the maximum number of compress pass iterations
    21	
    22	use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};
    23	
    24	use pico_args::Arguments;
    25	
    26	use oxc_allocator::Allocator;
    27	use oxc_codegen::{Codegen, CodegenOptions, CodegenReturn, CommentOptions};
    28	use oxc_mangler::MangleOptions;
    29	use oxc_minifier::{CompressOptions, Minifier, MinifierOptions};
    30	use oxc_parser::Parser;
    31	use oxc_sourcemap::SourcemapVisualizer;
    32	use oxc_span::SourceType;
    33	
    34	// Instruction:
    35	// create a `test.js`,
    36	// run `cargo run -p oxc_minifier --example minifier` or `just example minifier`
    37	
    38	fn main() -> std::io::Result<()> {
    39	    let mut args = Arguments::from_env();
    40	
    41	    let mangle = args.contains("--mangle");
    42	    let nospace = args.contains("--nospace");
    43	    let twice = args.contains("--twice");
    44	    let sourcemap = args.contains("--sourcemap");
    45	    let max_iterations = args
    46	        .opt_value_from_str::<&str, u8>("--max-iterations")
    47	        .expect("Invalid number for --max-iterations");
    48	    let name = args.free_from_str().unwrap_or_else(|_| "test.js".to_string());
    49	
    50	    let path = Path::new(&name);
    51	    let source_text = std::fs::read_to_string(path)?;
    52	    let source_type = SourceType::from_path(path).unwrap();
    53	    let source_map_path = sourcemap.then(|| path.to_path_buf());
    54	
    55	    let mut allocator = Allocator::default();
    56	    let ret = minify(
    57	        &allocator,
    58	        &source_text,
    59	        source_type,
    60	        source_map_path,
    61	        mangle,
    62	        nospace,
    63	        max_iterations,
    64	    );
    65	    let printed = ret.code;
    66	    println!("{printed}");
    67	
    68	    if let Some(map) = ret.map {
    69	        let visualizer = SourcemapVisualizer::new(&printed, &map);
    70	        println!("{}", visualizer.get_url());
    71	        println!("{}", visualizer.get_text());
    72	    }
    73	
    74	    if twice {
    75	        allocator.reset();
    76	        let printed2 = minify(&allocator, &printed, source_type, None, mangle, nospace, None).code;
    77	        println!("{printed2}");
    78	        println!("same = {}", printed == printed2);
    79	    }
    80	
    81	    Ok(())
    82	}
    83	
    84	fn minify(
    85	    allocator: &Allocator,
    86	    source_text: &str,
    87	    source_type: SourceType,
    88	    source_map_path: Option<PathBuf>,
    89	    mangle: bool,
    90	    nospace: bool,
    91	    max_iterations: Option<u8>,
    92	) -> CodegenReturn {
    93	    let ret = Parser::new(allocator, source_text, source_type).parse();
    94	    assert!(ret.errors.is_empty());
    95	    let mut program = ret.program;
    96	    let options = MinifierOptions {
    97	        mangle: mangle.then(MangleOptions::default),
    98	        compress: Some(CompressOptions { max_iterations, ..CompressOptions::smallest() }),
    99	    };
   100	    let ret = Minifier::new(options).minify(allocator, &mut program);
   101	    Codegen::new()
   102	        .with_options(CodegenOptions {
   103	            source_map_path,
   104	            minify: nospace,
   105	            comments: CommentOptions::disabled(),
   106	            ..CodegenOptions::default()
   107	        })
   108	        .with_scoping(ret.scoping)
   109	        .build(&program)
   110	}
   111

1. `rafId` identifier's value_type is `Undetermined` (since it's not a global reference)
2. `null` literal's value_type is `Null`
3. The `== null` comparison should not be evaluable to a constant

Let me check if the issue is in the `evaluate_value_to` for the binary expression. Looking at line 187-188 in `mod.rs`:

```rust

    return None;
}
```

There's a FIXME that skips evaluation when target type is boolean. But this is only for the `BinaryExpression` path. The `Expression` path first tries `to_boolean`, then falls through to the match. Let me trace more carefully.

In `Expression::evaluate_value_to`:
1. First tries `self.to_boolean(ctx)` when target_ty is `Some(ValueType::Boolean)`.
2. For a `BinaryExpression`, `to_boolean` is not directly implemented - it falls through to the `_` match arm in `to_boolean` which returns `None`.
3. Then it matches `Expression::BinaryExpression(e) => e.evaluate_value_to(ctx, target_ty)`
4. In `BinaryExpression::evaluate_value_to`, it checks `if target_ty == Some(ValueType::Boolean) { return None; }` - so it returns None.

So the evaluation should fail and the condition should not be removable. Let me check where the removal actually happens. Let me look at `try_fold_if` and how it determines if a condition can be folded

    15	/// Remove Dead Code from the AST.
    16	///
    17	/// Terser option: `dead_code: true`.
    18	///
    19	/// See `KeepVar` at the end of this file for `var` hoisting logic.
    20	/// <https://github.com/google/closure-compiler/blob/v20240609/src/com/google/javascript/jscomp/PeepholeRemoveDeadCode.java>
    21	impl<'a> PeepholeOptimizations {
    22	    /// Remove block from single line blocks
    23	    /// `{ block } -> block`
    24	    pub fn try_optimize_block(stmt: &mut Statement<'a>, ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>) {
    25	        let Statement::BlockStatement(s) = stmt else { return };
    26	        match s.body.len() {
    27	            0 => {
    28	                let parent = ctx.parent();
    29	                if parent.is_while_statement()
    30	                    || parent.is_do_while_statement()
    31	                    || parent.is_for_statement()
    32	                    || parent.is_for_in_statement()
    33	                    || parent.is_for_of_statement()
    34	                    || parent.is_block_statement()
    35	                    || parent.is_program()
    36	                {
    37	                    // Remove the block if it is empty and the parent is a block statement.
    38	                    *stmt = ctx.ast.statement_empty(s.span);
    39	                    ctx.state.changed = true;
    40	                }
    41	            }
    42	            1 => {
    43	                let first = &s.body[0];
    44	                if matches!(first, Statement::VariableDeclaration(decl) if !decl.kind.is_var())
    45	                    || matches!(first, Statement::ClassDeclaration(_))
    46	                    || matches!(first, Statement::FunctionDeclaration(_))
    47	                {
    48	                    return;
    49	                }
    50	                *stmt = s.body.remove(0);
    51	                ctx.state.changed = true;
    52	            }
    53	            _ => {}
    54	        }
    55	    }
    56	
    57	    pub fn try_fold_if(stmt: &mut Statement<'a>, ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>) {
    58	        let Statement::IfStatement(if_stmt) = stmt else { return };
    59	        // Descend and remove `else` blocks first.
    60	        match &mut if_stmt.alternate {
    61	            Some(Statement::IfStatement(_)) => {
    62	                Self::try_fold_if(if_stmt.alternate.as_mut().unwrap(), ctx);
    63	            }
    64	            Some(Statement::BlockStatement(s)) if s.body.is_empty() => {
    65	                if_stmt.alternate = None;
    66	            }
    67	            Some(Statement::EmptyStatement(_)) => {
    68	                if_stmt.alternate = None;
    69	            }
    70	            _ => {}
    71	        }
    72	
    73	        if let Some(boolean) = if_stmt.test.evaluate_value_to_boolean(ctx) {
    74	            let test_has_side_effects = if_stmt.test.may_have_side_effects(ctx);
    75	            // Use "1" and "0" instead of "true" and "false" to be shorter.
    76	            // And also prevent swapping consequent and alternate when `!0` is encountered.
    77	            if !test_has_side_effects {
    78	                if_stmt.test = ctx.ast.expression_numeric_literal(
    79	                    if_stmt.test.span(),
    80	                    if boolean { 1.0 } else { 0.0 },
    81	                    None,
    82	                    NumberBase::Decimal,
    83	                );
    84	            }
    85	            let mut keep_var = KeepVar::new(ctx.ast);
    86	            if boolean {
    87	                if let Some(alternate) = &if_stmt.alternate {
    88	                    keep_var.visit_statement(alternate);
    89	                }
    90	            } else {
    91	                keep_var.visit_statement(&if_stmt.consequent);
    92	            }
    93	            let var_stmt = keep_var
    94	                .get_variable_declaration_statement()
    95	                .and_then(|stmt| Self::remove_unused_variable_declaration(stmt, ctx));
    96	            let has_var_stmt = var_stmt.is_some();
    97	            if let Some(var_stmt) = var_stmt {
    98	                if boolean {
    99	                    if_stmt.alternate = Some(var_stmt);
   100	                } else {
   101	                    if_stmt.consequent = var_stmt;
   102	                }
   103	                return;
   104	            }
   105	            if test_has_side_effects {
   106	                if !has_var_stmt {
   107	                    if boolean {
   108	                        if_stmt.alternate = None;
   109	                    } else {
   110	                        if_stmt.consequent = ctx.ast.statement_empty(if_stmt.consequent.span());
   111	                    }
   112	                }
   113	                return;
   114	            }
   115	            *stmt = if boolean {
   116	                if_stmt.consequent.take_in(ctx.ast)
   117	            } else if let Some(alternate) = if_stmt.alternate.take() {
   118	                alternate
   119	            } else {
   120	                ctx.ast.statement_empty(if_stmt.span)
   121	            };
   122	            ctx.state.changed = true;
   123	        }
   124	    }
   125	
   126	    pub fn try_fold_for(stmt: &mut Statement<'a>, ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>) {
   127	        let Statement::ForStatement(for_stmt) = stmt else { return };
   128	        if let Some(init) = &mut for_stmt.init
   129	            && let Some(init) = init.as_expression_mut()
   130	            && Self::remove_unused_expression(init, ctx)
   131	        {
   132	            for_stmt.init = None;
   133	            ctx.state.changed = true;
   134	        }
   135	        if let Some(update) = &mut for_stmt.update
   136	            && Self::remove_unused_expression(update, ctx)
   137	        {
   138	            for_stmt.update = None;
   139	            ctx.state.changed = true;
   140	        }
   141	
   142	        let test_boolean =
   143	            for_stmt.test.as_ref().and_then(|test| test.evaluate_value_to_boolean(ctx));
   144	        if for_stmt.test.as_ref().is_some_and(|test| test.may_have_side_effects(ctx)) {
   145	            return;
   146	        }
   147	        match test_boolean {
   148	            Some(false) => match &for_stmt.init {
   149	                Some(ForStatementInit::VariableDeclaration(_)) => {
   150	                    let mut keep_var = KeepVar::new(ctx.ast);
   151	                    keep_var.visit_statement(&for_stmt.body);
   152	                    let mut var_decl = keep_var.get_variable_declaration();
   153	                    let Some(ForStatementInit::VariableDeclaration(var_init)) = &mut for_stmt.init
   154	                    else {
   155	                        return;
   156	                    };
   157	                    if var_init.kind.is_var() {
   158	                        if let Some(var_decl) = &mut var_decl {
   159	                            var_decl
   160	                                .declarations
   161	                                .splice(0..0, var_init.declarations.take_in(ctx.ast));
   162	                        } else {
   163	                            var_decl = Some(var_init.take_in_box(ctx.ast));
   164	                        }
   165	                    }
   166	                    *stmt = var_decl.map_or_else(
   167	                        || ctx.ast.statement_empty(for_stmt.span),
   168	                        Statement::VariableDeclaration,
   169	                    );
   170	                    ctx.state.changed = true;
   171	                }
   172	                None => {
   173	                    let mut keep_var = KeepVar::new(ctx.ast);
   174	                    keep_var.visit_statement(&for_stmt.body);
   175	                    *stmt = keep_var.get_variable_declaration().map_or_else(
   176	                        || ctx.ast.statement_empty(for_stmt.span),
   177	                        Statement::VariableDeclaration,
   178	                    );
   179	                    ctx.state.changed = true;
   180	                }
   181	                _ => {}
   182	            },
   183	            Some(true) => {
   184	                // Remove the test expression.
   185	                for_stmt.test = None;
   186	                ctx.state.changed = true;
   187	            }
   188	            None => {}
   189	        }
   190	    }
   191	
   192	    /// Remove meaningless labeled statements.
   193	    ///
   194	    /// ```js
   195	    /// a: break a;
   196	    /// ```
   197	    pub fn try_fold_labeled(stmt: &mut Statement<'a>, ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>) {
   198	        let Statement::LabeledStatement(s) = stmt else { return };
   199	        let id = s.label.name.as_str();
   200	
   201	        if ctx.options().drop_labels.contains(id) {
   202	            *stmt = ctx.ast.statement_empty(s.span);
   203	            ctx.state.changed = true;
   204	            return;
   205	        }
   206	
   207	        // Check the first statement in the block, or just the `break [id] ` statement.
   208	        // Check if we need to remove the whole block.
   209	        match &mut s.body {
   210	            Statement::BreakStatement(break_stmt)
   211	                if break_stmt.label.as_ref().is_some_and(|l| l.name.as_str() == id) => {}
   212	            Statement::BlockStatement(block) if block.body.first().is_some_and(|first| matches!(first, Statement::BreakStatement(break_stmt) if break_stmt.label.as_ref().is_some_and(|l| l.name.as_str() == id))) => {}
   213	            Statement::EmptyStatement(_) => {
   214	                *stmt = ctx.ast.statement_empty(s.span);
   215	                ctx.state.changed = true;
   216	                return;
   217	            }
   218	            _ => return
   219	        }
   220	        let mut var = KeepVar::new(ctx.ast);
   221	        var.visit_statement(&s.body);
   222	        let var_decl = var.get_variable_declaration_statement();
   223	        *stmt = var_decl.unwrap_or_else(|| ctx.ast.statement_empty(s.span));
   224	        ctx.state.changed = true;
   225	    }
   226	
   227	    pub fn try_fold_expression_stmt(stmt: &mut Statement<'a>, ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>) {
   228	        let Statement::ExpressionStatement(expr_stmt) = stmt else { return };
   229	        // We need to check if it is in arrow function with `expression: true`.
   230	        // This is the only scenario where we can't remove it even if `ExpressionStatement`.
   231	        if let Ancestor::ArrowFunctionExpressionBody(body) = ctx.ancestry.ancestor(1)
   232	            && *body.expression()
   233	        {
   234	            return;
   235	        }
   236	
   237	        if Self::remove_unused_expression(&mut expr_stmt.expression, ctx) {
   238	            *stmt = ctx.ast.statement_empty(expr_stmt.span);
   239	            ctx.state.changed = true;
   240	        }
   241	    }
   242	
   243	    pub fn try_fold_try(stmt: &mut Statement<'a>, ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>) {
   244	        let Statement::TryStatement(s) = stmt else { return };
   245	        if let Some(handler) = &s.handler
   246	            && s.block.body.is_empty()
   247	        {
   248	            let mut var = KeepVar::new(ctx.ast);
   249	            var.visit_block_statement(&handler.body);
   250	            let Some(handler) = &mut s.handler else { return };
   251	            handler.body.body.clear();
   252	            if let Some(var_decl) = var.get_variable_declaration_statement() {
   253	                handler.body.body.push(var_decl);
   254	            }
   255	        }
   256	
   257	        if let Some(finalizer) = &s.finalizer
   258	            && finalizer.body.is_empty()
   259	            && s.handler.is_some()
   260	        {
   261	            s.finalizer = None;
   262	        }
   263	
   264	        if s.block.body.is_empty()
   265	            && s.handler.as_ref().is_none_or(|handler| handler.body.body.is_empty())
   266	        {
   267	            *stmt = if let Some(finalizer) = &mut s.finalizer {
   268	                let mut block = ctx.ast.block_statement(finalizer.span, ctx.ast.vec());
   269	                std::mem::swap(&mut **finalizer, &mut block);
   270	                Statement::BlockStatement(ctx.ast.alloc(block))
   271	            } else {
   272	                ctx.ast.statement_empty(s.span)
   273	            };
   274	            ctx.state.changed = true;
   275	        }
   276	    }
   277	
   278	    /// Try folding conditional expression (?:) if the condition results of the condition is known.
   279	    pub fn try_fold_conditional_expression(expr: &mut Expression<'a>, ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>) {
   280	        let Expression::ConditionalExpression(e) = expr else { return };
   281	        let Some(v) = e.test.evaluate_value_to_boolean(ctx) else { return };
   282	        ctx.state.changed = true;
   283	        *expr = if e.test.may_have_side_effects(ctx) {
   284	            // "(a, true) ? b : c" => "a, b"
   285	            let exprs = ctx.ast.vec_from_array([
   286	                {
   287	                    let mut test = e.test.take_in(ctx.ast);
   288	                    Self::remove_unused_expression(&mut test, ctx);
   289	                    test
   290	                },
   291	                if v { e.consequent.take_in(ctx.ast) } else { e.alternate.take_in(ctx.ast) },
   292	            ]);
   293	            ctx.ast.expression_sequence(e.span, exprs)
   294	        } else {
   295	            let result_expr =
   296	                if v { e.consequent.take_in(ctx.ast) } else { e.alternate.take_in(ctx.ast) };
   297	            let should_keep_as_sequence_expr = Self::should_keep_indirect_access(&result_expr, ctx);
   298	            // "(1 ? a.b : 0)()" => "(0, a.b)()"
   299	            if should_keep_as_sequence_expr {
   300	                ctx.ast.expression_sequence(
   301	                    e.span,
   302	                    ctx.ast.vec_from_array([
   303	                        ctx.ast.expression_numeric_literal(e.span, 0.0, None, NumberBase::Decimal),
   304	                        result_expr,
   305	                    ]),
   306	                )
   307	            } else {
   308	                result_expr
   309	            }
   310	        };
   311	    }
   312	
   313	    pub fn remove_sequence_expression(expr: &mut Expression<'a>, ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>) {
   314	        let Expression::SequenceExpression(e) = expr else { return };
   315	        let should_keep_as_sequence_expr = e
   316	            .expressions
   317	            .last()
   318	            .is_some_and(|last_expr| Self::should_keep_indirect_access(last_expr, ctx));
   319	        if should_keep_as_sequence_expr
   320	            && e.expressions.len() == 2
   321	            && e.expressions.first().unwrap().is_number_0()
   322	        {
   323	            return;
   324	        }
   325	        let old_len = e.expressions.len();
   326	        let mut i = 0;
   327	        e.expressions.retain_mut(|e| {
   328	            i += 1;
   329	            if should_keep_as_sequence_expr && i == old_len - 1 {
   330	                if Self::remove_unused_expression(e, ctx) {
   331	                    *e = ctx.ast.expression_numeric_literal(
   332	                        e.span(),
   333	                        0.0,
   334	                        None,
   335	                        NumberBase::Decimal,
   336	                    );
   337	                    ctx.state.changed = true;
   338	                }
   339	                return true;
   340	            }
   341	            if i == old_len {
   342	                return true;
   343	            }
   344	            !Self::remove_unused_expression(e, ctx)
   345	        });
   346	        if e.expressions.len() != old_len {
   347	            ctx.state.changed = true;
   348	        }
   349	        if e.expressions.len() == 1 {
   350	            *expr = e.expressions.pop().unwrap();
   351	            ctx.state.changed = true;
   352	        }
   353	    }
   354	
   355	    pub fn keep_track_of_pure_functions(stmt: &mut Statement<'a>, ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>) {
   356	        match stmt {
   357	            Statement::FunctionDeclaration(f) => {
   358	                if let Some(body) = &f.body {
   359	                    Self::try_save_pure_function(
   360	                        f.id.as_ref(),
   361	                        &f.params,
   362	                        body,
   363	                        f.r#async,
   364	                        f.generator,
   365	                        ctx,
   366	                    );
   367	                }
   368	            }
   369	            Statement::VariableDeclaration(decl) => {
   370	                for d in &decl.declarations {
   371	                    if let BindingPattern::BindingIdentifier(id) = &d.id {
   372	                        match &d.init {
   373	                            Some(Expression::ArrowFunctionExpression(a)) => {
   374	                                Self::try_save_pure_function(
   375	                                    Some(id),
   376	                                    &a.params,
   377	                                    &a.body,
   378	                                    a.r#async,
   379	                                    false,
   380	                                    ctx,
   381	                                );
   382	                            }
   383	                            Some(Expression::FunctionExpression(f)) => {
   384	                                if let Some(body) = &f.body {
   385	                                    Self::try_save_pure_function(
   386	                                        Some(id),
   387	                                        &f.params,
   388	                                        body,
   389	                                        f.r#async,
   390	                                        f.generator,
   391	                                        ctx,
   392	                                    );
   393	                                }
   394	                            }

1. First it tries `self.to_boolean(ctx)`. For a `BinaryExpression`, `to_boolean` is not directly implemented - it falls to the `_ => None` arm. So it returns `None`.
2. Then it matches `Expression::BinaryExpression(e) => e.evaluate_value_to(ctx, target_ty)`.
3. In `BinaryExpression::evaluate_value_to`, line 187-188: `if target_ty == Some(ValueType::Boolean) { return None; }`.

So for `==` comparisons with `null`, the evaluation should return `None` and the condition should not be folded. But the bug shows it IS being folded.

     2	use oxc_ast::ast::*;

    10	impl<'a> PeepholeOptimizations {
    11	    /// Simplify syntax when we know it's used inside a boolean context, e.g. `if (boolean_context) {}`.
    12	    ///
    13	    /// `SimplifyBooleanExpr`: <https://github.com/evanw/esbuild/blob/v0.24.2/internal/js_ast/js_ast_helpers.go#L2059>
    14	    pub fn minimize_expression_in_boolean_context(
    15	        expr: &mut Expression<'a>,
    16	        ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>,
    17	    ) {
    18	        match expr {
    19	            // "!!a" => "a"
    20	            Expression::UnaryExpression(u1) if u1.operator.is_not() => {
    21	                if let Expression::UnaryExpression(u2) = &mut u1.argument
    22	                    && u2.operator.is_not()
    23	                {
    24	                    let mut e = u2.argument.take_in(ctx.ast);
    25	                    Self::minimize_expression_in_boolean_context(&mut e, ctx);
    26	                    *expr = e;
    27	                    ctx.state.changed = true;
    28	                }
    29	            }
    30	            Expression::BinaryExpression(e)
    31	                if e.operator.is_equality()
    32	                    && matches!(&e.right, Expression::NumericLiteral(lit) if lit.value == 0.0)
    33	                    && e.left.is_int32_or_uint32(ctx) =>
    34	            {
    35	                let argument = e.left.take_in(ctx.ast);
    36	                *expr = if matches!(
    37	                    e.operator,
    38	                    BinaryOperator::StrictInequality | BinaryOperator::Inequality
    39	                ) {
    40	                    // `if ((a | b) !== 0)` -> `if (a | b);`
    41	                    argument
    42	                } else {
    43	                    // `if ((a | b) === 0);", "if (!(a | b));")`
    44	                    ctx.ast.expression_unary(e.span, UnaryOperator::LogicalNot, argument)
    45	                };
    46	                ctx.state.changed = true;
    47	            }
    48	            // "if (!!a && !!b)" => "if (a && b)"
    49	            Expression::LogicalExpression(e) if e.operator.is_and() => {
    50	                Self::minimize_expression_in_boolean_context(&mut e.left, ctx);
    51	                Self::minimize_expression_in_boolean_context(&mut e.right, ctx);
    52	                // "if (anything && truthyNoSideEffects)" => "if (anything)"
    53	                if e.right.get_side_free_boolean_value(ctx) == Some(true) {
    54	                    *expr = e.left.take_in(ctx.ast);
    55	                    ctx.state.changed = true;
    56	                }
    57	            }
    58	            // "if (!!a ||!!b)" => "if (a || b)"
    59	            Expression::LogicalExpression(e) if e.operator == LogicalOperator::Or => {
    60	                Self::minimize_expression_in_boolean_context(&mut e.left, ctx);
    61	                Self::minimize_expression_in_boolean_context(&mut e.right, ctx);
    62	                // "if (anything || falsyNoSideEffects)" => "if (anything)"
    63	                if e.right.get_side_free_boolean_value(ctx) == Some(false) {
    64	                    *expr = e.left.take_in(ctx.ast);
    65	                    ctx.state.changed = true;
    66	                }
    67	            }
    68	            Expression::ConditionalExpression(e) => {
    69	                // "if (a ? !!b : !!c)" => "if (a ? b : c)"
    70	                Self::minimize_expression_in_boolean_context(&mut e.consequent, ctx);
    71	                Self::minimize_expression_in_boolean_context(&mut e.alternate, ctx);
    72	                if let Some(boolean) = e.consequent.get_side_free_boolean_value(ctx) {
    73	                    let right = e.alternate.take_in(ctx.ast);
    74	                    let left = e.test.take_in(ctx.ast);
    75	                    let span = e.span;
    76	                    let (op, left) = if boolean {
    77	                        // "if (anything1 ? truthyNoSideEffects : anything2)" => "if (anything1 || anything2)"
    78	                        (LogicalOperator::Or, left)
    79	                    } else {
    80	                        // "if (anything1 ? falsyNoSideEffects : anything2)" => "if (!anything1 && anything2)"
    81	                        (LogicalOperator::And, Self::minimize_not(left.span(), left, ctx))
    82	                    };
    83	                    *expr = Self::join_with_left_associative_op(span, op, left, right, ctx);
    84	                    ctx.state.changed = true;
    85	                    return;
    86	                }
    87	                if let Some(boolean) = e.alternate.get_side_free_boolean_value(ctx) {
    88	                    let left = e.test.take_in(ctx.ast);
    89	                    let right = e.consequent.take_in(ctx.ast);
    90	                    let span = e.span;
    91	                    let (op, left) = if boolean {
    92	                        // "if (anything1 ? anything2 : truthyNoSideEffects)" => "if (!anything1 || anything2)"
    93	                        (LogicalOperator::Or, Self::minimize_not(left.span(), left, ctx))
    94	                    } else {
    95	                        // "if (anything1 ? anything2 : falsyNoSideEffects)" => "if (anything1 && anything2)"
    96	                        (LogicalOperator::And, left)
    97	                    };
    98	                    *expr = Self::join_with_left_associative_op(span, op, left, right, ctx);
    99	                    ctx.state.changed = true;
   100	                }
   101	            }
   102	            Expression::SequenceExpression(seq_expr) => {
   103	                if let Some(last) = seq_expr.expressions.last_mut() {
   104	                    Self::minimize_expression_in_boolean_context(last, ctx);
   105	                }
   106	            }
   107	            _ => {}
   108	        }
   109	    }

    21	use super::TraverseCtx;
    22	
    23	pub fn is_exact_int64(num: f64) -> bool {
    24	    num.fract() == 0.0
    25	}

    27	impl<'a> GlobalContext<'a> for TraverseCtx<'a, MinifierState<'a>> {
    28	    fn is_global_reference(&self, ident: &IdentifierReference<'_>) -> bool {
    29	        ident.is_global_reference(self.scoping())
    30	    }
    31	
    32	    fn get_constant_value_for_reference_id(
    33	        &self,
    34	        reference_id: ReferenceId,
    35	    ) -> Option<ConstantValue<'a>> {
    36	        self.scoping()
    37	            .get_reference(reference_id)
    38	            .symbol_id()
    39	            .and_then(|symbol_id| self.state.symbol_values.get_symbol_value(symbol_id))
    40	            .filter(|sv| sv.write_references_count == 0)
    41	            .and_then(|sv| sv.initialized_constant.as_ref())
    42	            .cloned()
    43	    }
    44	}

    77	    fn manual_pure_functions(&self, callee: &Expression) -> bool {
    78	        let pure_functions = &self.state.options.treeshake.manual_pure_functions;
    79	        if pure_functions.is_empty() {
    80	            return false;
    81	        }
    82	        is_pure_function(callee, pure_functions)
    83	    }

   160	impl<'a> TraverseCtx<'a, MinifierState<'a>> {
   161	    pub fn options(&self) -> &CompressOptions {
   162	        &self.state.options
   163	    }
   164	
   165	    /// Check if the target engines supports a feature.
   166	    ///
   167	    /// Returns `true` if the feature is supported.
   168	    pub fn supports_feature(&self, feature: ESFeature) -> bool {
   169	        !self.options().target.has_feature(feature)
   170	    }
   171	
   172	    pub fn source_type(&self) -> SourceType {
   173	        self.state.source_type
   174	    }
   175	
   176	    pub fn is_global_reference(&self, ident: &IdentifierReference<'a>) -> bool {
   177	        ident.is_global_reference(self.scoping())
   178	    }
   179	
   180	    pub fn eval_binary(&self, e: &BinaryExpression<'a>) -> Option<Expression<'a>> {
   181	        if e.may_have_side_effects(self) {
   182	            None
   183	        } else {
   184	            e.evaluate_value(self).map(|v| self.value_to_expr(e.span, v))
   185	        }
   186	    }
   187	
   188	    pub fn eval_binary_operation(
   189	        &self,
   190	        operator: BinaryOperator,
   191	        left: &Expression<'a>,
   192	        right: &Expression<'a>,
   193	    ) -> Option<ConstantValue<'a>> {
   194	        binary_operation_evaluate_value(operator, left, right, self)
   195	    }
   196	
   197	    pub fn value_to_expr(&self, span: Span, value: ConstantValue<'a>) -> Expression<'a> {
   198	        match value {
   199	            ConstantValue::Number(n) => {
   200	                let number_base =
   201	                    if is_exact_int64(n) { NumberBase::Decimal } else { NumberBase::Float };
   202	                self.ast.expression_numeric_literal(span, n, None, number_base)
   203	            }
   204	            ConstantValue::BigInt(bigint) => {
   205	                let value = format_str!(self.ast.allocator, "{bigint}");
   206	                self.ast.expression_big_int_literal(span, value, None, BigintBase::Decimal)
   207	            }
   208	            ConstantValue::String(s) => {
   209	                self.ast.expression_string_literal(span, self.ast.str_from_cow(&s), None)
   210	            }
   211	            ConstantValue::Boolean(b) => self.ast.expression_boolean_literal(span, b),
   212	            ConstantValue::Undefined => self.ast.void_0(span),
   213	            ConstantValue::Null => self.ast.expression_null_literal(span),
   214	        }
   215	    }
   216	
   217	    pub fn is_expression_undefined(&self, expr: &Expression) -> bool {
   218	        match expr {
   219	            Expression::Identifier(ident) if self.is_identifier_undefined(ident) => true,
   220	            Expression::UnaryExpression(e) if e.operator.is_void() && e.argument.is_number() => {
   221	                true
   222	            }
   223	            _ => false,
   224	        }
   225	    }
   226	
   227	    #[inline]
   228	    pub fn is_identifier_undefined(&self, ident: &IdentifierReference) -> bool {
   229	        if ident.name == "undefined" && ident.is_global_reference(self.scoping()) {
   230	            return true;
   231	        }
   232	        false
   233	    }
   234	
   235	    pub fn init_value(
   236	        &mut self,
   237	        symbol_id: SymbolId,
   238	        constant: Option<ConstantValue<'a>>,
   239	        is_fresh_value: bool,
   240	    ) {
   241	        let mut exported = false;
   242	        if self.scoping.current_scope_id() == self.scoping().root_scope_id() {
   243	            for ancestor in self.ancestors() {
   244	                if ancestor.is_export_named_declaration()
   245	                    || ancestor.is_export_all_declaration()
   246	                    || ancestor.is_export_default_declaration()
   247	                {
   248	                    exported = true;
   249	                }
   250	            }
   251	        }
   252	
   253	        let mut read_references_count = 0;
   254	        let mut write_references_count = 0;
   255	        let mut member_write_target_read_count = 0;
   256	        for r in self.scoping().get_resolved_references(symbol_id) {
   257	            if r.is_read() {
   258	                read_references_count += 1;
   259	            }
   260	            if r.is_write() {
   261	                write_references_count += 1;
   262	            }
   263	            if r.flags().is_member_write_target() {
   264	                member_write_target_read_count += 1;
   265	            }
   266	        }
   267	
   268	        let scope_id = self.scoping().symbol_scope_id(symbol_id);
   269	        let scope_flags = self.scoping().scope_flags(scope_id);
   270	
   271	        let initialized_constant =
   272	            if scope_flags.contains(ScopeFlags::DirectEval) { None } else { constant };
   273	
   274	        let symbol_value = SymbolValue {
   275	            initialized_constant,
   276	            exported,
   277	            read_references_count,
   278	            write_references_count,
   279	            member_write_target_read_count,
   280	            is_fresh_value,
   281	            scope_id,
   282	        };
   283	        self.state.symbol_values.init_value(symbol_id, symbol_value);
   284	    }
   285	
   286	    /// If two expressions are equal.
   287	    /// Special case `undefined` == `void 0`
   288	    pub fn expr_eq(&self, a: &Expression<'a>, b: &Expression<'a>) -> bool {
   289	        use oxc_span::ContentEq;
   290	        a.content_eq(b) || (self.is_expression_undefined(a) && self.is_expression_undefined(b))
   291	    }
   292	
   293	    // https://github.com/evanw/esbuild/blob/v0.24.2/internal/js_ast/js_ast_helpers.go#L2641
   294	    pub fn string_to_equivalent_number_value(s: &str) -> Option<f64> {
   295	        if s.is_empty() {
   296	            return None;
   297	        }
   298	        let mut is_negative = false;
   299	        let mut int_value = 0i32;
   300	        let mut start = 0;
   301	        let bytes = s.as_bytes();
   302	        if bytes[0] == b'-' && s.len() > 1 {
   303	            is_negative = true;
   304	            int_value = -int_value;
   305	            start += 1;
   306	        }
   307	        if bytes[start] == b'0' && s.len() > 1 {
   308	            return None;
   309	        }
   310	        for b in &bytes[start..] {
   311	            if !b.is_ascii_digit() {
   312	                return None;
   313	            }
   314	            int_value = int_value.checked_mul(10).and_then(|v| {
   315	                let n = i32::from(b & 15);
   316	                if is_negative { v.checked_sub(n) } else { v.checked_add(n) }
   317	            })?;
   318	        }
   319	        Some(f64::from(int_value))
   320	    }
   321	
   322	    /// Whether the closest function scope is created by an async generator
   323	    pub fn is_closest_function_scope_an_async_generator(&self) -> bool {
   324	        self.ancestors()
   325	            .find_map(|ancestor| match ancestor {
   326	                Ancestor::FunctionBody(body) => Some(*body.r#async() && *body.generator()),
   327	                Ancestor::ArrowFunctionExpressionBody(_) => Some(false),
   328	                _ => None,
   329	            })
   330	            .unwrap_or_default()
   331	    }
   332	
   333	    /// Whether the assignment expression needs to be kept to preserve the name
   334	    pub fn is_expression_whose_name_needs_to_be_kept(&self, expr: &Expression) -> bool {
   335	        let options = &self.options().keep_names;
   336	        if !options.class && !options.function {
   337	            return false;
   338	        }
   339	        if !expr.is_anonymous_function_definition() {
   340	            return false;
   341	        }
   342	        let is_class = matches!(expr.without_parentheses(), Expression::ClassExpression(_));
   343	        (options.class && is_class) || (options.function && !is_class)
   344	    }
   345	}
   346

   396	impl<'a> ConstantEvaluation<'a> for LogicalExpression<'a> {
   397	    fn evaluate_value_to(
   398	        &self,
   399	        ctx: &impl ConstantEvaluationCtx<'a>,
   400	        target_ty: Option<ValueType>,
   401	    ) -> Option<ConstantValue<'a>> {
   402	        match self.operator {
   403	            LogicalOperator::And => match self.left.evaluate_value_to_boolean(ctx) {
   404	                Some(true) => self.right.evaluate_value(ctx),
   405	                Some(false) => self.left.evaluate_value(ctx),
   406	                None => {
   407	                    // ToBoolean(a && false) -> false
   408	                    if target_ty == Some(ValueType::Boolean)
   409	                        && self.right.evaluate_value_to_boolean(ctx) == Some(false)
   410	                    {
   411	                        Some(ConstantValue::Boolean(false))
   412	                    } else {
   413	                        None
   414	                    }
   415	                }
   416	            },
   417	            LogicalOperator::Or => match self.left.evaluate_value_to_boolean(ctx) {
   418	                Some(true) => self.left.evaluate_value(ctx),
   419	                Some(false) => self.right.evaluate_value(ctx),
   420	                None => {
   421	                    // ToBoolean(a || true) -> true
   422	                    if target_ty == Some(ValueType::Boolean)
   423	                        && self.right.evaluate_value_to_boolean(ctx) == Some(true)
   424	                    {
   425	                        Some(ConstantValue::Boolean(true))
   426	                    } else {
   427	                        None
   428	                    }
   429	                }
   430	            },
   431	            LogicalOperator::Coalesce => None,
   432	        }
   433	    }
   434	}
   435	
   436	impl<'a> ConstantEvaluation<'a> for UnaryExpression<'a> {
   437	    fn evaluate_value_to(
   438	        &self,
   439	        ctx: &impl ConstantEvaluationCtx<'a>,
   440	        _target_ty: Option<ValueType>,
   441	    ) -> Option<ConstantValue<'a>> {
   442	        match self.operator {
   443	            UnaryOperator::Typeof => {
   444	                let arg_ty = self.argument.value_type(ctx);
   445	                let s = match arg_ty {
   446	                    ValueType::BigInt => "bigint",
   447	                    ValueType::Number => "number",
   448	                    ValueType::String => "string",
   449	                    ValueType::Boolean => "boolean",
   450	                    ValueType::Undefined => "undefined",

Now I can trace through for `rafId == null`:
- `operator = BinaryOperator::Equality`
- It goes to line 384-386:
  ```rust
  BinaryOperator::Equality => {
      let value = abstract_equality_comparison(ctx, left, right)?;
      Some(ConstantValue::Boolean(value))
  }
  ```

Now in `abstract_equality_comparison` (equality_comparison.rs):
- `left = Identifier("rafId")`, `right = NullLiteral`
- `left_val = left.value_type(ctx)` = `ValueType::Undetermined` (because `rafId` is not a global ref)
- `right_val = right.value_type(ctx)` = `ValueType::Null`
- Since `left` is `Undetermined`, the condition `left != ValueType::Undetermined && right != ValueType::Undetermined` is FALSE
- So it returns `None`

So `abstract_equality_comparison` returns `None`, and the `?` causes `binary_operation_evaluate_value_to` to return `None`.

So `eval_binary` should return `None`. And `evaluate_value_to_boolean` also returns `None` via the FIXME shortcut.

   546	    pub fn substitute_loose_equals_undefined(expr: &mut Expression<'a>, ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>) {
   547	        let Expression::BinaryExpression(e) = expr else { return };
   548	        // `foo == void 0` -> `foo == null`, `foo == undefined` -> `foo == null`
   549	        // `foo != void 0` -> `foo == null`, `foo == undefined` -> `foo == null`
   550	        if e.operator == BinaryOperator::Inequality || e.operator == BinaryOperator::Equality {
   551	            let (left, right) = if ctx.is_expression_undefined(&e.right) {
   552	                (e.left.take_in(ctx.ast), ctx.ast.expression_null_literal(e.right.span()))
   553	            } else if ctx.is_expression_undefined(&e.left) {
   554	                (e.right.take_in(ctx.ast), ctx.ast.expression_null_literal(e.left.span()))
   555	            } else {
   556	                return;
   557	            };
   558	            *expr = ctx.ast.expression_binary(e.span, left, e.operator, right);
   559	            ctx.state.changed = true;
   560	        }
   561	    }

   566	    /// Transforms the common Babel/TS output:
   567	    /// ```js
   568	    ///   for (var e = arguments.length, r = Array(e), a = 0; a < e; a++)
   569	    ///     r[a] = arguments[a];
   570	    /// ```
   571	    /// into:
   572	    /// ```js
   573	    ///   for (var r = [...arguments]; 0; ) ;
   574	    /// ```

   578	    /// ```

     1	use crate::TraverseCtx;
     2	use oxc_allocator::TakeIn;
     3	use oxc_ast::{NONE, ast::*};
     4	use oxc_compat::ESFeature;
     5	use oxc_ecmascript::{
     6	    constant_evaluation::{ConstantEvaluation, ConstantValue, DetermineValueType},

     9	use oxc_span::{ContentEq, GetSpan};
