    11	use super::PeepholeOptimizations;
    12	
    13	impl<'a> PeepholeOptimizations {
    14	    pub fn minimize_conditional(
    15	        span: Span,
    16	        test: Expression<'a>,
    17	        consequent: Expression<'a>,
    18	        alternate: Expression<'a>,
    19	        ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>,
    20	    ) -> Expression<'a> {
    21	        let mut cond_expr = ctx.ast.conditional_expression(span, test, consequent, alternate);
    22	        Self::minimize_conditional_expression(&mut cond_expr, ctx)
    23	            .unwrap_or_else(|| Expression::ConditionalExpression(ctx.ast.alloc(cond_expr)))
    24	    }
    25	
    26	    /// `MangleIfExpr`: <https://github.com/evanw/esbuild/blob/v0.24.2/internal/js_ast/js_ast_helpers.go#L2745>
    27	    pub fn minimize_conditional_expression(
    28	        expr: &mut ConditionalExpression<'a>,
    29	        ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>,
    30	    ) -> Option<Expression<'a>> {
    31	        match &mut expr.test {
    32	            // "(a, b) ? c : d" => "a, b ? c : d"
    33	            Expression::SequenceExpression(sequence_expr)
    34	                if sequence_expr.expressions.len() > 1 =>
    35	            {
    36	                let span = expr.span();
    37	                let mut sequence = expr.test.take_in(ctx.ast);
    38	                let Expression::SequenceExpression(sequence_expr) = &mut sequence else {
    39	                    unreachable!()
    40	                };
    41	                let expr = Self::minimize_conditional(
    42	                    span,
    43	                    sequence_expr.expressions.pop().unwrap(),
    44	                    expr.consequent.take_in(ctx.ast),
    45	                    expr.alternate.take_in(ctx.ast),
    46	                    ctx,
    47	                );
    48	                sequence_expr.expressions.push(expr);
    49	                return Some(sequence);
    50	            }
    51	            // "!a ? b : c" => "a ? c : b"
    52	            Expression::UnaryExpression(test_expr) if test_expr.operator.is_not() => {
    53	                let test = test_expr.argument.take_in(ctx.ast);
    54	                let consequent = expr.alternate.take_in(ctx.ast);
    55	                let alternate = expr.consequent.take_in(ctx.ast);
    56	                return Some(Self::minimize_conditional(
    57	                    expr.span, test, consequent, alternate, ctx,
    58	                ));
    59	            }
    60	            Expression::Identifier(id) => {
    61	                // "a ? a : b" => "a || b"
    62	                if let Expression::Identifier(id2) = &expr.consequent
    63	                    && id.name == id2.name
    64	                {
    65	                    return Some(Self::join_with_left_associative_op(
    66	                        expr.span,
    67	                        LogicalOperator::Or,
    68	                        expr.test.take_in(ctx.ast),
    69	                        expr.alternate.take_in(ctx.ast),
    70	                        ctx,
    71	                    ));
    72	                }
    73	                // "a ? b : a" => "a && b"
    74	                if let Expression::Identifier(id2) = &expr.alternate
    75	                    && id.name == id2.name
    76	                {
    77	                    return Some(Self::join_with_left_associative_op(
    78	                        expr.span,
    79	                        LogicalOperator::And,
    80	                        expr.test.take_in(ctx.ast),
    81	                        expr.consequent.take_in(ctx.ast),
    82	                        ctx,
    83	                    ));
    84	                }
    85	            }
    86	            // `x != y ? b : c` -> `x == y ? c : b`
    87	            Expression::BinaryExpression(test_expr) => {
    88	                if matches!(
    89	                    test_expr.operator,
    90	                    BinaryOperator::Inequality | BinaryOperator::StrictInequality
    91	                ) {
    92	                    test_expr.operator = test_expr.operator.equality_inverse_operator().unwrap();
    93	                    let test = expr.test.take_in(ctx.ast);
    94	                    let consequent = expr.consequent.take_in(ctx.ast);
    95	                    let alternate = expr.alternate.take_in(ctx.ast);
    96	                    return Some(Self::minimize_conditional(
    97	                        expr.span, test, alternate, consequent, ctx,
    98	                    ));
    99	                }
   100	            }
   101	            _ => {}
   102	        }
   103	
   104	        // "a ? b ? c : d : d" => "a && b ? c : d"
   105	        if let Expression::ConditionalExpression(consequent) = &mut expr.consequent
   106	            && ctx.expr_eq(&consequent.alternate, &expr.alternate)
   107	        {
   108	            return Some(ctx.ast.expression_conditional(
   109	                expr.span,
   110	                Self::join_with_left_associative_op(
   111	                    expr.test.span(),
   112	                    LogicalOperator::And,
   113	                    expr.test.take_in(ctx.ast),
   114	                    consequent.test.take_in(ctx.ast),
   115	                    ctx,
   116	                ),
   117	                consequent.consequent.take_in(ctx.ast),
   118	                consequent.alternate.take_in(ctx.ast),
   119	            ));
   120	        }
   121	
   122	        // "a ? b : c ? b : d" => "a || c ? b : d"
   123	        if let Expression::ConditionalExpression(alternate) = &mut expr.alternate
   124	            && ctx.expr_eq(&alternate.consequent, &expr.consequent)
   125	        {
   126	            return Some(ctx.ast.expression_conditional(
   127	                expr.span,
   128	                Self::join_with_left_associative_op(
   129	                    expr.test.span(),
   130	                    LogicalOperator::Or,
   131	                    expr.test.take_in(ctx.ast),
   132	                    alternate.test.take_in(ctx.ast),
   133	                    ctx,
   134	                ),
   135	                expr.consequent.take_in(ctx.ast),
   136	                alternate.alternate.take_in(ctx.ast),
   137	            ));
   138	        }
   139	
   140	        // "a ? c : (b, c)" => "(a || b), c"
   141	        if let Expression::SequenceExpression(alternate) = &mut expr.alternate
   142	            && alternate.expressions.len() == 2
   143	            && ctx.expr_eq(&alternate.expressions[1], &expr.consequent)
   144	        {
   145	            return Some(ctx.ast.expression_sequence(
   146	                expr.span,
   147	                ctx.ast.vec_from_array([
   148	                    Self::join_with_left_associative_op(
   149	                        expr.test.span(),
   150	                        LogicalOperator::Or,
   151	                        expr.test.take_in(ctx.ast),
   152	                        alternate.expressions[0].take_in(ctx.ast),
   153	                        ctx,
   154	                    ),
   155	                    expr.consequent.take_in(ctx.ast),
   156	                ]),
   157	            ));
   158	        }
   159	
   160	        // "a ? (b, c) : c" => "(a && b), c"
   161	        if let Expression::SequenceExpression(consequent) = &mut expr.consequent
   162	            && consequent.expressions.len() == 2
   163	            && ctx.expr_eq(&consequent.expressions[1], &expr.alternate)
   164	        {
   165	            return Some(ctx.ast.expression_sequence(
   166	                expr.span,
   167	                ctx.ast.vec_from_array([
   168	                    Self::join_with_left_associative_op(
   169	                        expr.test.span(),
   170	                        LogicalOperator::And,
   171	                        expr.test.take_in(ctx.ast),
   172	                        consequent.expressions[0].take_in(ctx.ast),
   173	                        ctx,
   174	                    ),
   175	                    expr.alternate.take_in(ctx.ast),
   176	                ]),
   177	            ));
   178	        }
   179	
   180	        // "a ? b || c : c" => "(a && b) || c"
   181	        if let Expression::LogicalExpression(logical_expr) = &mut expr.consequent
   182	            && logical_expr.operator.is_or()
   183	            && ctx.expr_eq(&logical_expr.right, &expr.alternate)
   184	        {
   185	            return Some(ctx.ast.expression_logical(
   186	                expr.span,
   187	                Self::join_with_left_associative_op(
   188	                    expr.test.span(),
   189	                    LogicalOperator::And,
   190	                    expr.test.take_in(ctx.ast),
   191	                    logical_expr.left.take_in(ctx.ast),
   192	                    ctx,
   193	                ),
   194	                LogicalOperator::Or,
   195	                expr.alternate.take_in(ctx.ast),
   196	            ));
   197	        }
   198	
   199	        // "a ? c : b && c" => "(a || b) && c"
   200	        if let Expression::LogicalExpression(logical_expr) = &mut expr.alternate
   201	            && logical_expr.operator == LogicalOperator::And
   202	            && ctx.expr_eq(&logical_expr.right, &expr.consequent)
   203	        {
   204	            return Some(ctx.ast.expression_logical(
   205	                expr.span,
   206	                Self::join_with_left_associative_op(
   207	                    expr.test.span(),
   208	                    LogicalOperator::Or,
   209	                    expr.test.take_in(ctx.ast),
   210	                    logical_expr.left.take_in(ctx.ast),
   211	                    ctx,
   212	                ),
   213	                LogicalOperator::And,
   214	                expr.consequent.take_in(ctx.ast),
   215	            ));
   216	        }
   217	
   218	        // `a ? b(c, d) : b(e, d)` -> `b(a ? c : e, d)`
   219	        if let (Expression::CallExpression(consequent), Expression::CallExpression(alternate)) =
   220	            (&mut expr.consequent, &mut expr.alternate)
   221	        {
   222	            // `a ? b() : b()` is handled later
   223	            if !consequent.arguments.is_empty() &&
   224	                consequent.arguments.len() == alternate.arguments.len()
   225	                // we can improve compression by allowing side effects on one side if the other side is
   226	                // an identifier that is not modified after it is declared.
   227	                // but for now, we only perform compression if neither side has side effects.
   228	                && !expr.test.may_have_side_effects(ctx)
   229	                && !consequent.callee.may_have_side_effects(ctx)
   230	                && ctx.expr_eq(&consequent.callee, &alternate.callee)
   231	                && consequent
   232	                    .arguments
   233	                    .iter()
   234	                    .zip(&alternate.arguments)
   235	                    .skip(1)
   236	                    .all(|(a, b)| a.content_eq(b))
   237	            {
   238	                // `a ? b(...c) : b(...e)` -> `b(...a ? c : e)``
   239	                if matches!(consequent.arguments[0], Argument::SpreadElement(_))
   240	                    && matches!(alternate.arguments[0], Argument::SpreadElement(_))
   241	                {
   242	                    let callee = consequent.callee.take_in(ctx.ast);
   243	                    let consequent_first_arg = {
   244	                        let Argument::SpreadElement(el) = &mut consequent.arguments[0] else {
   245	                            unreachable!()
   246	                        };
   247	                        el.argument.take_in(ctx.ast)
   248	                    };
   249	                    let alternate_first_arg = {
   250	                        let Argument::SpreadElement(el) = &mut alternate.arguments[0] else {
   251	                            unreachable!()
   252	                        };
   253	                        el.argument.take_in(ctx.ast)
   254	                    };
   255	                    let mut args = std::mem::replace(&mut consequent.arguments, ctx.ast.vec());
   256	                    args[0] = ctx.ast.argument_spread_element(
   257	                        expr.span,
   258	                        ctx.ast.expression_conditional(
   259	                            expr.test.span(),
   260	                            expr.test.take_in(ctx.ast),
   261	                            consequent_first_arg,
   262	                            alternate_first_arg,
   263	                        ),
   264	                    );
   265	                    return Some(ctx.ast.expression_call(expr.span, callee, NONE, args, false));
   266	                }
   267	                // `a ? b(c) : b(e)` -> `b(a ? c : e)`
   268	                if !matches!(consequent.arguments[0], Argument::SpreadElement(_))
   269	                    && !matches!(alternate.arguments[0], Argument::SpreadElement(_))
   270	                {
   271	                    let callee = consequent.callee.take_in(ctx.ast);
   272	
   273	                    let consequent_first_arg =
   274	                        consequent.arguments[0].to_expression_mut().take_in(ctx.ast);
   275	                    let alternate_first_arg =
   276	                        alternate.arguments[0].to_expression_mut().take_in(ctx.ast);
   277	                    let mut args = std::mem::replace(&mut consequent.arguments, ctx.ast.vec());
   278	                    let cond_expr = Self::minimize_conditional(
   279	                        expr.test.span(),
   280	                        expr.test.take_in(ctx.ast),
   281	                        consequent_first_arg,
   282	                        alternate_first_arg,
   283	                        ctx,
   284	                    );
   285	                    args[0] = Argument::from(cond_expr);
   286	                    return Some(ctx.ast.expression_call(expr.span, callee, NONE, args, false));
   287	                }
   288	            }
   289	        }
   290	
   291	        // Not part of esbuild
   292	        if let Some(e) = Self::try_merge_conditional_expression_inside(expr, ctx) {
   293	            return Some(e);
   294	        }
   295	
   296	        // Try using the "??" or "?." operators
   297	        if (ctx.supports_feature(ESFeature::ES2020NullishCoalescingOperator)
   298	            || ctx.supports_feature(ESFeature::ES2020OptionalChaining))
   299	            && let Expression::BinaryExpression(test_binary) = &mut expr.test
   300	            && let Some(is_negate) = match test_binary.operator {
   301	                BinaryOperator::Inequality => Some(true),
   302	                BinaryOperator::Equality => Some(false),
   303	                _ => None,
   304	            }
   305	        {
   306	            // a == null / a != null / (a = foo) == null / (a = foo) != null
   307	            let value_expr_with_id_name = if test_binary.left.is_null() {
   308	                if let Some(id) = Self::extract_id_or_assign_to_id(&test_binary.right)
   309	                    .filter(|id| !ctx.is_global_reference(id))
   310	                {
   311	                    Some((id.name, &mut test_binary.right))
   312	                } else {
   313	                    None
   314	                }
   315	            } else if test_binary.right.is_null() {
   316	                if let Some(id) = Self::extract_id_or_assign_to_id(&test_binary.left)
   317	                    .filter(|id| !ctx.is_global_reference(id))
   318	                {
   319	                    Some((id.name, &mut test_binary.left))
   320	                } else {
   321	                    None
   322	                }
   323	            } else {
   324	                None
   325	            };
   326	            if let Some((target_id_name, value_expr)) = value_expr_with_id_name {
   327	                if ctx.supports_feature(ESFeature::ES2020NullishCoalescingOperator) {
   328	                    // `a == null ? b : a` -> `a ?? b`
   329	                    // `a != null ? a : b` -> `a ?? b`
   330	                    // `(a = foo) == null ? b : a` -> `(a = foo) ?? b`
   331	                    // `(a = foo) != null ? a : b` -> `(a = foo) ?? b`
   332	                    let maybe_same_id_expr =
   333	                        if is_negate { &mut expr.consequent } else { &mut expr.alternate };
   334	                    if maybe_same_id_expr.is_specific_id(&target_id_name) {
   335	                        return Some(ctx.ast.expression_logical(
   336	                            expr.span,
   337	                            value_expr.take_in(ctx.ast),
   338	                            LogicalOperator::Coalesce,
   339	                            if is_negate {
   340	                                expr.alternate.take_in(ctx.ast)
   341	                            } else {
   342	                                expr.consequent.take_in(ctx.ast)
   343	                            },
   344	                        ));
   345	                    }
   346	                }
   347	                if ctx.supports_feature(ESFeature::ES2020OptionalChaining) {
   348	                    // "a == null ? undefined : a.b.c[d](e)" => "a?.b.c[d](e)"
   349	                    // "a != null ? a.b.c[d](e) : undefined" => "a?.b.c[d](e)"
   350	                    // "(a = foo) == null ? undefined : a.b.c[d](e)" => "(a = foo)?.b.c[d](e)"
   351	                    // "(a = foo) != null ? a.b.c[d](e) : undefined" => "(a = foo)?.b.c[d](e)"
   352	                    let maybe_undefined_expr =
   353	                        if is_negate { &expr.alternate } else { &expr.consequent };
   354	                    if ctx.is_expression_undefined(maybe_undefined_expr) {

     1	use crate::generated::ancestor::Ancestor;
     2	use oxc_ast::ast::*;
     3	use oxc_ecmascript::constant_evaluation::{ConstantEvaluation, ConstantValue};
     4	use oxc_span::GetSpan;

     6	use crate::TraverseCtx;

    10	impl<'a> PeepholeOptimizations {
    11	    pub fn init_symbol_value(decl: &VariableDeclarator<'a>, ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>) {
    12	        let BindingPattern::BindingIdentifier(ident) = &decl.id else { return };
    13	        let Some(symbol_id) = ident.symbol_id.get() else { return };
    14	        let value = if decl.kind.is_var() || Self::is_for_statement_init(ctx) {
    15	            // - Skip constant value inlining for `var` declarations, due to TDZ problems.
    16	            // - Set None for for statement initializers as the value of these are set by the for statement.
    17	            None
    18	        } else {
    19	            decl.init.as_ref().map_or(Some(ConstantValue::Undefined), |e| e.evaluate_value(ctx))
    20	        };
    21	        let is_fresh_value = decl.init.as_ref().is_some_and(Self::is_fresh_value_expression);
    22	        ctx.init_value(symbol_id, value, is_fresh_value);
    23	    }
    24	
    25	    /// Check if an expression creates a fresh value that cannot alias another binding
    26	    /// and has no setters/getters that could trigger side effects on property writes.
    27	    fn is_fresh_value_expression(expr: &Expression<'a>) -> bool {
    28	        match expr {
    29	            Expression::ArrayExpression(_)
    30	            | Expression::ArrowFunctionExpression(_)
    31	            | Expression::FunctionExpression(_) => true,
    32	            Expression::ObjectExpression(obj) => {
    33	                // Object literals with setter/getter properties are not safe to treat as fresh.
    34	                // Setters trigger side effects on property writes.
    35	                // Getter-only properties throw TypeError in strict mode on write.
    36	                // Also check property values for nested setters/getters.
    37	                !obj.properties.iter().any(|prop| {
    38	                    matches!(
    39	                        prop,
    40	                        ObjectPropertyKind::ObjectProperty(p)
    41	                            if matches!(p.kind, PropertyKind::Set | PropertyKind::Get)
    42	                                || Self::expression_has_setter_or_getter(&p.value)
    43	                                // `{ __proto__: ... }` sets the prototype chain and could
    44	                                // install setters that make property writes side-effectful.
    45	                                || (p.kind == PropertyKind::Init
    46	                                    && !p.computed
    47	                                    && p.key.is_specific_static_name("__proto__"))
    48	                    )
    49	                })
    50	            }
    51	            Expression::ClassExpression(class) => {
    52	                !Self::class_may_have_property_side_effects(class)
    53	            }
    54	            _ => false,
    55	        }
    56	    }
    57	
    58	    /// Check if a class may have side effects on property writes.
    59	    /// Returns `true` if the class has static setters, static accessor properties,
    60	    /// static property definitions with values, or an `extends` clause.
    61	    /// Following SWC's approach: any class with static property definitions
    62	    /// is not considered fresh, because the static initializer runs during
    63	    /// class creation and defines the property via `[[DefineOwnProperty]]`.
    64	    fn class_may_have_property_side_effects(class: &Class<'a>) -> bool {
    65	        // Classes with `extends` may inherit static setters from the parent.
    66	        // We can't statically determine the parent's static setters,
    67	        // so conservatively mark as non-fresh.
    68	        if class.super_class.is_some() {
    69	            return true;
    70	        }
    71	        class.body.body.iter().any(|element| match element {
    72	            ClassElement::MethodDefinition(method) => {
    73	                method.r#static && method.kind == MethodDefinitionKind::Set
    74	            }
    75	            // `static accessor foo` auto-generates a getter+setter pair
    76	            ClassElement::AccessorProperty(prop) => prop.r#static,
    77	            // Any static property definition with a value prevents fresh marking.
    78	            // The value is evaluated during class creation and could interact with
    79	            // property writes in unexpected ways (e.g. nested setters, proxies).
    80	            ClassElement::PropertyDefinition(prop) => prop.r#static && prop.value.is_some(),
    81	            _ => false,
    82	        })
    83	    }
    84	
    85	    /// Check if an expression contains setter or getter definitions (recursively).
    86	    fn expression_has_setter_or_getter(expr: &Expression<'a>) -> bool {
    87	        match expr {
    88	            Expression::ObjectExpression(obj) => obj.properties.iter().any(|prop| {
    89	                matches!(
    90	                    prop,
    91	                    ObjectPropertyKind::ObjectProperty(p)
    92	                        if matches!(p.kind, PropertyKind::Set | PropertyKind::Get)
    93	                            || Self::expression_has_setter_or_getter(&p.value)
    94	                )
    95	            }),
    96	            Expression::ClassExpression(class) => Self::class_may_have_property_side_effects(class),
    97	            _ => false,
    98	        }
    99	    }
   100	
   101	    /// Initialize symbol value for function declarations.
   102	    /// Function declarations always create fresh values (cannot alias another binding).
   103	    pub fn init_function_declaration_symbol_value(
   104	        id: Option<&BindingIdentifier<'a>>,
   105	        ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>,
   106	    ) {
   107	        let Some(id) = id else { return };
   108	        let Some(symbol_id) = id.symbol_id.get() else { return };
   109	        ctx.init_value(symbol_id, None, true);
   110	    }
   111	
   112	    /// Initialize symbol value for class declarations.
   113	    /// Class declarations create fresh values, but classes with static setters
   114	    /// are not considered fresh because property writes trigger setter side effects.
   115	    pub fn init_class_declaration_symbol_value(class: &Class<'a>, ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>) {
   116	        let Some(id) = &class.id else { return };
   117	        let Some(symbol_id) = id.symbol_id.get() else { return };
   118	        let is_fresh = !Self::class_may_have_property_side_effects(class);
   119	        ctx.init_value(symbol_id, None, is_fresh);
   120	    }
   121	
   122	    fn is_for_statement_init(ctx: &TraverseCtx<'a>) -> bool {
   123	        ctx.ancestors().nth(1).is_some_and(Ancestor::is_parent_of_for_statement_left)
   124	    }
   125	
   126	    pub fn inline_identifier_reference(expr: &mut Expression<'a>, ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>) {
   127	        let Expression::Identifier(ident) = expr else { return };
   128	        let reference_id = ident.reference_id();
   129	        let Some(symbol_id) = ctx.scoping().get_reference(reference_id).symbol_id() else { return };
   130	        let Some(symbol_value) = ctx.state.symbol_values.get_symbol_value(symbol_id) else {
   131	            return;
   132	        };
   133	        // Skip if there are write references.
   134	        if symbol_value.write_references_count > 0 {
   135	            return;
   136	        }
   137	        let Some(cv) = &symbol_value.initialized_constant else { return };
   138	        if symbol_value.read_references_count == 1
   139	            || match cv {
   140	                ConstantValue::Number(n) => n.fract() == 0.0 && *n >= -99.0 && *n <= 999.0,
   141	                ConstantValue::BigInt(_) => false,
   142	                ConstantValue::String(s) => s.len() <= 3,
   143	                ConstantValue::Boolean(_) | ConstantValue::Undefined | ConstantValue::Null => true,
   144	            }
   145	        {
   146	            *expr = ctx.value_to_expr(expr.span(), cv.clone());
   147	            ctx.state.changed = true;
   148	        }
   149	    }
   150	}

For `let rafId;` (no initializer), the declared symbol gets `Some(ConstantValue::Undefined)` as its constant value. Then in `get_constant_value_for_reference_id`:

```rust
fn get_constant_value_for_reference_id(
    &self,
    reference_id: ReferenceId,
) -> Option<ConstantValue<'a>> {
    self.scoping()
        .get_reference(reference_id)
        .symbol_id()
        .and_then(|symbol_id| self.state.symbol_values.get_symbol_value(symbol_id))
        .filter(|sv| sv.write_references_count == 0)
        .and_then(|sv| sv.initialized_constant.as_ref())
        .cloned()
}
```

So `rafId` is initialized to `ConstantValue::Undefined` and has `write_references_count == 0` (at least initially, before the minifier sees the write).

Now in `IdentifierReference::evaluate_value_to` (mod.rs line 119-137):

```rust
impl<'a> ConstantEvaluation<'a> for IdentifierReference<'a> {
    fn evaluate_value_to(
        &self,
        ctx: &impl ConstantEvaluationCtx<'a>,
        _target_ty: Option<ValueType>,
    ) -> Option<ConstantValue<'a>> {
        match self.name.as_str() {
            "undefined" if ctx.is_global_reference(self) => Some(ConstantValue::Undefined),
            "NaN" if ctx.is_global_reference(self) => Some(ConstantValue::Number(f64::NAN)),
            "Infinity" if ctx.is_global_reference(self) => {
                Some(ConstantValue::Number(f64::INFINITY))
            }
            _ => self
                .reference_id
                .get()
                .and_then(|reference_id| ctx.get_constant_value_for_reference_id(reference_id)),
        }
    }
}
```

So `rafId` can be evaluated to `ConstantValue::Undefined`!

Now let's trace through `rafId == null`:
- `left` = `Identifier("rafId")` evaluates to `ConstantValue::Undefined`
- `right` = `NullLiteral` evaluates to `ConstantValue::Null`

In `abstract_equality_comparison`:
- `left_val = left.value_type(ctx)` → `Undefined` (since `rafId` evaluates to `ConstantValue::Undefined`)
- `right_val = right.value_type(ctx)` → `Null`
- Both are determined, not `Undetermined`
- `left != right` (Undefined != Null)
- Matches `(ValueType::Null, ValueType::Undefined) | (ValueType::Undefined, ValueType::Null)` → returns `Some(true)`

So `rafId == null` evaluates to `true`! And the if condition is folded, removing the guard entirely.

But wait, the `value_type` for `rafId` is determined from its constant value. The question is: is this correct? The problem is that for `let rafId;` (TypeScript: `let rafId: number | undefined`), the initial value is indeed `undefined`. But the variable can later be assigned a value. The minifier should NOT use the constant value if the variable has write references.

Let me check the condition in `get_constant_value_for_reference_id`:
```rust
.filter(|sv| sv.write_references_count == 0)
```

This filters out symbols that have write references. But `rafId` DOES have a write reference (inside the `if` block: `rafId = requestAnimationFrame(...)`). So why is `write_references_count == 0`?

Maybe the issue is that the write reference count is computed BEFORE the minification process starts, and the minification might change things. Or maybe the issue is that on the first pass, the write hasn't been processed yet.

Actually wait, let me re-read the code more carefully. The `init_symbol_value` is called during `exit_variable_declarator`, and `init_value` computes `write_references_count` by scanning the references. At that point, `rafId` has a write reference (the assignment inside the if), so `write_references_count` should be > 0.

But wait, maybe the issue is that `value_type` is computed from `evaluate_value`, not from `get_constant_value_for_reference_id`. Let me check how `value_type` works for identifiers:

Looking at `value_type.rs` line 87-96:
```rust
Expression::Identifier(ident) => {
    if ctx.is_global_reference(ident) {
        match ident.name.as_str() {
            "undefined" => ValueType::Undefined,
            "NaN" | "Infinity" => ValueType::Number,
            _ => ValueType::Undetermined,
        }
    } else {
        ValueType::Undetermined
    }
}
```

So `value_type` for `rafId` returns `Undetermined`. But `abstract_equality_comparison` calls `value_type` first:

```rust
let left = left_expr.value_type(ctx);
let right = right_expr.value_type(ctx);
if left != ValueType::Undetermined && right != ValueType::Undetermined {
    ...
}
return None;
```

Since `left` (rafId) is `Undetermined`, it returns `None`. So `abstract_equality_comparison` should return `None`.

But wait! Look at `eval_binary`:

```rust
pub fn eval_binary(&self, e: &BinaryExpression<'a>) -> Option<Expression<'a>> {
    if e.may_have_side_effects(self) {
        None
    } else {
        e.evaluate_value(self).map(|v| self.value_to_expr(e.span, v))
    }
}
```

This calls `e.evaluate_value(self)` which calls `evaluate_value_to(ctx, None)`. Let me check what happens for the `BinaryExpression` with `Equality` operator:

In `binary_operation_evaluate_value_to`:
```rust
BinaryOperator::Equality => {
    let value = abstract_equality_comparison(ctx, left, right)?;
    Some(ConstantValue::Boolean(value))
}
```

And `abstract_equality_comparison`:
- `left_val = left.value_type(ctx)` - for `rafId`, this is `ValueType::Undetermined`
- `right_val = right.value_type(ctx)` - for `null`, this is `ValueType::Null`
- Condition `left != ValueType::Undetermined && right != ValueType::Undetermined` is FALSE
- Returns `None`

So `evaluate_value` returns `None`. And `eval_binary` returns `None`. So this path is fine.

But what about `evaluate_value_to_boolean`? Let me check `BinaryExpression::evaluate_value_to`:

```rust
impl<'a> ConstantEvaluation<'a> for BinaryExpression<'a> {
    fn evaluate_value_to(
        &self,
        ctx: &impl ConstantEvaluationCtx<'a>,
        target_ty: Option<ValueType>,
    ) -> Option<ConstantValue<'a>> {
        // FIXME: skipped for now to avoid performance regression, can be removed
        if target_ty == Some(ValueType::Boolean) {
            return None;
        }
        binary_operation_evaluate_value_to(self.operator, &self.left, &self.right, ctx, target_ty)
    }
}
```

And `Expression::evaluate_value_to`:
```rust
fn evaluate_value_to(...) -> Option<ConstantValue<'a>> {
    let result = match target_ty {
        Some(ValueType::Boolean) => self.to_boolean(ctx).map(ConstantValue::Boolean),
        ...
    };
    if result.is_some() {
        return result;
    }
    match self {
        Expression::BinaryExpression(e) => e.evaluate_value_to(ctx, target_ty),
        ...
    }
}
```

So for `evaluate_value_to_boolean`:
1. First tries `self.to_boolean(ctx)` - `BinaryExpression` is not a literal type, so `to_boolean` returns `None`
2. Then matches `Expression::BinaryExpression(e) => e.evaluate_value_to(ctx, Some(Boolean))`
3. Which hits the FIXME and returns `None`

So `evaluate_value_to_boolean` should return `None`.

Let me now check `try_fold_if` again:

```rust
if let Some(boolean) = if_stmt.test.evaluate_value_to_boolean(ctx) {
```

This should fail. So the if condition should NOT be removable via `try_fold_if`.

But the bug is happening. Let me add some debug prints to understand what's actually happening.

Let me write a test script that uses the minifier directly and see what happens:

     1	//! # Oxc Minifier
     2	//!
     3	//! High-performance JavaScript/TypeScript minifier focused on maximum compression.
     4	//!
     5	//! ## Overview
     6	//!
     7	//! The Oxc minifier applies a comprehensive set of optimizations to JavaScript code
     8	//! to achieve the smallest possible output while maintaining correctness. It draws
     9	//! inspiration from industry-leading minifiers like Closure Compiler, Terser, esbuild,
    10	//! and SWC.
    11	//!
    12	//! ## Features
    13	//!
    14	//! - **Maximum Compression**: Fixed-point iteration ensures all optimization opportunities are found
    15	//! - **Comprehensive Optimizations**: 17+ transformation passes and growing
    16	//! - **100% Correct**: Extensive testing with test262, Babel, and TypeScript test suites
    17	//! - **Fast**: Efficient algorithms and arena allocation for performance
    18	//!
    19	//! ## Example
    20	//!
    21	//! ```rust
    22	//! use oxc_minifier::{Minifier, MinifierOptions};
    23	//! use oxc_allocator::Allocator;
    24	//! use oxc_parser::Parser;
    25	//! use oxc_span::SourceType;
    26	//!
    27	//! let allocator = Allocator::default();
    28	//! let source_text = "const x = 1 + 1; console.log(x);";
    29	//! let source_type = SourceType::mjs();
    30	//! let ret = Parser::new(&allocator, source_text, source_type).parse();
    31	//! let mut program = ret.program;
    32	//!
    33	//! let options = MinifierOptions::default();
    34	//! let minifier = Minifier::new(options);
    35	//! let result = minifier.minify(&allocator, &mut program);
    36	//! ```
    37	//!
    38	//! ## Architecture
    39	//!
    40	//! The minifier consists of:
    41	//! - **Compressor**: Orchestrates the optimization pipeline
    42	//! - **Peephole Optimizations**: Individual transformation passes
    43	//! - **Mangler**: Variable renaming for size reduction
    44	//!
    45	//! See the [crate documentation](https://github.com/oxc-project/oxc/tree/main/crates/oxc_minifier) for more details.
    46	
    47	mod compressor;
    48	pub(crate) mod generated;
    49	mod keep_var;
    50	mod minifier_traverse;
    51	mod options;
    52	mod peephole;
    53	mod state;

    60	use oxc_mangler::Mangler;

    62	use oxc_str::CompactStr;
    63	use oxc_syntax::class::ClassId;
    64	use rustc_hash::FxHashMap;
    65	
    66	pub use oxc_mangler::{MangleOptions, MangleOptionsKeepNames};
    67	
    68	pub(crate) use crate::generated::traverse::Traverse;
    69	#[doc(hidden)]
    70	pub(crate) use crate::traverse_context::MinifierTraverseCtx as TraverseCtx;
    71	pub(crate) use crate::traverse_context::ReusableMinifierTraverseCtx as ReusableTraverseCtx;
    72	pub use crate::{compressor::Compressor, options::*};
    73	
    74	#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
    75	pub struct MinifierOptions {
    76	    pub mangle: Option<MangleOptions>,
    77	    pub compress: Option<CompressOptions>,
    78	}
    79	
    80	impl Default for MinifierOptions {
    81	    fn default() -> Self {
    82	        Self { mangle: Some(MangleOptions::default()), compress: Some(CompressOptions::default()) }
    83	    }
    84	}
    85	
    86	pub struct MinifierReturn {
    87	    pub scoping: Option<Scoping>,
    88	
    89	    /// A vector where each element corresponds to a class in declaration order.
    90	    /// Each element is a mapping from original private member names to their mangled names.
    91	    pub class_private_mappings: Option<IndexVec<ClassId, FxHashMap<String, CompactStr>>>,
    92	
    93	    /// Total number of iterations ran. Useful for debugging performance issues.
    94	    pub iterations: u8,
    95	}
    96	
    97	pub struct Minifier {
    98	    options: MinifierOptions,
    99	}
   100	
   101	impl<'a> Minifier {
   102	    pub fn new(options: MinifierOptions) -> Self {
   103	        Self { options }
   104	    }
   105	
   106	    pub fn minify(self, allocator: &'a Allocator, program: &mut Program<'a>) -> MinifierReturn {
   107	        self.build(false, allocator, program)
   108	    }
   109	
   110	    pub fn dce(self, allocator: &'a Allocator, program: &mut Program<'a>) -> MinifierReturn {
   111	        self.build(true, allocator, program)
   112	    }
   113	
   114	    fn build(
   115	        self,
   116	        dce: bool,
   117	        allocator: &'a Allocator,
   118	        program: &mut Program<'a>,
   119	    ) -> MinifierReturn {
   120	        let (stats, iterations) = self
   121	            .options
   122	            .compress
   123	            .map(|options| {
   124	                let semantic = SemanticBuilder::new().build(program).semantic;
   125	                let stats = semantic.stats();
   126	                let scoping = semantic.into_scoping();
   127	                let compressor = Compressor::new(allocator);
   128	                let iterations = if dce {
   129	                    let options = CompressOptions {
   130	                        target: options.target,
   131	                        treeshake: options.treeshake,
   132	                        ..CompressOptions::dce()
   133	                    };
   134	                    compressor.dead_code_elimination_with_scoping(program, scoping, options)
   135	                } else {
   136	                    compressor.build_with_scoping(program, scoping, options)
   137	                };
   138	                (stats, iterations)
   139	            })
   140	            .unwrap_or_default();
   141	        let (scoping, class_private_mappings) = self
   142	            .options
   143	            .mangle
   144	            .map(|options| {
   145	                let mut semantic = SemanticBuilder::new().with_stats(stats).build(program).semantic;
   146	                let class_private_mappings = Mangler::default()
   147	                    .with_options(options)
   148	                    .build_with_semantic(&mut semantic, program);
   149	                (semantic.into_scoping(), class_private_mappings)
   150	            })
   151	            .map_or((None, None), |(scoping, mappings)| (Some(scoping), Some(mappings)));
   152	        MinifierReturn { scoping, class_private_mappings, iterations }
   153	    }
   154	}
   155

2026-05-31 09:17:07,142 - INFO - swea-agent-oxc-project__oxc-21546 - 💭 THOUGHT
Now let me trace through the code more carefully. The issue is that `get_constant_value_for_reference_id` returns `ConstantValue::Undefined` for `rafId` because it was initialized without a value. Let me verify this by looking at how `init_value` is called.

In `inline.rs`:
```rust
pub fn init_symbol_value(decl: &VariableDeclarator<'a>, ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>) {
    let BindingPattern::BindingIdentifier(ident) = &decl.id else { return };
    let Some(symbol_id) = ident.symbol_id.get() else { return };
    let value = if decl.kind.is_var() || Self::is_for_statement_init(ctx) {
        None
    } else {
        decl.init.as_ref().map_or(Some(ConstantValue::Undefined), |e| e.evaluate_value(ctx))
    };
    let is_fresh_value = decl.init.as_ref().is_some_and(Self::is_fresh_value_expression);
    ctx.init_value(symbol_id, value, is_fresh_value);
}
```

For `let rafId;` (no init), `value = Some(ConstantValue::Undefined)`.

Then `ctx.init_value` sets this value in the symbol_values map.

In `get_constant_value_for_reference_id`:
```rust
fn get_constant_value_for_reference_id(
    &self,
    reference_id: ReferenceId,
) -> Option<ConstantValue<'a>> {
    self.scoping()
        .get_reference(reference_id)
        .symbol_id()
        .and_then(|symbol_id| self.state.symbol_values.get_symbol_value(symbol_id))
        .filter(|sv| sv.write_references_count == 0)
        .and_then(|sv| sv.initialized_constant.as_ref())
        .cloned()
}
```

There's a filter `.filter(|sv| sv.write_references_count == 0)`. Let me check if `write_references_count` is correctly computed.

Let me look at `init_value` in `ecma_context.rs`:

```rust
pub fn init_value(
    &mut self,
    symbol_id: SymbolId,
    constant: Option<ConstantValue<'a>>,
    is_fresh_value: bool,
) {
    ...
    let mut write_references_count = 0;
    for r in self.scoping().get_resolved_references(symbol_id) {
        if r.is_read() {
            read_references_count += 1;
        }
        if r.is_write() {
            write_references_count += 1;
        }
        ...
    }
    ...
    let symbol_value = SymbolValue {
        initialized_constant,
        ...
        write_references_count,
        ...
    };
    self.state.symbol_values.init_value(symbol_id, symbol_value);
}
```

So `write_references_count` counts write references at the time `init_value` is called. For `rafId`, there's a write reference inside the `if` block (`rafId = requestAnimationFrame(...)`), so it should be counted.

But wait - maybe the issue is that the minifier runs multiple passes, and on the first pass, the if statement hasn't been processed yet? No, `init_symbol_value` is called during `exit_variable_declarator`, which happens bottom-up. The variable declarator is visited, and then the write references are counted at that point.

Let me actually add some debug output to trace what's happening.

Actually, I think I should check if maybe the issue is in the `Normalize` step. Let me check the normalize step:

    11	use crate::{ReusableTraverseCtx, Traverse, TraverseCtx, minifier_traverse::traverse_mut_with_ctx};
    12	
    13	#[derive(Default)]
    14	pub struct NormalizeOptions {
    15	    pub convert_while_to_fors: bool,
    16	    pub convert_const_to_let: bool,
    17	    pub remove_unnecessary_use_strict: bool,
    18	}
    19	
    20	/// Normalize AST
    21	///
    22	/// Make subsequent AST passes easier to analyze:
    23	///
    24	/// * remove `Statement::EmptyStatement`
    25	/// * remove `ParenthesizedExpression`
    26	/// * convert whiles to fors
    27	/// * convert `const` to `let` for non-exported variables
    28	/// * convert `Infinity` to `f64::INFINITY`
    29	/// * convert `NaN` and `Number.NaN` to `f64::NaN`
    30	/// * convert `var x; void x` to `void 0`
    31	/// * convert `undefined` to `void 0`
    32	/// * apply `pure` to side-effect free global constructors (e.g. `new WeakMap()`)
    33	/// * remove unnecessary 'use strict' directive
    34	///
    35	/// Also
    36	///
    37	/// * remove `debugger` and `console.log` (optional)
    38	///
    39	/// <https://github.com/google/closure-compiler/blob/v20240609/src/com/google/javascript/jscomp/Normalize.java>
    40	pub struct Normalize {
    41	    options: NormalizeOptions,
    42	}
    43	
    44	impl<'a> Normalize {
    45	    pub fn build(&mut self, program: &mut Program<'a>, ctx: &mut ReusableTraverseCtx<'a>) {
    46	        traverse_mut_with_ctx(self, program, ctx);
    47	    }
    48	}
    49	
    50	impl<'a> Traverse<'a> for Normalize {
    51	    fn exit_program(&mut self, node: &mut Program<'a>, _ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>) {
    52	        if self.options.remove_unnecessary_use_strict && node.source_type.is_module() {
    53	            node.directives.drain_filter(|d| d.directive.as_str() == "use strict");
    54	        }
    55	    }
    56	
    57	    fn exit_statements(&mut self, stmts: &mut Vec<'a, Statement<'a>>, ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>) {
    58	        stmts.retain(|stmt| match stmt {
    59	            Statement::EmptyStatement(_) => false,
    60	            Statement::DebuggerStatement(_) if ctx.state.options.drop_debugger => false,
    61	            Statement::ExpressionStatement(expr) if ctx.state.options.drop_console => {
    62	                !Self::is_console_expression(&expr.expression)
    63	            }
    64	            _ => true,
    65	        });
    66	    }
    67	
    68	    fn exit_variable_declaration(
    69	        &mut self,
    70	        decl: &mut VariableDeclaration<'a>,
    71	        ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>,
    72	    ) {
    73	        if self.options.convert_const_to_let {
    74	            Self::convert_const_to_let(decl, ctx);
    75	        }
    76	    }
    77	
    78	    fn exit_statement(&mut self, stmt: &mut Statement<'a>, ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>) {
    79	        match stmt {
    80	            Statement::WhileStatement(_) if self.options.convert_while_to_fors => {
    81	                Self::convert_while_to_for(stmt, ctx);
    82	            }
    83	            _ => {}
    84	        }
    85	    }
    86	
    87	    fn exit_expression(&mut self, expr: &mut Expression<'a>, ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>) {
    88	        if let Expression::ParenthesizedExpression(paren_expr) = expr {
    89	            *expr = paren_expr.expression.take_in(ctx.ast);
    90	        }
    91	        if let Some(e) = match expr {
    92	            Expression::Identifier(ident) => Self::try_compress_identifier(ident, ctx),
    93	            Expression::UnaryExpression(e) if e.operator.is_void() => {
    94	                Self::fold_void_ident(e, ctx);
    95	                None
    96	            }
    97	            Expression::ArrowFunctionExpression(e) => {
    98	                Self::recover_arrow_expression_after_drop_console(e, ctx);
    99	                None
   100	            }
   101	            Expression::CallExpression(call_expr)
   102	                if ctx.state.options.drop_console
   103	                    && Self::is_console_call_expression(call_expr) =>
   104	            {
   105	                Some(ctx.ast.void_0(call_expr.span))
   106	            }
   107	            Expression::StaticMemberExpression(e) => Self::fold_number_nan_to_nan(e, ctx),
   108	            _ => None,
   109	        } {
   110	            *expr = e;
   111	        }
   112	    }
   113	
   114	    fn exit_call_expression(&mut self, e: &mut CallExpression<'a>, ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>) {
   115	        Self::set_no_side_effects_to_call_expr(e, ctx);
   116	    }
   117	
   118	    fn exit_new_expression(&mut self, e: &mut NewExpression<'a>, ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>) {
   119	        Self::set_pure_or_no_side_effects_to_new_expr(e, ctx);
   120	    }
   121	
   122	    fn exit_function_body(&mut self, body: &mut FunctionBody<'a>, ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>) {
   123	        if self.options.remove_unnecessary_use_strict {
   124	            Self::remove_unused_use_strict_directive(body, ctx);
   125	        }
   126	    }
   127	}
   128	
   129	impl<'a> Normalize {
   130	    pub fn new(options: NormalizeOptions) -> Self {
   131	        Self { options }
   132	    }
   133	
   134	    fn recover_arrow_expression_after_drop_console(
   135	        expr: &mut ArrowFunctionExpression<'a>,
   136	        ctx: &TraverseCtx<'a>,
   137	    ) {
   138	        if ctx.state.options.drop_console && expr.expression && expr.body.is_empty() {
   139	            expr.expression = false;
   140	        }
   141	    }
   142	
   143	    fn is_console_expression(expr: &Expression<'_>) -> bool {
   144	        matches!(expr, Expression::CallExpression(call_expr) if Self::is_console_call_expression(call_expr))
   145	    }
   146	
   147	    fn is_console_call_expression(call_expr: &CallExpression<'_>) -> bool {
   148	        let Some(member_expr) = call_expr.callee.as_member_expression() else { return false };
   149	        let obj = member_expr.object();
   150	        let Some(ident) = obj.get_identifier_reference() else { return false };
   151	        ident.name == "console"
   152	    }
   153	
   154	    fn convert_while_to_for(stmt: &mut Statement<'a>, ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>) {
   155	        let Statement::WhileStatement(while_stmt) = stmt.take_in(ctx.ast) else { return };
   156	        let while_stmt = while_stmt.unbox();
   157	        let for_stmt = ctx.ast.alloc_for_statement_with_scope_id(
   158	            while_stmt.span,
   159	            None,
   160	            Some(while_stmt.test),
   161	            None,
   162	            while_stmt.body,
   163	            ctx.create_child_scope_of_current(ScopeFlags::empty()),
   164	        );
   165	        *stmt = Statement::ForStatement(for_stmt);
   166	    }
   167	
   168	    fn convert_const_to_let(decl: &mut VariableDeclaration<'a>, ctx: &TraverseCtx<'a>) {
   169	        // checking whether the current scope is the root scope instead of
   170	        // checking whether any variables are exposed to outside (e.g. `export` in ESM)
   171	        if decl.kind.is_const()
   172	            && ctx.current_scope_id() != ctx.scoping().root_scope_id()
   173	            // direct eval may have a assignment inside
   174	            && !ctx.current_scope_flags().contains_direct_eval()
   175	        {
   176	            let all_declarations_are_only_read = decl.declarations.iter().all(|d| {
   177	                d.id.all_binding_identifiers(&mut |id| {
   178	                    ctx.scoping()
   179	                        .get_resolved_references(id.symbol_id())
   180	                        .all(|reference| reference.flags().is_read_only())
   181	                })
   182	            });
   183	            if all_declarations_are_only_read {
   184	                // mark all declarations as `let`
   185	                decl.kind = VariableDeclarationKind::Let;
   186	                for decl in &mut decl.declarations {
   187	                    decl.kind = VariableDeclarationKind::Let;
   188	                }
   189	            }
   190	        }
   191	    }
   192	
   193	    /// Transforms `undefined` => `void 0`, `Infinity` => `f64::Infinity`, `NaN` -> `f64::NaN`.
   194	    /// So subsequent passes don't need to look up whether these variables are shadowed or not.
   195	    fn try_compress_identifier(
   196	        ident: &IdentifierReference<'a>,
   197	        ctx: &TraverseCtx<'a>,
   198	    ) -> Option<Expression<'a>> {
   199	        match ident.name.as_str() {
   200	            "undefined" if ident.is_global_reference(ctx.scoping()) => {
   201	                // `delete undefined` returns `false`
   202	                // `delete void 0` returns `true`
   203	                if Self::is_unary_delete_ancestor(ctx.ancestors()) {
   204	                    return None;
   205	                }
   206	                Some(ctx.ast.void_0(ident.span))
   207	            }
   208	            "Infinity" if ident.is_global_reference(ctx.scoping()) => {
   209	                // `delete Infinity` returns `false`
   210	                // `delete 1/0` returns `true`
   211	                if Self::is_unary_delete_ancestor(ctx.ancestors()) {
   212	                    return None;
   213	                }
   214	                Some(ctx.ast.expression_numeric_literal(
   215	                    ident.span,
   216	                    f64::INFINITY,
   217	                    None,
   218	                    NumberBase::Decimal,
   219	                ))
   220	            }
   221	            "NaN" if ident.is_global_reference(ctx.scoping()) => {
   222	                // `delete NaN` returns `false`
   223	                // `delete 0/0` returns `true`
   224	                if Self::is_unary_delete_ancestor(ctx.ancestors()) {
   225	                    return None;
   226	                }
   227	                Some(ctx.ast.nan(ident.span))
   228	            }
   229	            _ => None,
   230	        }
   231	    }
   232	
   233	    fn is_unary_delete_ancestor<'t>(ancestors: impl Iterator<Item = Ancestor<'a, 't>>) -> bool {
   234	        for ancestor in ancestors {
   235	            match ancestor {
   236	                Ancestor::UnaryExpressionArgument(e) if e.operator().is_delete() => {
   237	                    return true;
   238	                }
   239	                Ancestor::ParenthesizedExpressionExpression(_)
   240	                | Ancestor::SequenceExpressionExpressions(_) => {}
   241	                _ => return false,
   242	            }
   243	        }
   244	        false
   245	    }
   246	
   247	    fn fold_void_ident(e: &mut UnaryExpression<'a>, ctx: &TraverseCtx<'a>) {
   248	        debug_assert!(e.operator.is_void());
   249	        let Expression::Identifier(ident) = &e.argument else { return };
   250	        if ident.is_global_reference(ctx.scoping()) {
   251	            return;
   252	        }
   253	        e.argument = ctx.ast.expression_numeric_literal(ident.span, 0.0, None, NumberBase::Decimal);
   254	    }
   255	
   256	    fn fold_number_nan_to_nan(
   257	        e: &StaticMemberExpression<'a>,
   258	        ctx: &TraverseCtx<'a>,
   259	    ) -> Option<Expression<'a>> {
   260	        let Expression::Identifier(ident) = &e.object else { return None };
   261	        if ident.name != "Number" {
   262	            return None;
   263	        }
   264	        if e.property.name != "NaN" {
   265	            return None;
   266	        }
   267	        if !ctx.is_global_reference(ident) {
   268	            return None;
   269	        }
   270	        Some(ctx.ast.nan(ident.span))
   271	    }
   272	
   273	    fn set_no_side_effects_to_call_expr(call_expr: &mut CallExpression<'a>, ctx: &TraverseCtx<'a>) {
   274	        if call_expr.pure {
   275	            return;
   276	        }
   277	        let Some(ident) = call_expr.callee.get_identifier_reference() else {
   278	            return;
   279	        };
   280	        if let Some(symbol_id) = ctx.scoping().get_reference(ident.reference_id()).symbol_id() {
   281	            // Apply `/* #__NO_SIDE_EFFECTS__ */`
   282	            if ctx.scoping().no_side_effects().contains(&symbol_id) {
   283	                call_expr.pure = true;
   284	            }
   285	        }
   286	    }
   287	
   288	    /// Set `pure` on side effect free `new Expr()`s.
   289	    /// `PC` or `PC_WITH_ARRAY` in <https://github.com/rollup/rollup/blob/v4.42.0/src/ast/nodes/shared/knownGlobals.ts>
   290	    fn set_pure_or_no_side_effects_to_new_expr(
   291	        new_expr: &mut NewExpression<'a>,
   292	        ctx: &TraverseCtx<'a>,
   293	    ) {
   294	        if new_expr.pure {
   295	            return;
   296	        }
   297	        let Some(ident) = new_expr.callee.get_identifier_reference() else {
   298	            return;
   299	        };
   300	        if let Some(symbol_id) = ctx.scoping().get_reference(ident.reference_id()).symbol_id() {
   301	            // Apply `/* #__NO_SIDE_EFFECTS__ */`
   302	            if ctx.scoping().no_side_effects().contains(&symbol_id) {
   303	                new_expr.pure = true;
   304	            }
   305	            return;
   306	        }
   307	        // callee is a global reference.
   308	        let len = new_expr.arguments.len();
   309	
   310	        let (zero_arg_throws_error, one_arg_array_throws_error, one_arg_throws_error): (
   311	            bool,
   312	            bool,
   313	            &'static [ValueType],
   314	        ) = match ident.name.as_str() {
   315	            "AggregateError" => (
   316	                true,
   317	                false,
   318	                &[
   319	                    ValueType::Undefined,
   320	                    ValueType::Null,
   321	                    ValueType::Number,
   322	                    ValueType::BigInt,
   323	                    ValueType::Boolean,
   324	                    ValueType::Object,
   325	                ],
   326	            ),
   327	            "DataView" => (
   328	                true,
   329	                true,
   330	                &[
   331	                    ValueType::Undefined,
   332	                    ValueType::Null,
   333	                    ValueType::Number,
   334	                    ValueType::Boolean,
   335	                    ValueType::BigInt,
   336	                    ValueType::String,
   337	                    ValueType::Boolean,
   338	                    ValueType::Object,
   339	                ],
   340	            ),
   341	            "Set" | "Map" | "WeakSet" | "WeakMap" => (
   342	                false,
   343	                false,
   344	                &[
   345	                    ValueType::Number,
   346	                    ValueType::Boolean,
   347	                    ValueType::BigInt,
   348	                    ValueType::Boolean,
   349	                    ValueType::Object,
   350	                ],
   351	            ),
   352	            "ArrayBuffer" | "Date" => (false, false, &[ValueType::BigInt]),
   353	            "Boolean" | "Error" | "EvalError" | "RangeError" | "ReferenceError" | "SyntaxError"
   354	            | "TypeError" | "URIError" | "Number" | "Object" | "String" => (false, false, &[]),
   355	            // RegExp needs special validation using the regex parser
   356	            "RegExp" => {
   357	                if Self::can_set_pure(ident, ctx) && is_valid_regexp(&new_expr.arguments) {
   358	                    new_expr.pure = true;
   359	                }
   360	                return;
   361	            }
   362	            _ => return,
   363	        };
   364	
   365	        if !Self::can_set_pure(ident, ctx) {
   366	            return;
   367	        }
   368	
   369	        if match len {
   370	            0 if !zero_arg_throws_error => true,
   371	            1 => match new_expr.arguments[0].as_expression() {
   372	                Some(Expression::ArrayExpression(array_expr)) => {
   373	                    array_expr.elements.is_empty() && !one_arg_array_throws_error
   374	                }
   375	                Some(e) => {
   376	                    if let Expression::NewExpression(new_expr) = e {
   377	                        new_expr.pure
   378	                    } else {
   379	                        let value_type = e.value_type(&ctx);
   380	                        if value_type.is_undetermined() {
   381	                            false
   382	                        } else {
   383	                            !one_arg_throws_error.contains(&value_type)
   384	                        }
   385	                    }
   386	                }
   387	                _ => false,
   388	            },
   389	            _ => false,
   390	        } {
   391	            new_expr.pure = true;
   392	        }
   393	    }
   394	
   395	    fn can_set_pure(ident: &IdentifierReference<'a>, ctx: &TraverseCtx<'a>) -> bool {
   396	        ctx.is_global_reference(ident)
   397	            // Throw is never pure.
   398	            && !matches!(ctx.parent(), Ancestor::ThrowStatementArgument(_))
   399	    }
   400	
   401	    fn remove_unused_use_strict_directive(body: &mut FunctionBody<'a>, ctx: &TraverseCtx<'a>) {
   402	        if !body.directives.is_empty()
   403	            && ctx
   404	                .scoping()
   405	                .scope_parent_id(ctx.current_scope_id())
   406	                .map(|scope_id| ctx.scoping().scope_flags(scope_id))
   407	                .is_some_and(ScopeFlags::is_strict_mode)
   408	        {
   409	            body.directives.drain_filter(|d| d.directive.as_str() == "use strict");
   410	        }
   411	    }
   412	}

    16	impl<'a> PeepholeOptimizations {
    17	    /// `SimplifyUnusedExpr`: <https://github.com/evanw/esbuild/blob/v0.24.2/internal/js_ast/js_ast_helpers.go#L534>
    18	    pub fn remove_unused_expression(e: &mut Expression<'a>, ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>) -> bool {
    19	        match e {
    20	            Expression::ArrayExpression(_) => Self::remove_unused_array_expr(e, ctx),
    21	            Expression::AssignmentExpression(_) => Self::remove_unused_assignment_expr(e, ctx),
    22	            Expression::BinaryExpression(_) => Self::remove_unused_binary_expr(e, ctx),
    23	            Expression::CallExpression(_) => Self::remove_unused_call_expr(e, ctx),
    24	            Expression::ClassExpression(_) => Self::remove_unused_class_expr(e, ctx),
    25	            Expression::ConditionalExpression(_) => Self::remove_unused_conditional_expr(e, ctx),
    26	            Expression::LogicalExpression(_) => Self::remove_unused_logical_expr(e, ctx),
    27	            Expression::NewExpression(_) => Self::remove_unused_new_expr(e, ctx),
    28	            Expression::ObjectExpression(_) => Self::remove_unused_object_expr(e, ctx),
    29	            Expression::SequenceExpression(_) => Self::remove_unused_sequence_expr(e, ctx),
    30	            Expression::TemplateLiteral(_) => Self::remove_unused_template_literal(e, ctx),
    31	            Expression::UnaryExpression(_) => Self::remove_unused_unary_expr(e, ctx),
    32	            _ => !e.may_have_side_effects(ctx),
    33	        }
    34	    }
    35	
    36	    fn remove_unused_unary_expr(e: &mut Expression<'a>, ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>) -> bool {
    37	        let Expression::UnaryExpression(unary_expr) = e else { return false };
    38	        match unary_expr.operator {
    39	            UnaryOperator::Void | UnaryOperator::LogicalNot => {
    40	                *e = unary_expr.argument.take_in(ctx.ast);
    41	                ctx.state.changed = true;
    42	                Self::remove_unused_expression(e, ctx)
    43	            }
    44	            UnaryOperator::Typeof => {
    45	                if unary_expr.argument.is_identifier_reference() {
    46	                    true
    47	                } else {
    48	                    *e = unary_expr.argument.take_in(ctx.ast);
    49	                    ctx.state.changed = true;
    50	                    Self::remove_unused_expression(e, ctx)
    51	                }
    52	            }
    53	            _ => !e.may_have_side_effects(ctx),
    54	        }
    55	    }
    56	
    57	    fn remove_unused_sequence_expr(e: &mut Expression<'a>, ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>) -> bool {
    58	        let Expression::SequenceExpression(sequence_expr) = e else { return false };
    59	        let old_len = sequence_expr.expressions.len();
    60	        sequence_expr.expressions.retain_mut(|e| !Self::remove_unused_expression(e, ctx));
    61	        if sequence_expr.expressions.len() != old_len {
    62	            ctx.state.changed = true;
    63	        }
    64	        sequence_expr.expressions.is_empty()
    65	    }
    66	
    67	    fn remove_unused_logical_expr(e: &mut Expression<'a>, ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>) -> bool {
    68	        if !e.may_have_side_effects(ctx) {
    69	            return true;
    70	        }
    71	        let Expression::LogicalExpression(logical_expr) = e else { return false };
    72	        if !logical_expr.operator.is_coalesce() {
    73	            Self::minimize_expression_in_boolean_context(&mut logical_expr.left, ctx);
    74	        }
    75	        if Self::remove_unused_expression(&mut logical_expr.right, ctx) {
    76	            Self::remove_unused_expression(&mut logical_expr.left, ctx);
    77	            *e = logical_expr.left.take_in(ctx.ast);
    78	            ctx.state.changed = true;
    79	            return false;
    80	        }
    81	
    82	        // try optional chaining and nullish coalescing
    83	        if ctx.supports_feature(ESFeature::ES2020OptionalChaining)
    84	            || ctx.supports_feature(ESFeature::ES2020NullishCoalescingOperator)
    85	        {
    86	            let LogicalExpression {
    87	                span: logical_span,
    88	                left: logical_left,
    89	                right: logical_right,
    90	                operator: logical_op,
    91	                ..
    92	            } = logical_expr.as_mut();
    93	            if let Expression::BinaryExpression(binary_expr) = logical_left {
    94	                match (logical_op, binary_expr.operator) {
    95	                    // "a != null && a.b()" => "a?.b()"
    96	                    // "a == null || a.b()" => "a?.b()"
    97	                    (LogicalOperator::And, BinaryOperator::Inequality)
    98	                    | (LogicalOperator::Or, BinaryOperator::Equality)
    99	                        if ctx.supports_feature(ESFeature::ES2020OptionalChaining) =>
   100	                    {
   101	                        let name_and_id = if let Expression::Identifier(id) = &binary_expr.left {
   102	                            (!ctx.is_global_reference(id) && binary_expr.right.is_null())
   103	                                .then_some((id.name, &mut binary_expr.left))
   104	                        } else if let Expression::Identifier(id) = &binary_expr.right {
   105	                            (!ctx.is_global_reference(id) && binary_expr.left.is_null())
   106	                                .then_some((id.name, &mut binary_expr.right))
   107	                        } else {
   108	                            None
   109	                        };
   110	                        if let Some((name, id)) = name_and_id
   111	                            && Self::inject_optional_chaining_if_matched(
   112	                                &name,
   113	                                id,
   114	                                logical_right,
   115	                                ctx,
   116	                            )
   117	                        {
   118	                            *e = logical_right.take_in(ctx.ast);
   119	                            ctx.state.changed = true;
   120	                            return false;
   121	                        }
   122	                    }
   123	                    // "a == null && b" => "a ?? b"
   124	                    // "a != null || b" => "a ?? b"
   125	                    // "a == null && (a = b)" => "a ??= b"
   126	                    // "a != null || (a = b)" => "a ??= b"
   127	                    (LogicalOperator::And, BinaryOperator::Equality)
   128	                    | (LogicalOperator::Or, BinaryOperator::Inequality)
   129	                        if ctx.supports_feature(ESFeature::ES2020NullishCoalescingOperator) =>
   130	                    {
   131	                        let new_left_hand_expr = if binary_expr.right.is_null() {
   132	                            Some(&mut binary_expr.left)
   133	                        } else if binary_expr.left.is_null() {
   134	                            Some(&mut binary_expr.right)
   135	                        } else {
   136	                            None
   137	                        };
   138	                        if let Some(new_left_hand_expr) = new_left_hand_expr {
   139	                            if ctx.supports_feature(ESFeature::ES2021LogicalAssignmentOperators)
   140	                                    && let Expression::AssignmentExpression(assignment_expr) =
   141	                                        logical_right
   142	                                    && assignment_expr.operator == AssignmentOperator::Assign
   143	                                    && Self::has_no_side_effect_for_evaluation_same_target(
   144	                                        &assignment_expr.left,
   145	                                        new_left_hand_expr,
   146	                                        ctx,
   147	                                    )
   148	                                    // Don't transform `x.y != null || (x = {}, x.y = 3)` to `x.y ??= (x = {}, 3)` because
   149	                                    // `??=` evaluates `x.y` (capturing `x`) before the RHS reassigns `x`.
   150	                                    // https://github.com/oxc-project/oxc/pull/16802#discussion_r2619369597
   151	                                    && !Self::member_object_may_be_mutated(&assignment_expr.left, ctx)
   152	                            {
   153	                                assignment_expr.span = *logical_span;
   154	                                assignment_expr.operator = AssignmentOperator::LogicalNullish;
   155	                                *e = logical_right.take_in(ctx.ast);
   156	                                ctx.state.changed = true;
   157	                                return false;
   158	                            }
   159	
   160	                            *e = ctx.ast.expression_logical(
   161	                                *logical_span,
   162	                                new_left_hand_expr.take_in(ctx.ast),
   163	                                LogicalOperator::Coalesce,
   164	                                logical_right.take_in(ctx.ast),
   165	                            );
   166	                            ctx.state.changed = true;
   167	                            return false;
   168	                        }
   169	                    }
   170	                    _ => {}
   171	                }
   172	            }
   173	        }
   174	
   175	        false
   176	    }
   177	
   178	    // `([1,2,3, foo()])` -> `foo()`
   179	    fn remove_unused_array_expr(e: &mut Expression<'a>, ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>) -> bool {
   180	        let Expression::ArrayExpression(array_expr) = e else {
   181	            return false;
   182	        };
   183	        if array_expr.elements.is_empty() {
   184	            return true;
   185	        }
   186	
   187	        let old_len = array_expr.elements.len();
   188	        array_expr.elements.retain_mut(|el| match el {
   189	            ArrayExpressionElement::SpreadElement(_) => el.may_have_side_effects(ctx),
   190	            ArrayExpressionElement::Elision(_) => false,
   191	            match_expression!(ArrayExpressionElement) => {
   192	                let el_expr = el.to_expression_mut();
   193	                !Self::remove_unused_expression(el_expr, ctx)
   194	            }
   195	        });
   196	        if array_expr.elements.len() != old_len {
   197	            ctx.state.changed = true;
   198	        }
   199	
   200	        if array_expr.elements.is_empty() {
   201	            return true;
   202	        }
   203	
   204	        // try removing the brackets "[]", if the array does not contain spread elements
   205	        // `a(), b()` is shorter than `[a(), b()]`,
   206	        // but `[...a], [...b]` is not shorter than `[...a, ...b]`
   207	        let keep_as_array = array_expr
   208	            .elements
   209	            .iter()
   210	            .any(|el| matches!(el, ArrayExpressionElement::SpreadElement(_)));
   211	        if keep_as_array {
   212	            return false;
   213	        }
   214	
   215	        let mut expressions = ctx.ast.vec_from_iter(
   216	            array_expr.elements.drain(..).map(ArrayExpressionElement::into_expression),
   217	        );
   218	        if expressions.is_empty() {
   219	            return true;
   220	        } else if expressions.len() == 1 {
   221	            *e = expressions.pop().unwrap();
   222	            return false;
   223	        }
   224	
   225	        *e = ctx.ast.expression_sequence(array_expr.span, expressions);
   226	        false
   227	    }
   228	
   229	    fn remove_unused_new_expr(e: &mut Expression<'a>, ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>) -> bool {
   230	        let Expression::NewExpression(new_expr) = e else { return false };
   231	        if (new_expr.pure && ctx.annotations()) || ctx.manual_pure_functions(&new_expr.callee) {
   232	            let mut exprs =
   233	                Self::fold_arguments_into_needed_expressions(&mut new_expr.arguments, ctx);
   234	            if exprs.is_empty() {
   235	                return true;
   236	            } else if exprs.len() == 1 {
   237	                *e = exprs.pop().unwrap();
   238	                ctx.state.changed = true;
   239	                return false;
   240	            }
   241	            *e = ctx.ast.expression_sequence(new_expr.span, exprs);
   242	            ctx.state.changed = true;
   243	            return false;
   244	        }
   245	        false
   246	    }
   247	
   248	    // "`${1}2${foo()}3`" -> "`${foo()}`"
   249	    fn remove_unused_template_literal(e: &mut Expression<'a>, ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>) -> bool {
   250	        let Expression::TemplateLiteral(temp_lit) = e else { return false };
   251	        if temp_lit.expressions.is_empty() {
   252	            return true;
   253	        }
   254	        if temp_lit.expressions.iter().all(|e| e.to_primitive(ctx).is_symbol() != Some(false))
   255	            && temp_lit.quasis.iter().all(|q| q.value.raw.is_empty())
   256	        {
   257	            return false;
   258	        }
   259	
   260	        let mut transformed_elements = ctx.ast.vec();
   261	        let mut pending_to_string_required_exprs = ctx.ast.vec();
   262	
   263	        for mut e in temp_lit.expressions.drain(..) {
   264	            if e.to_primitive(ctx).is_symbol() != Some(false) {
   265	                pending_to_string_required_exprs.push(e);
   266	            } else if !Self::remove_unused_expression(&mut e, ctx) {
   267	                if !pending_to_string_required_exprs.is_empty() {
   268	                    // flush pending to string required expressions
   269	                    let expressions =
   270	                        ctx.ast.vec_from_iter(pending_to_string_required_exprs.drain(..));
   271	                    let mut quasis = ctx.ast.vec_from_iter(
   272	                        iter::repeat_with(|| {
   273	                            ctx.ast.template_element(
   274	                                e.span(),
   275	                                TemplateElementValue { raw: "".into(), cooked: Some("".into()) },
   276	                                false,
   277	                                false,
   278	                            )
   279	                        })
   280	                        .take(expressions.len() + 1),
   281	                    );
   282	                    quasis
   283	                        .last_mut()
   284	                        .expect("template literal must have at least one quasi")
   285	                        .tail = true;
   286	                    transformed_elements.push(ctx.ast.expression_template_literal(
   287	                        e.span(),
   288	                        quasis,
   289	                        expressions,
   290	                    ));
   291	                }
   292	                transformed_elements.push(e);
   293	            }
   294	        }
   295	
   296	        if !pending_to_string_required_exprs.is_empty() {
   297	            let expressions = ctx.ast.vec_from_iter(pending_to_string_required_exprs.drain(..));
   298	            let mut quasis = ctx.ast.vec_from_iter(
   299	                iter::repeat_with(|| {
   300	                    ctx.ast.template_element(
   301	                        temp_lit.span,
   302	                        TemplateElementValue { raw: "".into(), cooked: Some("".into()) },
   303	                        false,
   304	                        false,
   305	                    )
   306	                })
   307	                .take(expressions.len() + 1),
   308	            );
   309	            quasis.last_mut().expect("template literal must have at least one quasi").tail = true;
   310	            transformed_elements.push(ctx.ast.expression_template_literal(
   311	                temp_lit.span,
   312	                quasis,
   313	                expressions,
   314	            ));
   315	        }
   316	
   317	        if transformed_elements.is_empty() {
   318	            return true;
   319	        } else if transformed_elements.len() == 1 {
   320	            *e = transformed_elements.pop().unwrap();
   321	            ctx.state.changed = true;
   322	            return false;
   323	        }
   324	
   325	        *e = ctx.ast.expression_sequence(temp_lit.span, transformed_elements);
   326	        ctx.state.changed = true;
   327	        false
   328	    }
   329	
   330	    // `({ 1: 1, [foo()]: bar() })` -> `foo(), bar()`
   331	    fn remove_unused_object_expr(e: &mut Expression<'a>, ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>) -> bool {
   332	        let Expression::ObjectExpression(object_expr) = e else {
   333	            return false;
   334	        };
   335	        if object_expr.properties.is_empty() {
   336	            return true;
   337	        }
   338	        if object_expr.properties.iter().all(ObjectPropertyKind::is_spread) {
   339	            // All-spread objects like `({...x})` can only be removed if
   340	            // the spread arguments themselves have no side effects.
   341	            return !object_expr
   342	                .properties
   343	                .iter()
   344	                .any(|property| property.may_have_side_effects(ctx));
   345	        }
   346	
   347	        let mut transformed_elements = ctx.ast.vec();
   348	        let mut pending_spread_elements = ctx.ast.vec();
   349	
   350	        for prop in object_expr.properties.drain(..) {
   351	            match prop {
   352	                ObjectPropertyKind::SpreadProperty(_) => {
   353	                    pending_spread_elements.push(prop);
   354	                }
   355	                ObjectPropertyKind::ObjectProperty(prop) => {
   356	                    if !pending_spread_elements.is_empty() {
