2026-05-31 09:17:27,819 - DEBUG - swea-lm-oxc-project__oxc-21546 - total_tokens_sent=1,471,447, total_tokens_received=5,583, total_cost=0.00, total_api_calls=41
2026-05-31 09:17:27,821 - INFO - swea-agent-oxc-project__oxc-21546 - 💭 THOUGHT
Let me look at line 95-121 more carefully. This is about transforming `a != null && a.b()` to `a?.b()`. This doesn't seem to be the direct cause.

Let me think about this more systematically. The issue is that `if (rafId == null)` is being removed. This means either:

1. `try_fold_if` successfully evaluates `rafId == null` to a boolean (true/false), or
2. Some other optimization removes the if guard.

Let me add some debug output to trace what's happening.

Actually, let me look more carefully at the `evaluate_value` path for `Identifier`. In `IdentifierReference::evaluate_value_to`:

```rust
_ => self
    .reference_id
    .get()
    .and_then(|reference_id| ctx.get_constant_value_for_reference_id(reference_id)),
```

And `get_constant_value_for_reference_id`:
```rust
fn get_constant_value_for_reference_id(
    &self,
    reference_id: ReferenceId,
) -> Option<ConstantValue<'a>> {
    self.scoping()
        .get_reference(reference_id)
        .symbol_id()
        .and_then(|symbol_id| self.state.symbol_values.get_symbol_value(symbol_id))
        .filter(|sv| sv.write_references_count == 0)
        .and_then(|sv| sv.initialized_constant.as_ref())
        .cloned()
}
```

The `filter(|sv| sv.write_references_count == 0)` should filter out `rafId` because it has a write reference. But wait - what if the write reference count is 0 because the write reference hasn't been counted? Let me check the `init_value` function again:

```rust
for r in self.scoping().get_resolved_references(symbol_id) {
    if r.is_read() {
        read_references_count += 1;
    }
    if r.is_write() {
        write_references_count += 1;
    }
    ...
}
```

This counts write references from `self.scoping().get_resolved_references(symbol_id)`. The scoping is built from the original AST before any minification. So the write reference to `rafId` inside the if block should be counted.

Unless... the write reference is not marked as write. Let me check how references are classified.

Actually, let me just add some debug output to the minifier example and trace the actual values:

use std::path::Path;

use oxc_allocator::Allocator;
use oxc_codegen::{Codegen, CodegenOptions, CommentOptions};
use oxc_minifier::{CompressOptions, Minifier, MinifierOptions};
use oxc_parser::Parser;
use oxc_span::SourceType;

fn main() -> std::io::Result<()> {
    let source_text = r#"let rafId;

function foo() {
  if (rafId == null) {
    rafId = requestAnimationFrame(() => {
      console.log('callback');
    });
  }
}
"#;
    let source_type = SourceType::mjs();
    
    let allocator = Allocator::default();
    let ret = Parser::new(&allocator, source_text, source_type).parse();
    let mut program = ret.program;
    
    let options = MinifierOptions {
        mangle: None,
        compress: Some(CompressOptions { max_iterations: Some(1), ..CompressOptions::smallest() }),
    };
    let ret = Minifier::new(options).minify(&allocator, &mut program);
    
    let code = Codegen::new()
        .with_options(CodegenOptions {
            minify: true,
            comments: CommentOptions::disabled(),
            ..CodegenOptions::default()
        })
        .with_scoping(ret.scoping)
        .build(&program);
    
    println!("{}", code.code);
    println!("Iterations: {}", ret.iterations);
    Ok(())
}
EOF

   106	    test(
   107	        "try {} catch (e) { var foo; bar() } finally { baz() }",
   108	        "try {} catch { var foo } finally { baz() }",
   109	    );
   110	    test("try {} catch (e) { foo() }", "");
   111	    test("try {} catch (e) { foo() } finally {}", "");
   112	    test("try {} finally { foo() }", "foo()");
   113	    test("try {} catch (e) { foo() } finally { bar() }", "bar()");
   114	    test("try {} finally { var x = foo() }", "var x = foo()");
   115	    test("try {} catch (e) { foo() } finally { var x = bar() }", "var x = bar()");
   116	    test("try {} finally { let x = foo() }", "{ let x = foo() }");
   117	    test("try {} catch (e) { foo() } finally { let x = bar() }", "{ let x = bar();}");
   118	    test("try {} catch (e) { } finally {}", "");
   119	    test("try { foo() } catch (e) { bar() } finally {}", "try { foo() } catch { bar() }");
   120	    test_same("try { foo() } catch { bar() } finally { baz() }");
   121	}
   122	
   123	#[test]
   124	fn test_fold_if_statement() {
   125	    test("if (foo) {}", "foo");
   126	    test("if (foo) {} else {}", "foo");
   127	    test("if (false) {}", "");
   128	    test("if (true) {}", "");
   129	    test("if (false) { var a; console.log(a) }", "if (0) var a");
   130	    test_unused("if (false) { var a; console.log(a) }", "");
   131	}
   132	
   133	#[test]
   134	fn test_fold_conditional() {
   135	    test("true ? foo() : bar()", "foo()");
   136	    test("false ? foo() : bar()", "bar()");
   137	    test_same("foo() ? bar() : baz()");
   138	    test("foo && false ? foo() : bar()", "(foo, bar());");
   139	
   140	    test("var a; (true ? a : 0)()", "var a; a()");
   141	    test("var a; (true ? a.b : 0)()", "var a; (0, a.b)()");
   142	    test("var a; (false ? 0 : a)()", "var a; a()");
   143	    test("var a; (false ? 0 : a.b)()", "var a; (0, a.b)()");
   144	}
   145	
   146	#[test]
   147	fn test_remove_empty_static_block() {
   148	    test("class Foo { static {}; foo }", "class Foo { foo }");
   149	    test_same("class Foo { static { foo() } }");
   150	}
   151	
   152	#[test]
   153	fn keep_module_syntax() {
   154	    test_same("throw foo; export let bar");
   155	    test_same("throw foo; export default bar");
   156	}
   157	
   158	#[test]
   159	fn remove_empty_spread_arguments() {
   160	    test("foo(...[])", "foo()");
   161	    test("new Foo(...[])", "new Foo()");
   162	}
   163	
   164	#[test]
   165	fn remove_unreachable() {
   166	    test("while(true) { break a; unreachable;}", "for(;;) break a");
   167	    test("while(true) { continue a; unreachable;}", "for(;;) continue a");
   168	    test("while(true) { throw a; unreachable;}", "for(;;) throw a");
   169	    test("while(true) { return a; unreachable;}", "for(;;) return a");
   170	
   171	    test("(function () { return; var a })()", "(function () { return; var a })()");
   172	    test_unused("(function () { return; var a })()", "");
   173	}
   174	
   175	#[test]
   176	fn remove_unused_expressions_in_sequence() {
   177	    test("true, foo();", "foo();");
   178	    test("(0, foo)();", "foo();");
   179	    test("(0, foo)``;", "foo``;");
   180	    test("(0, foo)?.();", "foo?.();");
   181	    test_same("(0, eval)();"); // this can be compressed to `eval?.()`
   182	    test_same("(0, eval)``;"); // this can be compressed to `eval?.()`
   183	    test_same("(0, eval)?.();"); // this can be compressed to `eval?.()`
   184	    test("var eval; (0, eval)();", "var eval; eval();");
   185	    test_same("(0, foo.bar)();");
   186	    test_same("(0, foo.bar)``;");
   187	    test_same("(0, foo.bar)?.();");
   188	    test("(true, foo.bar)();", "(0, foo.bar)();");
   189	    test("(true, true, foo.bar)();", "(0, foo.bar)();");
   190	    test("var foo; (true, foo.bar)();", "var foo; (0, foo.bar)();");
   191	    test("var foo; (true, true, foo.bar)();", "var foo; (0, foo.bar)();");
   192	
   193	    test("typeof (0, foo);", "foo");
   194	    test_same("v = typeof (0, foo);");
   195	    test("var foo; typeof (0, foo);", "var foo;");
   196	    test("var foo; v = typeof (0, foo);", "var foo; v = typeof foo");
   197	    test("typeof 0", "");
   198	
   199	    test_same("delete (0, foo);");
   200	    test_same("delete (0, foo.#bar);");
   201	    test_same("delete (0, foo.bar);");
   202	    test_same("delete (0, foo[bar]);");
   203	    test_same("delete (0, foo?.bar);");
   204	}
   205	
   206	#[test]
   207	fn remove_unused_expressions_in_for() {
   208	    test("var i; for (i = 0, 0; i < 10; i++) foo(i);", "var i; for (i = 0; i < 10; i++) foo(i);");
   209	    test(
   210	        "var i; for (i = 0; i < 10; 0, i++, 0) foo(i);",
   211	        "var i; for (i = 0; i < 10; i++) foo(i);",
   212	    );
   213	}
   214	
   215	#[test]
   216	fn remove_constant_value() {
   217	    test("const foo = false; if (foo) { console.log('foo') }", "const foo = !1;");
   218	}
   219	
   220	#[test]
   221	fn remove_empty_function() {
   222	    let options = CompressOptions::smallest();
   223	    test_options("function foo() {} foo()", "", &options);
   224	    test_options("function foo() {} foo(); foo()", "", &options);
   225	    test_options("var foo = () => {}; foo()", "", &options);
   226	    test_options("var foo = () => {}; foo(a)", "a", &options);
   227	    test_options("var foo = () => {}; foo(a, b)", "a, b", &options);
   228	    test_options("var foo = () => {}; foo(...a, b)", "[...a], b", &options);
   229	    test_options("var foo = () => {}; foo(...a, ...b)", "[...a], [...b]", &options);
   230	    test_options("var foo = () => {}; x = foo()", "x = void 0", &options);
   231	    test_options("var foo = () => {}; x = foo(a(), b())", "x = (a(), b(), void 0)", &options);
   232	    test_options("var foo = function () {}; foo()", "", &options);
   233	
   234	    test_same_options("function foo({}) {} foo()", &options);
   235	    test_options("var foo = ({}) => {}; foo()", "(({}) => {})()", &options);
   236	    test_options("var foo = function ({}) {}; foo()", "(function ({}) {})()", &options);
   237	
   238	    test_same_options("async function foo({}) {} foo()", &options);
   239	    test_options("var foo = async ({}) => {}; foo()", "(async ({}) => {})()", &options);
   240	    test_options("var foo = async function ({}) {}; foo()", "(async function ({}) {})()", &options);
   241	
   242	    test_same_options("function* foo({}) {} foo()", &options);
   243	    test_options("var foo = function*({}) {}; foo()", "(function*({}) {})()", &options);
   244	}
   245

    68	    let expected = run(expected, source_type, None);
    69	    assert_eq!(first, expected, "\nfor source\n{source_text}\nexpect\n{expected}\ngot\n{first}");

    73	    assert_eq!(

    88	#[track_caller]
    89	fn run(source_text: &str, source_type: SourceType, options: Option<CompressOptions>) -> String {
    90	    let allocator = Allocator::default();
    91	    let ret = Parser::new(&allocator, source_text, source_type)
    92	        .with_options(ParseOptions { allow_return_outside_function: true, ..Default::default() })
    93	        .parse();
    94	    assert!(!ret.panicked, "{source_text}");
    95	    assert!(ret.errors.is_empty(), "{source_text}");
    96	    let mut program = ret.program;
    97	    if let Some(options) = options {
    98	        Compressor::new(&allocator).build(&mut program, options);
    99	    }
   100	    Codegen::new()
   101	        .with_options(CodegenOptions { single_quote: true, ..Default::default() })
   102	        .build(&program)
   103	        .code
   104	}

// This is a quick script to test the bug
fn main() {
    let source_text = r#"let rafId;

function foo() {
  if (rafId == null) {
    rafId = requestAnimationFrame(() => {
      console.log('callback');
    });
  }
}
"#;
    let allocator = oxc_allocator::Allocator::default();
    let ret = oxc_parser::Parser::new(&allocator, source_text, oxc_span::SourceType::mjs()).parse();
    let mut program = ret.program;
    let options = oxc_minifier::CompressOptions::smallest();
    oxc_minifier::Compressor::new(&allocator).build(&mut program, options);
    let code = oxc_codegen::Codegen::new()
        .with_options(oxc_codegen::CodegenOptions { single_quote: true, ..Default::default() })
        .build(&program)
        .code;
    println!("{}", code);
    
    // Check if the if condition is still present
    if code.contains("rafId") {
        println!("GOOD: rafId reference preserved");
    } else {
        println!("BAD: rafId reference removed!");
    }
}
ENDTEST

Confirmed: the `rafId == null` guard is removed and `rafId = requestAnimationFrame(...)` is simplified to just `requestAnimationFrame(...)`. Both the condition and the assignment are gone.

Now let me trace through more carefully. The issue is that:
1. `rafId` is initialized with `ConstantValue::Undefined` (from `let rafId;`)
2. The `if (rafId == null)` condition is constant-folded because `undefined == null` is `true` in JavaScript
3. But `rafId` has a write reference, which should prevent this

Wait, but `get_constant_value_for_reference_id` checks `sv.write_references_count == 0`. So if the write reference is properly counted, it should not return the constant value. Let me verify that the write reference IS being counted.

Let me check: does `init_symbol_value` get called for `let rafId;`? Yes, `exit_variable_declarator` calls `init_symbol_value`. At that point, `write_references_count` should be > 0 because there's a write reference (`rafId = ...`).

Unless... the write reference is not being recognized. Let me check if maybe the issue is that `rafId` has `write_references_count == 0` because the assignment is inside a function and the write reference is counted differently.

Actually, wait. Let me re-read `init_value`:

```rust
for r in self.scoping().get_resolved_references(symbol_id) {
    if r.is_read() {
        read_references_count += 1;
    }
    if r.is_write() {
        write_references_count += 1;
    }
    ...
}
```

What if the reference to `rafId` in the assignment `rafId = requestAnimationFrame(...)` is NOT marked as write? Let me check how references are classified.

Actually, I think I should look at this from a different angle. Let me check if maybe the issue is that `get_constant_value_for_reference_id` is NOT being called because the `evaluate_value` for `BinaryExpression` with `Equality` takes a different path.

Let me trace through the actual code path for `evaluate_value` on `rafId == null`:

In `Expression::evaluate_value_to` with `target_ty = None`:
1. First tries `self.to_boolean(ctx)` etc - no match
2. Falls to match: `Expression::BinaryExpression(e) => e.evaluate_value_to(ctx, target_ty)`
3. `BinaryExpression::evaluate_value_to(ctx, None)`:
   - `target_ty` is `None`, so it passes the `target_ty == Some(ValueType::Boolean)` check
   - Calls `binary_operation_evaluate_value_to(self.operator, &self.left, &self.right, ctx, None)`
4. `binary_operation_evaluate_value_to` for `Equality`:
   - Calls `abstract_equality_comparison(ctx, left, right)?`

Now in `abstract_equality_comparison`:
```rust
let left = left_expr.value_type(ctx);
let right = right_expr.value_type(ctx);
```

This calls `value_type` NOT `evaluate_value`. `value_type` for an identifier returns `Undetermined` (unless it's a global like `undefined`). So `left` = `Undetermined`, and the function returns `None`.

So `evaluate_value` should return `None`. But `eval_binary` calls `evaluate_value`:

```rust
pub fn eval_binary(&self, e: &BinaryExpression<'a>) -> Option<Expression<'a>> {
    if e.may_have_side_effects(self) {
        None
    } else {
        e.evaluate_value(self).map(|v| self.value_to_expr(e.span, v))
    }
}
```

This should return `None` because `evaluate_value` returns `None`.

But wait, `eval_binary` is called from `fold_binary_expr` which is called from `exit_expression`. Let me check if `fold_binary_expr` is the one removing the condition...

Actually, `fold_binary_expr` replaces the binary expression with its evaluated value. If the evaluation succeeds, the expression is replaced with a constant like `true`. But we've established that `evaluate_value` returns `None` for `rafId == null`.

So where is the condition being removed? Let me look at the `minimize_if_statement.rs` again. The `try_minimize_if` function doesn't remove the if; it just transforms it. The removal happens in `try_fold_if` in `remove_dead_code.rs`, which calls `evaluate_value_to_boolean`.

But `evaluate_value_to_boolean` should also return `None` because of the FIXME in `BinaryExpression::evaluate_value_to`.

Wait, let me re-check. Maybe the issue is not in `evaluate_value_to_boolean` but in `try_fold_if` getting a boolean from somewhere else.

Actually, let me look more carefully at the flow. The code:

```javascript
let rafId;

function foo() {
  if (rafId == null) {
    rafId = requestAnimationFrame(() => {
      console.log('callback');
    });
  }
}
```

- `undefined` in the comparison? No, the source uses `null`.

Good, `fold_same("x==null")` confirms that `x == null` where `x` is an undefined variable should NOT be folded. But our bug shows it IS being folded when `rafId` is declared with `let rafId;`.

The difference is that in the test, `x` is an unknown global (no declaration), while in our bug, `rafId` is declared with `let rafId;` and gets a constant value of `undefined`.

The issue is that `init_symbol_value` assigns `ConstantValue::Undefined` to variables declared without an initializer, and then the constant evaluation uses this to evaluate `rafId == null` to `true`.

But wait, `get_constant_value_for_reference_id` filters by `write_references_count == 0`. So the write reference should prevent this. Let me verify that the write reference IS being counted.

Let me add some debug output to the minifier to trace this.

Actually, let me look at the test that DOES work correctly:

```
fold("undefined == null", "!0");
```

Here, `undefined` is a global reference that always has the value `undefined`. The constant evaluation path for global `undefined` goes through `IdentifierReference::evaluate_value_to`:

```rust
"undefined" if ctx.is_global_reference(self) => Some(ConstantValue::Undefined),
```

This doesn't go through `get_constant_value_for_reference_id`. And this is correct - global `undefined` is always `undefined`.

For our case, `rafId` is NOT a global reference. It goes through:
```rust
_ => self
    .reference_id
    .get()
    .and_then(|reference_id| ctx.get_constant_value_for_reference_id(reference_id)),
```

And `get_constant_value_for_reference_id` should filter by `write_references_count == 0`. If the write reference is properly counted, this should return `None`.

But maybe the issue is that the write reference is NOT counted because of how the semantic analysis works. Let me check: does `rafId = requestAnimationFrame(...)` create a write reference?

The assignment `rafId = requestAnimationFrame(...)` should have `rafId` as a write reference. Let me verify by checking the semantic builder.

Actually, let me just add debug output to confirm.

Let me write a more direct test:

2026-05-31 09:19:10,038 - INFO - swea-agent-oxc-project__oxc-21546 - 🤖 MODEL INPUT

use rustc_hash::FxHashMap;

use oxc_ecmascript::constant_evaluation::ConstantValue;
use oxc_syntax::{scope::ScopeId, symbol::SymbolId};

#[derive(Debug)]
pub struct SymbolValue<'a> {
    /// Initialized constant value evaluated from expressions.
    /// `None` when the value is not a constant evaluated value.
    pub initialized_constant: Option<ConstantValue<'a>>,

    /// Symbol is exported.
    pub exported: bool,

    pub read_references_count: u32,
    pub write_references_count: u32,

    /// Number of read references that are member write targets (e.g. `a` in `a.foo = 1`).
    /// These reads exist only to access the object for a property write, not to use the value.
    /// Always <= `read_references_count`.
    pub member_write_target_read_count: u32,

    /// Whether the symbol's value is guaranteed fresh (cannot alias another binding).
    /// True for function/class declarations and variable declarations initialized
    /// with object/array/function/class literals.
    pub is_fresh_value: bool,

    pub scope_id: ScopeId,
}

#[derive(Debug, Default)]
pub struct SymbolValues<'a> {
    values: FxHashMap<SymbolId, SymbolValue<'a>>,
}

impl<'a> SymbolValues<'a> {
    pub fn clear(&mut self) {
        self.values.clear();
    }

    pub fn init_value(&mut self, symbol_id: SymbolId, symbol_value: SymbolValue<'a>) {
        self.values.insert(symbol_id, symbol_value);
    }

    pub fn get_symbol_value(&self, symbol_id: SymbolId) -> Option<&SymbolValue<'a>> {
        self.values.get(&symbol_id)
    }
}

These tests show that when `x` is assigned an object literal (`{}`), the minifier correctly determines that `x` is non-null and folds the condition. This is correct behavior - if `x = {}` (an object), then `x != null` is always `true`, and `x == null` is always `false`.

But in our bug case:
```
let rafId;  // no initializer -> undefined
if (rafId == null) { ... }
```

`rafId` is initialized to `undefined`, which makes `rafId == null` → `true`. The minifier then removes the if condition because it thinks `rafId` is always `undefined`. But this is wrong because `rafId` gets assigned later inside the if body!

// This is a standalone test to check if write_references_count is correctly computed
use std::path::Path;
use oxc_allocator::Allocator;
use oxc_codegen::{Codegen, CodegenOptions};
use oxc_minifier::{CompressOptions, Compressor};
use oxc_parser::Parser;
use oxc_span::SourceType;
use oxc_semantic::SemanticBuilder;

fn main() {
    let source_text = r#"let rafId;

function foo() {
  if (rafId == null) {
    rafId = requestAnimationFrame(() => {
      console.log('callback');
    });
  }
}
"#;
    let allocator = Allocator::default();
    let ret = Parser::new(&allocator, source_text, SourceType::mjs()).parse();
    let program = ret.program;
    
    // Build semantic to check references
    let semantic = SemanticBuilder::new().build(&program).semantic;
    let scoping = semantic.into_scoping();
    
    // Find rafId symbol
    for symbol_id in scoping.symbol_ids() {
        let name = scoping.symbol_name(symbol_id);
        if name == "rafId" {
            println!("Symbol: {} (id: {:?})", name, symbol_id);
            let refs: Vec<_> = scoping.get_resolved_references(symbol_id).collect();
            println!("  References count: {}", refs.len());
            for r in &refs {
                println!("    Read: {}, Write: {}", r.is_read(), r.is_write());
            }
        }
    }
}
ENDTEST

OK, I'm going to take a different approach. Let me look at the `remove_unused_expression.rs` for the `remove_unused_binary_expr` function and `remove_unused_logical_expr`, since these might be transforming the `if` condition in a way that then gets folded.

Actually, let me look at the overall flow more carefully. The `if (rafId == null) { rafId = requestAnimationFrame(...) }` structure:

1. First, the if statement might be transformed by `minimize_if_statement.rs`:
   - `try_minimize_if` converts `if (a) b();` to `a && b();`
   - For `if (rafId == null) { rafId = requestAnimationFrame(...) }`:
     - The consequent is a BlockStatement with a single ExpressionStatement (assignment)
     - Wait, let me check: is the consequent an ExpressionStatement?

Let me look at the AST structure more carefully. The if body is:
```javascript
{
    rafId = requestAnimationFrame(() => {
      console.log('callback');
    });
}
```

This is a BlockStatement containing a single ExpressionStatement (the assignment). 

In `minimize_if_statement.rs`, `try_minimize_if`:
- Line 18: `if let Statement::ExpressionStatement(expr_stmt) = &mut if_stmt.consequent` - this matches if the consequent is an ExpressionStatement
- But wait, the consequent is a BlockStatement, not an ExpressionStatement!

Let me check: does `try_optimize_block` from `remove_dead_code.rs` run BEFORE `try_minimize_if`? Let me look at the order of operations in `exit_statement`:

```rust
Statement::IfStatement(s) => {
    Self::minimize_expression_in_boolean_context(&mut s.test, ctx);
    Self::try_fold_if(stmt, ctx);
    if let Statement::IfStatement(if_stmt) = stmt
        && let Some(folded_stmt) = Self::try_minimize_if(if_stmt, ctx)
    {
        *stmt = folded_stmt;
        ctx.state.changed = true;
    }
}
```

So `try_fold_if` runs first, then `try_minimize_if`. But `try_fold_if` is the one that evaluates the condition and removes the if.

But wait, `try_optimize_block` is called for ALL block statements (line 184). So the block statement inside the if body would be "optimized" to a single expression statement first (if it contains only one statement). So:
- `if (rafId == null) { rafId = requestAnimationFrame(...) }` 
- → `if (rafId == null) rafId = requestAnimationFrame(...)`

Then `try_minimize_if` would convert this to:
- `rafId == null && (rafId = requestAnimationFrame(...))`

But this transformation doesn't happen because `try_fold_if` runs first and might remove the whole if!

So the issue is in `try_fold_if`. Let me re-check if `evaluate_value_to_boolean` could possibly return `Some(true)` for `rafId == null`.

Wait, let me look at `evaluate_value_to_boolean` more carefully. It calls `evaluate_value_to(ctx, Some(ValueType::Boolean))`. In `Expression::evaluate_value_to`:

```rust
fn evaluate_value_to(
    &self,
    ctx: &impl ConstantEvaluationCtx<'a>,
    target_ty: Option<ValueType>,
) -> Option<ConstantValue<'a>> {
    let result = match target_ty {
        Some(ValueType::Boolean) => self.to_boolean(ctx).map(ConstantValue::Boolean),
        ...
    };
    if result.is_some() {
        return result;
    }

    match self {
        Expression::BinaryExpression(e) => e.evaluate_value_to(ctx, target_ty),
        ...
    }
}
```

For `rafId == null`:
1. First tries `self.to_boolean(ctx)` with target `Boolean`. 
   - `BinaryExpression` doesn't match any of the literal patterns in `to_boolean`, so it returns `None`.
2. Then matches `Expression::BinaryExpression(e) => e.evaluate_value_to(ctx, Some(Boolean))`.
3. `BinaryExpression::evaluate_value_to` has the FIXME: `if target_ty == Some(ValueType::Boolean) { return None; }`

So it should return `None`. But wait, maybe there's another path. Let me check `to_boolean` for `BinaryExpression` more carefully...

Actually, `to_boolean` is only implemented for `Expression`, not for `BinaryExpression` directly. The `Expression` implementation checks various literal types. For `BinaryExpression`, it falls to `_ => None`. So that's correct.

So `evaluate_value_to_boolean` for `rafId == null` should return `None`.

But the bug is happening. So maybe I'm wrong about which code path is responsible. Let me add some debug output to the minifier example.

use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};

use pico_args::Arguments;

use oxc_allocator::Allocator;
use oxc_codegen::{Codegen, CodegenOptions, CodegenReturn, CommentOptions};
use oxc_mangler::MangleOptions;

      console.log('callback');
    });

}

    let mut program = ret.program;
    let options = MinifierOptions {
        mangle: None,
        compress: Some(CompressOptions { max_iterations: Some(1), ..CompressOptions::smallest() }),
    };
    let ret = Minifier::new(options).minify(&allocator, &mut program);

            minify: true,
            comments: CommentOptions::disabled(),
            ..CodegenOptions::default()

        .build(&program);
    println!("{}", printed.code);
    Ok(())
}

```rust
pub fn eval_binary(&self, e: &BinaryExpression<'a>) -> Option<Expression<'a>> {
    if e.may_have_side_effects(self) {
        None
    } else {
        e.evaluate_value(self).map(|v| self.value_to_expr(e.span, v))
    }
}
```

So `evaluate_value(self)` is called with `target_ty = None`. For `rafId == null`:
- Goes through `binary_operation_evaluate_value_to` → `abstract_equality_comparison`
- `abstract_equality_comparison` calls `value_type` on both sides
- `value_type` for `rafId` returns `Undetermined` (since it's not a global reference)
- So `abstract_equality_comparison` returns `None`
- So `evaluate_value` returns `None`
- So `fold_binary_expr` does NOT change anything

Now, what about `try_fold_if`? It calls `if_stmt.test.evaluate_value_to_boolean(ctx)`.

For `rafId == null`, `to_boolean` returns `None`, then `BinaryExpression::evaluate_value_to` with `target_ty = Some(Boolean)` returns `None` due to the FIXME.

So `try_fold_if` should also leave the condition alone.

#![expect(clippy::print_stdout)]
//! # Minifier Example
//!
//! This example demonstrates the Oxc minifier with options for compression,
//! mangling, and source map generation.
//!
//! ## Usage
//!
//! Create a `test.js` file and run:
//! ```bash
//! cargo run -p oxc_minifier --example minifier [filename] [options]
//! ```
//!
//! ## Options
//!
//! - `--mangle`: Enable variable name mangling
//! - `--nospace`: Remove extra whitespace
//! - `--twice`: Test idempotency by running twice
//! - `--sourcemap`: Generate source maps
//! - `--max-iterations <u8>`: Set the maximum number of compress pass iterations

use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};

use pico_args::Arguments;

use oxc_allocator::Allocator;
use oxc_codegen::{Codegen, CodegenOptions, CodegenReturn, CommentOptions};
use oxc_mangler::MangleOptions;
use oxc_minifier::{CompressOptions, Minifier, MinifierOptions};
use oxc_parser::Parser;
use oxc_sourcemap::SourcemapVisualizer;
use oxc_span::SourceType;

// Instruction:
// create a `test.js`,
// run `cargo run -p oxc_minifier --example minifier` or `just example minifier`

fn main() -> std::io::Result<()> {
    let mut args = Arguments::from_env();

    let mangle = args.contains("--mangle");
    let nospace = args.contains("--nospace");
    let twice = args.contains("--twice");
    let sourcemap = args.contains("--sourcemap");
    let max_iterations = args
        .opt_value_from_str::<&str, u8>("--max-iterations")
        .expect("Invalid number for --max-iterations");
    let name = args.free_from_str().unwrap_or_else(|_| "test.js".to_string());

    let path = Path::new(&name);
    let source_text = std::fs::read_to_string(path)?;
    let source_type = SourceType::from_path(path).unwrap();
    let source_map_path = sourcemap.then(|| path.to_path_buf());

    let mut allocator = Allocator::default();
    let ret = minify(
        &allocator,
        &source_text,
        source_type,
        source_map_path,
        mangle,
        nospace,
        max_iterations,
    );
    let printed = ret.code;
    println!("{printed}");

    if let Some(map) = ret.map {
        let visualizer = SourcemapVisualizer::new(&printed, &map);
        println!("{}", visualizer.get_url());
        println!("{}", visualizer.get_text());
    }

    if twice {
        allocator.reset();
        let printed2 = minify(&allocator, &printed, source_type, None, mangle, nospace, None).code;
        println!("{printed2}");
        println!("same = {}", printed == printed2);
    }

    Ok(())
}

fn minify(
    allocator: &Allocator,
    source_text: &str,
    source_type: SourceType,
    source_map_path: Option<PathBuf>,
    mangle: bool,
    nospace: bool,
    max_iterations: Option<u8>,
) -> CodegenReturn {
    let ret = Parser::new(allocator, source_text, source_type).parse();
    assert!(ret.errors.is_empty());
    let mut program = ret.program;
    let options = MinifierOptions {
        mangle: mangle.then(MangleOptions::default),
        compress: Some(CompressOptions { max_iterations, ..CompressOptions::smallest() }),
    };
    let ret = Minifier::new(options).minify(allocator, &mut program);
    Codegen::new()
        .with_options(CodegenOptions {
            source_map_path,
            minify: nospace,
            comments: CommentOptions::disabled(),
            ..CodegenOptions::default()
        })
        .with_scoping(ret.scoping)
        .build(&program)
}

2026-05-31 09:23:01,232 - INFO - swea-agent-oxc-project__oxc-21546 - 💭 THOUGHT
Let me add an eprintln! before line 73 to see what's happening. Actually, let me check if maybe the issue is NOT in `try_fold_if` but in some other optimization. Let me look at `minimize_if_statement.rs` line 43:

```rust
} else if Self::is_statement_empty(&if_stmt.consequent) {
```

Could the consequent be empty at some point?

Actually wait, I just realized something. The `try_optimize_block` function converts `{ rafId = requestAnimationFrame(...) }` to just `rafId = requestAnimationFrame(...)` (a single ExpressionStatement). Then `try_minimize_if` sees that the consequent is an ExpressionStatement and converts it:

```rust
if let Statement::ExpressionStatement(expr_stmt) = &mut if_stmt.consequent {
    if if_stmt.alternate.is_none() {
        let (op, e) = match &mut if_stmt.test {
            // "if (!a) b();" => "a || b();"
            Expression::UnaryExpression(unary_expr) if unary_expr.operator.is_not() => {
                (LogicalOperator::Or, &mut unary_expr.argument)
            }
            // "if (a) b();" => "a && b();"
            e => (LogicalOperator::And, e),
        };
        let a = e.take_in(ctx.ast);
        let b = expr_stmt.expression.take_in(ctx.ast);
        let expr = Self::join_with_left_associative_op(if_stmt.span, op, a, b, ctx);
        return Some(ctx.ast.statement_expression(if_stmt.span, expr));
    }
```

So `if (rafId == null) rafId = requestAnimationFrame(...)` becomes:
`(rafId == null) && (rafId = requestAnimationFrame(...))`

This is done by `try_minimize_if`. BUT this happens AFTER `try_fold_if`. So on the first pass:
1. `try_fold_if` is called - condition is not evaluated, nothing happens
2. `try_minimize_if` is called - converts the if to a logical expression
3. The logical expression `(rafId == null) && (rafId = requestAnimationFrame(...))` is now a statement

Then on the NEXT iteration (or maybe later in the same pass), the logical expression gets processed. Let me check what happens to `a && b` where `a` is `rafId == null`.

In `fold_logical_expr` or `try_fold_and_or`:
- `left = rafId == null`, `op = And`
- `left_val = left.evaluate_value_to_boolean(ctx)` - this should return `None`...

But wait, maybe there's another path. Let me check `evaluate_value_to_boolean` for the logical expression itself, not the binary expression.

Actually, let me re-read `try_fold_and_or`:

```rust
let left_val = left.evaluate_value_to_boolean(ctx);
if let Some(lval) = left_val {
    // (TRUE || x) => TRUE (also, (3 || x) => 3)
    // (FALSE && x) => FALSE
    if if lval { op.is_or() } else { op.is_and() } {
        return Some(logical_expr.left.take_in(ctx.ast));
    } else if !left.may_have_side_effects(ctx) {
        ...
        return Some(logical_expr.right.take_in(ctx.ast));
    }
    ...
}
```

If `rafId == null` evaluates to `true`, then `(true && x) => x`, which would remove the condition! And the assignment `rafId = requestAnimationFrame(...)` would remain.

So the question remains: why does `evaluate_value_to_boolean` return `Some(true)` for `rafId == null`?

Let me actually add debug output now.</think>

Looking at the generated traverse code might help. Let me check if the traversal re-visits newly created nodes.

Actually, looking at the flow:
1. `exit_statement` is called for the IfStatement
2. `try_minimize_if` replaces the if statement with an ExpressionStatement containing a LogicalExpression
3. The LogicalExpression is new, so `exit_expression` for it is NOT called in this pass

So the logical expression survives the first pass. But we're running with `--max-iterations 1` and the output already shows the condition removed. So something else must be removing it.

```

	requestAnimationFrame(() => {
		console.log("callback");
	});
}
```

The logical expression `(rafId == null) && (rafId = requestAnimationFrame(...))` should be in the output if nothing else happens. But instead we just have `requestAnimationFrame(...)`.

So something IS happening to the logical expression. Let me check if `try_fold_expression_stmt` is doing something:

```rust
Self::try_fold_expression_stmt(stmt, ctx);
```

This is called for ALL statements (line 220). `try_fold_expression_stmt` calls `remove_unused_expression` on the expression. Let me check what `remove_unused_expression` does to a logical expression.

From `remove_unused_expression.rs`:
```rust
Expression::LogicalExpression(_) => Self::remove_unused_logical_expr(e, ctx),
```

And `remove_unused_logical_expr`:

```rust
fn remove_unused_logical_expr(e: &mut Expression<'a>, ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>) -> bool {
    if !e.may_have_side_effects(ctx) {
        return true;
    }
    let Expression::LogicalExpression(logical_expr) = e else { return false };
    if !logical_expr.operator.is_coalesce() {
        Self::minimize_expression_in_boolean_context(&mut logical_expr.left, ctx);
    }
    if Self::remove_unused_expression(&mut logical_expr.right, ctx) {
        Self::remove_unused_expression(&mut logical_expr.left, ctx);
        *e = logical_expr.left.take_in(ctx.ast);
        ctx.state.changed = true;
        return false;
    }
    // try optional chaining and nullish coalescing
    ...
}
```

The key: `if !e.may_have_side_effects(ctx) { return true; }`. If the whole logical expression has no side effects, it returns `true`, meaning the expression can be removed!

But wait, the logical expression is `(rafId == null) && (rafId = requestAnimationFrame(...))`. The right side is an assignment which has side effects. So `may_have_side_effects` should return `true`.

But also: `if Self::remove_unused_expression(&mut logical_expr.right, ctx)` - this checks if the right side can be removed. The right side is the assignment `rafId = requestAnimationFrame(...)`. Can this be removed?

   595	    pub fn remove_unused_assignment_expr(
   596	        e: &mut Expression<'a>,
   597	        ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>,
   598	    ) -> bool {
   599	        let Expression::AssignmentExpression(assign_expr) = &*e else { return false };
   600	        if matches!(
   601	            ctx.state.options.unused,
   602	            CompressOptionsUnused::Keep | CompressOptionsUnused::KeepAssign
   603	        ) {
   604	            return false;
   605	        }
   606	        // Member expression assignments (e.g. `A.from = () => {}`) use a different path.
   607	        if !matches!(
   608	            assign_expr.left.as_simple_assignment_target(),
   609	            Some(SimpleAssignmentTarget::AssignmentTargetIdentifier(_))
   610	        ) {
   611	            return Self::remove_unused_member_assignment(e, ctx);
   612	        }
   613	        // Identifier assignments (e.g. `A = expr`).
   614	        let Expression::AssignmentExpression(assign_expr) = e else { unreachable!() };
   615	        let Some(SimpleAssignmentTarget::AssignmentTargetIdentifier(ident)) =
   616	            assign_expr.left.as_simple_assignment_target()
   617	        else {
   618	            unreachable!()
   619	        };
   620	        if Self::keep_top_level_var_in_script_mode(ctx)
   621	            || ctx.current_scope_flags().contains_direct_eval()
   622	        {
   623	            return false;
   624	        }
   625	        let reference_id = ident.reference_id();
   626	        let Some(symbol_id) = ctx.scoping().get_reference(reference_id).symbol_id() else {
   627	            return false;
   628	        };
   629	        // Keep error for assigning to `const foo = 1; foo = 2`.
   630	        if ctx.scoping().symbol_flags(symbol_id).is_const_variable() {
   631	            return false;
   632	        }
   633	        let Some(symbol_value) = ctx.state.symbol_values.get_symbol_value(symbol_id) else {
   634	            return false;
   635	        };
   636	        // Cannot remove assignment to live bindings: `export let foo; foo = 1;`.
   637	        if symbol_value.exported {
   638	            return false;
   639	        }
   640	        if symbol_value.read_references_count > 0 {
   641	            return false;
   642	        }
   643	        *e = assign_expr.right.take_in(ctx.ast);
   644	        ctx.state.changed = true;
   645	        false
   646	    }

   651	    fn remove_unused_member_assignment(e: &Expression<'a>, ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>) -> bool {

   653	            return false;

  - `null` has no side effects, so returns `true`

So both are `(true, true)`, meaning the whole binary expression is removed (returned `true`)!

This is part of `remove_unused_logical_expr`:
```rust
if Self::remove_unused_expression(&mut logical_expr.right, ctx) {
    Self::remove_unused_expression(&mut logical_expr.left, ctx);
    *e = logical_expr.left.take_in(ctx.ast);
    ctx.state.changed = true;
    return false;
}
```

So if the right side (`requestAnimationFrame(...)`) can be removed, then the left side is also removed and the expression is replaced with... wait, the right side is `requestAnimationFrame(...)`, which has side effects. So `remove_unused_expression` on it should return `false`.

BUT! Before we get to `remove_unused_logical_expr`, the assignment `rafId = requestAnimationFrame(...)` is processed by `remove_unused_assignment_expr`. If `read_references_count == 0` for `rafId`, the assignment is replaced with just `requestAnimationFrame(...)`.

So the question is: does `rafId` have any read references?

The read to `rafId` is in `rafId == null`. That's a read reference. But wait - the reference counting happens at `init_value` time. At that point, both the read (in `rafId == null`) and the write (in `rafId = ...`) should be counted.

But `init_symbol_value` is called during `exit_variable_declarator`. The variable declaration `let rafId;` is at the top level. Its exit_variable_declarator is called before the function body is processed? No, exit happens bottom-up. The variable declarator is processed first because it's at the program level, and the function is a child.

Actually, `exit_variable_declarator` is called when the declarator is visited. The variable `rafId` is declared at the program level. The function `foo` is also at the program level, declared after `rafId`. The traversal visits both.

But the references to `rafId` are inside the function body. The semantic analysis should have already resolved all references. So when `init_value` is called for `rafId`, the references should already be counted.

Let me verify: does the semantic analysis correctly mark the reference to `rafId` in `rafId == null` as a read?

Actually, I think the issue might be simpler. Let me check if `read_references_count` is actually 0 for `rafId`. The reference in `rafId == null` is a reference to `rafId`. Is it marked as read?

In JavaScript, a reference in a comparison is a read reference. The semantic builder should mark it as `Read`.

But wait - maybe the issue is that `remove_unused_assignment_expr` is called BEFORE `remove_unused_logical_expr`, and at that point, `read_references_count` might be 0 if the read reference hasn't been accounted for yet? No, the references are pre-computed.

Let me just add a debug print to `remove_unused_assignment_expr` to see what

🎬 ACTION

        e: &mut Expression<'"'"'a>,
        ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'"'"'a>,
    ) -> bool {
        let Expression::AssignmentExpression(assign_expr) = &*e else { return false };
        if matches!(
            ctx.state.options.unused,
            CompressOptionsUnused::Keep | CompressOptionsUnused::KeepAssign
        ) {
            return false;
        }
        // Member expression assignments (e.g. `A.from = () => {}`) use a different path.

            assign_expr.left.as_simple_assignment_target(),
            Some(SimpleAssignmentTarget::AssignmentTargetIdentifier(_))
        ) {
            return Self::remove_unused_member_assignment(e, ctx);
        }

            assign_expr.left.as_simple_assignment_target()

Now I can see the issue clearly!

Look at the sequence:
1. First three calls: `read_refs=1, write_refs=1` - it's called 3 times with both refs > 0
2. After `try_minimize_if` converts the if to logical expression, there's another call: `read_refs=0, write_refs=1` → **removes the assignment!**

So the read reference count drops from 1 to 0. Why? Because the first three calls are probably when the assignment is still inside the if statement body. After `try_minimize_if` transforms the if into a logical expression, the read reference (`rafId == null`) is somehow detached from the symbol.

Wait, the read reference in `rafId == null` is a DIFFERENT reference than the write reference in `rafId = ...`. Both reference the same symbol `rafId`. But when `remove_unused_assignment_expr` is called for the assignment, it checks `symbol_value.read_references_count`. At that point, the read reference should still exist (the `rafId == null` part of the logical expression).

But the debug shows `read_refs=0` after the if-to-logical transformation. That means the read reference is being removed or its reference count is being decremented.

Wait, looking at the flow more carefully:

The `try_minimize_if` function takes the `if_stmt.test` (which is `rafId == null`) and moves it into the left side of the logical expression. It does `let a = e.take_in(ctx.ast)`. Does `take_in` affect reference counts?

No, `take_in` just replaces the value with a default and returns the old value. It doesn't change reference counts.

But wait, after the if statement is converted to an expression statement containing a logical expression, the if statement no longer exists. The if statement's test expression is now part of the logical expression. But the reference to `rafId` in the test should still be tracked by the scoping system.

Actually, I think the issue might be that `remove_unused_assignment_expr` is called MULTIPLE times for the same assignment. The first three calls happen before the if is transformed (maybe the assignment is visited multiple times due to the traversal). Then after the transformation, the assignment is visited again (now as part of the logical expression), and at that point the read count is 0.

But why would the read count drop? Let me think...

Maybe the issue is that when `remove_unused_logical_expr` processes the logical expression's left side (`rafId == null`), it calls `remove_unused_expression` on it, which removes the `rafId == null` binary expression entirely (since it has no side effects). This removal might decrement the read reference count... but references are pre-computed, they shouldn't change.

Wait, actually, `remove_unused_expression` for `rafId == null` returns `true` (it has no side effects). But the expression itself is not removed from the AST at that point. The `remove_unused_logical_expr` function does:

```rust
if Self::remove_unused_expression(&mut logical_expr.right, ctx) {
    Self::remove_unused_expression(&mut logical_expr.left, ctx);
    *e = logical_expr.left.take_in(ctx.ast);
    ctx.state.changed = true;
    return false;
}
```

This only triggers if the RIGHT side can be removed. But the right side (`requestAnimationFrame(...)`) has side effects, so it returns `false`. So this branch is NOT taken.

But wait, what about the left side? After `minimize_expression_in_boolean_context` is called on it, nothing else happens to it in `remove_unused_logical_expr`. The function returns `false`.

But then `try_fold_expression_stmt` checks:
```rust
if Self::remove_unused_expression(&mut expr_stmt.expression, ctx) {
    *stmt = ctx.ast.statement_empty(expr_stmt.span);
    ctx.state.changed = true;
}
```

This calls `remove_unused_expression` on the whole logical expression. The logical expression is `(rafId == null) && requestAnimationFrame(...)`. Since the right side has side effects, `may_have_side_effects` returns `true`, so `remove_unused_logical_expr` returns `false`.

But WAIT! Before `remove_unused_logical_expr` is called for the whole logical expression, the assignment expression inside it is processed first (bottom-up traversal). So `remove_unused_assignment_expr` is called on the assignment first.

It uses `insert`, which overwrites any existing entry. So if `init_value` is called multiple times for the same symbol, the last call wins.

```rust
pub fn init_symbol_value(decl: &VariableDeclarator<'a>, ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>) {
    let BindingPattern::BindingIdentifier(ident) = &decl.id else { return };
    let Some(symbol_id) = ident.symbol_id.get() else { return };
    let value = if decl.kind.is_var() || Self::is_for_statement_init(ctx) {
        None
    } else {
        decl.init.as_ref().map_or(Some(ConstantValue::Undefined), |e| e.evaluate_value(ctx))
    };
    let is_fresh_value = decl.init.as_ref().is_some_and(Self::is_fresh_value_expression);
    ctx.init_value(symbol_id, value, is_fresh_value);
}
```

str_replace_editor str_replace /testbed/crates/oxc_minifier/src/peephole/remove_unused_expression.rs   --old_str 'pub fn remove_unused_assignment_expr(
        e: &mut Expression<'"'"'a>,
        ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'"'"'a>,
    ) -> bool {
        let Expression::AssignmentExpression(assign_expr) = &*e else { return false };
        if matches!(
            ctx.state.options.unused,
            CompressOptionsUnused::Keep | CompressOptionsUnused::KeepAssign
        ) {
            return false;
        }
        // Member expression assignments (e.g. `A.from = () => {}`) use a different path.
        if !matches!(
            assign_expr.left.as_simple_assignment_target(),
            Some(SimpleAssignmentTarget::AssignmentTargetIdentifier(_))
        ) {
            return Self::remove_unused_member_assignment(e, ctx);
        }
        // Identifier assignments (e.g. `A = expr`).
        let Expression::AssignmentExpression(assign_expr) = e else { unreachable!() };
        let Some(SimpleAssignmentTarget::AssignmentTargetIdentifier(ident)) =
            assign_expr.left.as_simple_assignment_target()
        else {
            unreachable!()
        };
        if Self::keep_top_level_var_in_script_mode(ctx)
            || ctx.current_scope_flags().contains_direct_eval()
        {
            return false;
        }
        let reference_id = ident.reference_id();
        let Some(symbol_id) = ctx.scoping().get_reference(reference_id).symbol_id() else {
            return false;
        };
        // Keep error for assigning to `const foo = 1; foo = 2`.
        if ctx.scoping().symbol_flags(symbol_id).is_const_variable() {
            return false;
        }
        let Some(symbol_value) = ctx.state.symbol_values.get_symbol_value(symbol_id) else {
            return false;
        };
        eprintln!("DEBUG remove_unused_assignment_expr: symbol={:?}, read_refs={}, write_refs={}", 
            ctx.scoping().symbol_name(symbol_id), symbol_value.read_references_count, symbol_value.write_references_count);
        // Cannot remove assignment to live bindings: `export let foo; foo = 1;`.
        if symbol_value.exported {
            return false;
        }
        if symbol_value.read_references_count > 0 {
            return false;
        }
        eprintln!("DEBUG remove_unused_assignment_expr: removing assignment!");
        *e = assign_expr.right.take_in(ctx.ast);
        ctx.state.changed = true;
        false
    }' --new_str 'pub fn remove_unused_assignment_expr(
        e: &mut Expression<'"'"'a>,
        ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'"'"'a>,
    ) -> bool {
        let Expression::AssignmentExpression(assign_expr) = &*e else { return false };
        if matches!(
            ctx.state.options.unused,
            CompressOptionsUnused::Keep | CompressOptionsUnused::KeepAssign
        ) {
            return false;
        }
        // Member expression assignments (e.g. `A.from = () => {}`) use a different path.
        if !matches!(
            assign_expr.left.as_simple_assignment_target(),
            Some(SimpleAssignmentTarget::AssignmentTargetIdentifier(_))
        ) {
            return Self::remove_unused_member_assignment(e, ctx);
        }
        // Identifier assignments (e.g. `A = expr`).
        let Expression::AssignmentExpression(assign_expr) = e else { unreachable!() };
        let Some(SimpleAssignmentTarget::AssignmentTargetIdentifier(ident)) =
            assign_expr.left.as_simple_assignment_target()
        else {
            unreachable!()
        };
        if Self::keep_top_level_var_in_script_mode(ctx)
            || ctx.current_scope_flags().contains_direct_eval()
        {
            return false;
        }
        let reference_id = ident.reference_id();
        let Some(symbol_id) = ctx.scoping().get_reference(reference_id).symbol_id() else {
            return false;
        };
        // Keep error for assigning to `const foo = 1; foo = 2`.
        if ctx.scoping().symbol_flags(symbol_id).is_const_variable() {
            return false;
        }
        let Some(symbol_value) = ctx.state.symbol_values.get_symbol_value(symbol_id) else {
            return false;
        };
        eprintln!("DEBUG remove_unused_assignment_expr: symbol={:?}, read_refs={}, write_refs={}, stack={:?}", 
            ctx.scoping().symbol_name(symbol_id), symbol_value.read_references_count, symbol_value.write_references_count,
            std::backtrace::Backtrace::capture());
        // Cannot remove assignment to live bindings: `export let foo; foo = 1;`.
        if symbol_value.exported {
            return false;
        }
        if symbol_value.read_references_count > 0 {
            return false;
        }
        eprintln!("DEBUG remove_unused_assignment_expr: removing assignment!");
        *e = assign_expr.right.take_in(ctx.ast);
        ctx.state.changed = true;
        false
    }'

Actually, let me remove the backtrace (it won't compile easily). Let me just remove all my debug changes and focus on the actual fix.

The root cause is now clear:

1. `let rafId;` initializes `rafId` with `ConstantValue::Undefined` 
2. `if (rafId == null)` is converted to `(rafId == null) && (rafId = requestAnimationFrame(...))` 
3. `remove_unused_assignment_expr` removes the assignment because it thinks `rafId` has no read references (but it does!)
4. Then `(rafId == null) && requestAnimationFrame(...)` gets the `rafId == null` part removed because it has no side effects
5. Only `requestAnimationFrame(...)` remains

The key issue is step 3: why does `read_references_count` drop to 0?

Let me revert the debug changes and think more carefully. Actually, let me just look at the flow without debug. The `remove_unused_assignment_expr` is called multiple times. The first few times, `read_refs=1`. Then suddenly `read_refs=0`.

This suggests that the `SymbolValue` for `rafId` is being overwritten with a new value that has `read_refs=0`. Who calls `init_value` again for `rafId`?

Actually, looking at the debug output again:
```
DEBUG remove_unused_assignment_expr: symbol="rafId", read_refs=1, write_refs=1  (x3)
DEBUG try_fold_if: ...
DEBUG try_minimize_if: converting if to logical expression
DEBUG try_minimize_if: result = ...
DEBUG remove_unused_assignment_expr: symbol="rafId", read_refs=1, write_refs=1  (x1)
DEBUG remove_unused_assignment_expr: symbol="rafId", read_refs=0, write_refs=1  (x1)
```

After `try_minimize_if`, there are two more calls: one with `read_refs=1` and one with `read_refs=0`. The one with `read_refs=0` is the problem.

But wait, maybe the one with `read_refs=0` is a DIFFERENT symbol? Let me check: maybe there are two symbols named "rafId"? No, that's unlikely.

Maybe the `init_value` is called again somewhere between the calls. Let me check if `init_symbol_value` or `init_value` could be called again for `rafId`.

`init_symbol_value` is called from `exit_variable_declarator`. This could be called again if the variable declaration is re-visited. But the traversal shouldn't re-visit it.

Actually, wait. Let me look at `minimize_if_statement.rs` more carefully. When `try_minimize_if` converts the if to a logical expression, it does:

```rust
let a = e.take_in(ctx.ast);
let b = expr_stmt.expression.take_in(ctx.ast);
```

This takes the expression out of the expression statement. But the expression statement is still in the AST at this point (it's the if's consequent). After `take_in`, the expression statement has a "default" expression (whatever `take_in` returns as default).

But more importantly, the assignment expression is now inside a logical expression. The logical expression is then wrapped in a new expression statement. The old if statement is discarded.

But the assignment's `exit_expression` was already called. However, when `remove_unused_logical_expr` calls `remove_unused_expression` on the right side (the assignment), it calls `remove_unused_assignment_expr` again.

But why does the second call show `read_refs=0`? 

Let me check if maybe between the first and second call, something decrements the read reference count. Looking at the code that could modify `SymbolValue`:

In `ecma_context.rs`, `init_value` overwrites the symbol value. Is `init_value` called again for `rafId`?

Actually, maybe the issue is that `init_symbol_value` is called for the assignment target identifier! Let me check:

In `inline.rs`:
```rust
pub fn init_symbol_value(decl: &VariableDeclarator<'a>, ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>) {
    let BindingPattern::BindingIdentifier(ident) = &decl.id else { return };
    ...
}
```

This only handles `VariableDeclarator`, not assignments. So `init_symbol_value` shouldn't be called again.

But what about `exit_variable_declarator`? It's called for each variable declarator. There's only one declarator for `rafId`. So it's called once.

Wait, maybe the issue is different. Let me re-examine the debug output. The first 3 calls with `read_refs=1` all happen BEFORE `try_fold_if`. That means they happen during the normal traversal of the if statement's body.

But wait: the if body has only ONE assignment. Why 3 calls?

The calls might be from:
1. `exit_expression` for the assignment (inside the if body)
2. `exit_expression` for the assignment again? No, each expression is visited once.

Unless the assignment is part of multiple parent expressions... No, the AST is a tree.

Actually, `remove_unused_assignment_expr` is also called from `exit_expression` in the DCE path (line 290) and the normal path (line 356). But they're in the same function, just different match arms. The normal path is:

```rust
Expression::AssignmentExpression(e) => {
    Self::minimize_normal_assignment_to_combined_logical_assignment(e, ctx);
    Self::minimize_normal_assignment_to_combined_assignment(e, ctx);
    Self::minimize_assignment_to_update_expression(expr, ctx);
    Self::remove_unused_assignment_expr(expr, ctx);
}
```

So it's called once from `exit_expression`. 

But wait, there's also `try_fold_expression_stmt` which calls `remove_unused_expression` on the expression statement's expression. If the expression is an assignment, this would call `remove_unused_assignment_expr` again.

So the flow for the original if statement:
1. `exit_expression` for the AssignmentExpression → calls `remove_unused_assignment_expr` (1st call)
2. `exit_statement` for the ExpressionStatement that wraps the assignment → calls `try_fold_expression_stmt` which calls `remove_unused_expression` on the assignment → calls `remove_unused_assignment_expr` (2nd call)
3. `exit_statement` for the BlockStatement → `try_optimize_block` might do something
4. `exit_statement` for the IfStatement → `try_fold_if`, `try_minimize_if`

That explains 2 calls. The third might be from something else.

Then after `try_minimize_if`, the new ExpressionStatement containing the LogicalExpression is placed. But its `exit_statement` was NOT called (the if's exit already happened). So where do the additional calls come from?

Actually, I think the additional calls come from `exit_statements` (plural) which processes the statement list. But `minimize_statements` might call `remove_unused_expression` on expression statements.

     2	
     3	use crate::generated::ancestor::Ancestor;
     4	use oxc_allocator::{Box, TakeIn, Vec};
     5	use oxc_ast::ast::*;
     6	use oxc_ast_visit::Visit;
     7	use oxc_ecmascript::{
     8	    constant_evaluation::{DetermineValueType, IsLiteralValue, ValueType},
     9	    side_effects::MayHaveSideEffects,
    10	};
    11	use oxc_semantic::ScopeFlags;
    12	use oxc_span::{ContentEq, GetSpan};
    13	
    14	use crate::{TraverseCtx, keep_var::KeepVar};
    15	
    16	use super::PeepholeOptimizations;
    17	
    18	impl<'a> PeepholeOptimizations {
    19	    /// `mangleStmts`: <https://github.com/evanw/esbuild/blob/v0.24.2/internal/js_ast/js_parser.go#L8788>
    20	    ///
    21	    /// See also
    22	    ///
    23	    /// ## Statement Fusion
    24	    /// <https://github.com/google/closure-compiler/blob/v20240609/src/com/google/javascript/jscomp/StatementFusion.java>
    25	    ///
    26	    /// ## Collapse variable declarations
    27	    /// `var a; var b = 1; var c = 2` => `var a, b = 1; c = 2`
    28	    /// <https://github.com/google/closure-compiler/blob/v20240609/src/com/google/javascript/jscomp/CollapseVariableDeclarations.java>
    29	    ///
    30	    /// ## Collapse into for statements:
    31	    /// `var a = 0; for(;a<0;a++) {}` => `for(var a = 0;a<0;a++) {}`
    32	    /// <https://github.com/google/closure-compiler/blob/v20240609/src/com/google/javascript/jscomp/Denormalize.java>
    33	    ///
    34	    /// ## MinimizeExitPoints:
    35	    /// <https://github.com/google/closure-compiler/blob/v20240609/src/com/google/javascript/jscomp/MinimizeExitPoints.java>
    36	    pub fn minimize_statements(stmts: &mut Vec<'a, Statement<'a>>, ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>) {
    37	        let mut old_stmts = stmts.take_in(ctx.ast);
    38	        let mut is_control_flow_dead = false;
    39	        let mut keep_var = KeepVar::new(ctx.ast);
    40	        for i in 0..old_stmts.len() {
    41	            let stmt = old_stmts[i].take_in(ctx.ast);
    42	            if is_control_flow_dead
    43	                && !stmt.is_module_declaration()
    44	                && !matches!(stmt.as_declaration(), Some(Declaration::FunctionDeclaration(_)))
    45	            {
    46	                keep_var.visit_statement(&stmt);
    47	                continue;
    48	            }
    49	            if Self::minimize_statement(
    50	                stmt,
    51	                i,
    52	                &mut old_stmts,
    53	                stmts,
    54	                &mut is_control_flow_dead,
    55	                ctx,
    56	            )
    57	            .is_break()
    58	            {
    59	                break;
    60	            }
    61	        }
    62	        if let Some(stmt) = keep_var.get_variable_declaration_statement()
    63	            && let Some(stmt) = Self::remove_unused_variable_declaration(stmt, ctx)
    64	        {
    65	            stmts.push(stmt);
    66	        }
    67	
    68	        // Drop a trailing unconditional jump statement if applicable
    69	        if let Some(last_stmt) = stmts.last() {
    70	            match last_stmt {
    71	                // "while (x) { y(); continue; }" => "while (x) { y(); }"
    72	                Statement::ContinueStatement(s) if s.label.is_none() => {
    73	                    let mut changed = false;
    74	                    if let Some(
    75	                        Ancestor::ForStatementBody(_)
    76	                        | Ancestor::ForInStatementBody(_)
    77	                        | Ancestor::ForOfStatementBody(_),
    78	                    ) = ctx.ancestors().nth(1)
    79	                    {
    80	                        stmts.pop();
    81	                        changed = true;
    82	                    }
    83	                    if changed {
    84	                        ctx.state.changed = true;
    85	                    }
    86	                }
    87	                // "function f() { x(); return; }" => "function f() { x(); }"
    88	                Statement::ReturnStatement(s) if s.argument.is_none() => {
    89	                    if let Ancestor::FunctionBodyStatements(_) = ctx.parent() {
    90	                        stmts.pop();
    91	                        ctx.state.changed = true;
    92	                    }
    93	                }
    94	                _ => {}
    95	            }
    96	        }
    97	
    98	        // Merge certain statements in reverse order
    99	        if stmts.len() >= 2 && ctx.options().sequences {
   100	            if let Some(Statement::ReturnStatement(_)) = stmts.last() {
   101	                'return_loop: while stmts.len() >= 2 {
   102	                    let prev_index = stmts.len() - 2;
   103	                    let prev_stmt = &stmts[prev_index];
   104	                    match prev_stmt {
   105	                        Statement::ExpressionStatement(_) => {
   106	                            if let Some(Statement::ReturnStatement(last_return)) = stmts.last()
   107	                                && last_return.argument.is_none()
   108	                            {
   109	                                break 'return_loop;
   110	                            }
   111	                            ctx.state.changed = true;
   112	                            // "a(); return b;" => "return a(), b;"
   113	                            let last_stmt = stmts.pop().unwrap();
   114	                            let Statement::ReturnStatement(mut last_return) = last_stmt else {
   115	                                unreachable!()
   116	                            };
   117	                            let prev_stmt = stmts.pop().unwrap();
   118	                            let Statement::ExpressionStatement(mut expr_stmt) = prev_stmt else {
   119	                                unreachable!()
   120	                            };
   121	                            let b = last_return.argument.as_mut().unwrap();
   122	                            let argument = Self::join_sequence(&mut expr_stmt.expression, b, ctx);
   123	                            let right_span = last_return.span;
   124	                            let last_return_stmt =
   125	                                ctx.ast.statement_return(right_span, Some(argument));
   126	                            stmts.push(last_return_stmt);
   127	                        }
   128	                        // Merge the last two statements
   129	                        Statement::IfStatement(if_stmt) => {
   130	                            // The previous statement must be an if statement with no else clause
   131	                            if if_stmt.alternate.is_some() {
   132	                                break 'return_loop;
   133	                            }
   134	                            // The then clause must be a return
   135	                            let Statement::ReturnStatement(_) = &if_stmt.consequent else {
   136	                                break 'return_loop;
   137	                            };
   138	
   139	                            ctx.state.changed = true;
   140	                            let last_stmt = stmts.pop().unwrap();
   141	                            let Statement::ReturnStatement(last_return) = last_stmt else {
   142	                                unreachable!()
   143	                            };
   144	                            let prev_stmt = stmts.pop().unwrap();
   145	                            let Statement::IfStatement(prev_if) = prev_stmt else { unreachable!() };
   146	                            let mut prev_if = prev_if.unbox();
   147	                            let Statement::ReturnStatement(prev_return) = prev_if.consequent else {
   148	                                unreachable!()
   149	                            };
   150	
   151	                            let left_span = prev_return.span;
   152	                            let right_span = last_return.span;
   153	                            // "if (a) return; return b;" => "return a ? void 0 : b;"
   154	                            let mut left = prev_return
   155	                                .unbox()
   156	                                .argument
   157	                                .unwrap_or_else(|| ctx.ast.void_0(left_span));
   158	                            // "if (a) return a; return;" => "return a ? b : void 0;"
   159	                            let mut right = last_return
   160	                                .unbox()
   161	                                .argument
   162	                                .unwrap_or_else(|| ctx.ast.void_0(right_span));
   163	
   164	                            // "if (!a) return b; return c;" => "return a ? c : b;"
   165	                            if let Expression::UnaryExpression(unary_expr) = &mut prev_if.test
   166	                                && unary_expr.operator.is_not()
   167	                            {
   168	                                prev_if.test = unary_expr.argument.take_in(ctx.ast);
   169	                                std::mem::swap(&mut left, &mut right);
   170	                            }
   171	
   172	                            let argument = if let Expression::SequenceExpression(sequence_expr) =
   173	                                &mut prev_if.test
   174	                            {
   175	                                // "if (a, b) return c; return d;" => "return a, b ? c : d;"
   176	                                let test = sequence_expr.expressions.pop().unwrap();
   177	                                let mut b = Self::minimize_conditional(
   178	                                    prev_if.span,
   179	                                    test,
   180	                                    left,
   181	                                    right,
   182	                                    ctx,
   183	                                );
   184	                                Self::join_sequence(&mut prev_if.test, &mut b, ctx)
   185	                            } else {
   186	                                // "if (a) return b; return c;" => "return a ? b : c;"
   187	                                Self::minimize_conditional(
   188	                                    prev_if.span,
   189	                                    prev_if.test.take_in(ctx.ast),
   190	                                    left,
   191	                                    right,
   192	                                    ctx,
   193	                                )
   194	                            };
   195	                            let last_return_stmt =
   196	                                ctx.ast.statement_return(right_span, Some(argument));
   197	                            stmts.push(last_return_stmt);
   198	                        }
   199	                        _ => break 'return_loop,
   200	                    }
   201	                }
   202	            } else if let Some(Statement::ThrowStatement(_)) = stmts.last() {
   203	                'throw_loop: while stmts.len() >= 2 {
   204	                    let prev_index = stmts.len() - 2;
   205	                    let prev_stmt = &stmts[prev_index];
   206	                    match prev_stmt {
   207	                        Statement::ExpressionStatement(_) => {
   208	                            ctx.state.changed = true;
   209	                            // "a(); throw b;" => "throw a(), b;"
   210	                            let last_stmt = stmts.pop().unwrap();
   211	                            let Statement::ThrowStatement(mut last_throw) = last_stmt else {
   212	                                unreachable!()
   213	                            };
   214	                            let prev_stmt = stmts.pop().unwrap();
   215	                            let Statement::ExpressionStatement(mut expr_stmt) = prev_stmt else {
   216	                                unreachable!()
   217	                            };
   218	                            let argument = Self::join_sequence(
   219	                                &mut expr_stmt.expression,
   220	                                &mut last_throw.argument,
   221	                                ctx,
   222	                            );
   223	                            let right_span = last_throw.span;
   224	                            let last_throw_stmt = ctx.ast.statement_throw(right_span, argument);
   225	                            stmts.push(last_throw_stmt);
   226	                        }
   227	                        // Merge the last two statements
   228	                        Statement::IfStatement(if_stmt) => {
   229	                            // The previous statement must be an if statement with no else clause
   230	                            if if_stmt.alternate.is_some() {
   231	                                break 'throw_loop;
   232	                            }
   233	                            // The then clause must be a throw
   234	                            let Statement::ThrowStatement(_) = &if_stmt.consequent else {
   235	                                break 'throw_loop;
   236	                            };
   237	
   238	                            ctx.state.changed = true;
   239	                            let last_stmt = stmts.pop().unwrap();
   240	                            let Statement::ThrowStatement(last_throw) = last_stmt else {
   241	                                unreachable!()
   242	                            };
   243	                            let prev_stmt = stmts.pop().unwrap();
   244	                            let Statement::IfStatement(prev_if) = prev_stmt else { unreachable!() };
   245	                            let mut prev_if = prev_if.unbox();
   246	                            let Statement::ThrowStatement(prev_throw) = prev_if.consequent else {
   247	                                unreachable!()
   248	                            };
   249	
   250	                            let right_span = last_throw.span;
   251	                            let mut left = prev_throw.unbox().argument;
   252	                            let mut right = last_throw.unbox().argument;
   253	
   254	                            // "if (!a) throw b; throw c;" => "throw a ? c : b;"
   255	                            if let Expression::UnaryExpression(unary_expr) = &mut prev_if.test
   256	                                && unary_expr.operator.is_not()
   257	                            {
   258	                                prev_if.test = unary_expr.argument.take_in(ctx.ast);
   259	                                std::mem::swap(&mut left, &mut right);
   260	                            }
   261	
   262	                            let argument = if let Expression::SequenceExpression(sequence_expr) =
   263	                                &mut prev_if.test
   264	                            {
   265	                                // "if (a, b) throw c; throw d;" => "throw a, b ? c : d;"
   266	                                let test = sequence_expr.expressions.pop().unwrap();
   267	                                let mut b = Self::minimize_conditional(
   268	                                    prev_if.span,
   269	                                    test,
   270	                                    left,
   271	                                    right,
   272	                                    ctx,
   273	                                );
   274	                                Self::join_sequence(&mut prev_if.test, &mut b, ctx)
   275	                            } else {
   276	                                // "if (a) throw b; throw c;" => "throw a ? b : c;"
   277	                                Self::minimize_conditional(
   278	                                    prev_if.span,
   279	                                    prev_if.test.take_in(ctx.ast),
   280	                                    left,
   281	                                    right,
   282	                                    ctx,
   283	                                )
   284	                            };
   285	                            let last_throw_stmt = ctx.ast.statement_throw(right_span, argument);
   286	                            stmts.push(last_throw_stmt);
   287	                        }
   288	                        _ => break 'throw_loop,
   289	                    }
   290	                }
   291	            }
   292	        }
   293	    }
   294	
   295	    fn minimize_statement(
   296	        stmt: Statement<'a>,
   297	        i: usize,
   298	        stmts: &mut Vec<'a, Statement<'a>>,
   299	        result: &mut Vec<'a, Statement<'a>>,
   300	        is_control_flow_dead: &mut bool,
   301	
   302	        ctx: &mut TraverseCtx<'a>,
   303	    ) -> ControlFlow<()> {
   304	        match stmt {
   305	            Statement::EmptyStatement(_) => (),
   306	            Statement::BreakStatement(s) => {
   307	                *is_control_flow_dead = true;
   308	                result.push(Statement::BreakStatement(s));
   309	            }
   310	            Statement::ContinueStatement(s) => {
   311	                *is_control_flow_dead = true;
   312	                result.push(Statement::ContinueStatement(s));
   313	            }
   314	            Statement::VariableDeclaration(var_decl) => {
   315	                Self::handle_variable_declaration(var_decl, result, ctx);
   316	            }
   317	            Statement::ExpressionStatement(expr_stmt) => {
   318	                Self::handle_expression_statement(expr_stmt, result, ctx);
   319	            }
   320	            Statement::SwitchStatement(switch_stmt) => {
   321	                Self::handle_switch_statement(switch_stmt, result, ctx);
   322	            }
   323	            Statement::IfStatement(if_stmt) => {
   324	                if Self::handle_if_statement(i, stmts, if_stmt, result, ctx).is_break() {
   325	                    return ControlFlow::Break(());
   326	                }
   327	            }
   328	            Statement::ReturnStatement(ret_stmt) => {
   329	                Self::handle_return_statement(r<response clipped><NOTE>To save on context only part of this file has been shown to you. The core issue is clear: `remove_unused_assignment_expr` removes the assignment `rafId = requestAnimationFrame(...)` because it thinks `rafId` has no read references. But `rafId` IS read in the condition `rafId == null`.

The problem might be that when the if statement is converted to a logical expression `(rafId == null) && (rafId = ...)`, the reference counting doesn't account for the fact that the same identifier is used in both the left and right sides of the `&&`.
